articles.description: A string of recent arrests connecting judicial officials to drug trafficking organizations in Costa Rica have raised concerns that institutional corruption facilitating the trade is becoming more commonplace, as a record amount of cocaine flows through the country.

articles.title: Albanian Mafia Leaves Trail of Blood in Ecuador
articles.description: The assassination of Albanian national Adriatik Tresa inside his luxury property in Ecuador has revealed details of his alleged criminal activities, painting a picture of violent Albanian drug emissaries operating in the Andean country.

articles.title: Uruguay Cracking Down on Cattle Rustling from Brazil
articles.description: A surge in cattle and horse rustling is plaguing ranchers along the Uruguay-Brazil border, but a new specialized unit dedicated to tackling the crime may be showing signs of success.

articles.title: Mexico City’s Roaring Trade in Wildlife Trafficking
articles.description: A series of unusual and contrasting seizures of rare fauna in the Mexico City area have raised important questions about the modus operandi of wildlife trafficking in and through Mexico’s capital, long regarded as a key regional hub of the illegal wildlife trade.

articles.title: Shrimp Gangs Rob, Extort, Kill in Ecuador
articles.description: The persistent robbery, extortion, assault and even murder of shrimp farm workers by armed gangs in Ecuador has led the shrimp sector to once more demand gun reforms, in a new attempt to challenge the criminal groups feeding off this lucrative industry.

articles.title: Argentina’s Glaciers are Under Threat
articles.description: As the country’s far-right government attempts to undo groundbreaking legislation that has protected glaciers for fifteen years, activists are fighting back.

articles.title: From Hope to Uncertainty: Venezuelans in Far-Right Chile
articles.description: In January, Venezuelans in Chile briefly celebrated the fall of Nicolás Maduro, but many now fear a harsher climate for migrants as the country shifts to the right.

articles.title: In His Father’s Name, an Indigenous Man Takes on the Government for Abuses at the Krenak Reformatory
articles.description: An Indigenous man in Brazil endured torture and forced labor for 19 months in a dictatorship “concentration camp”. Fifty-five years later, his son seeks justice.

articles.title: Counter-Archives to Border Fascism
articles.description: Even as borders harden, life continues to exceed them. The Spring 2026 issue of the NACLA Report traces how border violence has moved from the margins to the center of political life across the Americas.

articles.title: The FBI’s Return to Ecuador Is Nothing New
articles.description: The announcement of a permanent FBI office in Quito is the latest iteration of the U.S. using security cooperation to intervene in the country’s domestic politics.

articles.title: How the United States Stole Mexico | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 9
articles.description: Donald Trump has his sights set on Mexico. But his actions harken back to a much older era of the U.S. empire.

articles.title: Milei’s War against the Working Class
articles.description: Argentina’s sweeping labor reform, heavily resisted in the streets, is set to roll back nearly a century of hard-fought rights.

articles.title: Feminist Fútbol and Historical Memory in Argentina
articles.description: As Argentina marks 50 years since the beginning of its last bloody dictatorship, a feminist fútbol club in Buenos Aires keeps the memory of the disappeared alive as the government fosters negationism.

articles.title: ¿Perderá alguna vez la hoja de coca su estigma?
articles.description: La reciente decisión de la Organización Mundial de la Salud de rechazar la reclasificación de la hoja de coca supone un nuevo fracaso a la hora de comprender la importancia cultural de esta planta.

articles.title: Cuba’s Cosmopolitan Enclaves: Imperialism and Internationalism in Eastern Sugar Towns (Review)
articles.description: A new book places multiracial sugar workers in Cuba’s northeast at the vanguard of inter-war internationalism, solidarity, and anti-imperialism.

articles.title: Violencia de pandillas y la impunidad en Guatemala
articles.description: Los ataques perpetrados por el Barrio 18 en enero buscaban desestabilizar el país en beneficio de las influyentes élites políticas que han desafiado al gobierno del presidente Bernardo Arévalo.

articles.title: Will the Coca Leaf Ever Lose its Stigma?
articles.description: The World Health Organization’s recent refusal to reclassify coca leaf is yet another failure to understand the plant’s cultural significance.

articles.title: Gang Violence and the Defense of Impunity in Guatemala
articles.description: The Barrio 18’s January attacks sought to destabilize the country to benefit well-connected political elites who have challenged the government of President Bernardo Arévalo.

articles.title: The Donroe Coalition | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 8
articles.description: Last weekend, Donald Trump met with right-wing allies from Latin America to kick off a new military coalition to attack so-called drug cartels and push his agenda up and down the hemisphere.

articles.title: Regime Change and Real Estate in Cuba
articles.description: Trump’s pressure campaign could create an opening for him to develop hotels and real estate in Cuba, a goal he has pursued for decades.

articles.title: Colombia’s Left Has A Strong Showing in the Legislative Elections
articles.description: Amid a regional right-wing turn, the country’s left consolidated its political project, positioning itself well for the upcoming presidential elections.

articles.title: Faith and Body: New Battle Over Abortion in Argentina
articles.description: Reproductive rights in Argentina face increased obstacles as the administration of Javier Milei emboldens anti-rights groups and politicians.

articles.title: En Guatemala, un Consejo Indígena lucha contra una compañía minera
articles.description: Tras dos muertes y acusaciones legales fraudulentas, el pueblo Ch’orti’ sigue luchando para expulsar un proyecto minero que ha contaminado sus tierras.

articles.title: 38 Londres Street: On Impunity, Pinochet in England, and a Nazi in Patagonia (Review)
articles.description: Philipe Sands’ book on the attempts to hold a Nazi leader and Chilean dictator accountable provide lessons for today’s era in which the flouting of international law has become routine.

articles.title: Trump’s War on Cuba: Crisis Made in the U.S.A. | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 7
articles.description: The situation in Cuba is dire. It hasn’t been this bad for decades. And it’s on purpose. This is a humanitarian crisis, made in the United States

articles.title: Of Presidential Power and Prison Riots in Guatemala
articles.description: A wave of violence inside and outside prisons drew attention to President Bernardo Arévalo’s fragile political balancing act between the gangs and a corrupt political class.

articles.title: A Guatemalan Indigenous Council Struggles Against a Mine
articles.description: Targeted with death threats, violence, and bogus legal charges, Ch’orti’ people are still fighting to permanently close a mining project that has polluted their lands.

articles.title: Dispatch from Minneapolis: We Won, but the Fight is Far From Over
articles.description: From rapid response networks to labor strikes and tenant organizing, how anti-ICE organizers built a mass movement in the Twin Cities.

articles.title: Rotos corazones: amor y política en los setenta (Review)
articles.description: Cosse centers the role of love in her study of armed leftist movements in Argentina, unearthing how gender, sexuality, and subjectivity shaped political uprising—and its defeat.

articles.title: The Making of Cuba’s Crisis
articles.description: NACLA editors revisited decades of coverage and curated this selection of articles to help readers understand how Cuba’s current crisis has been years in the making.

articles.title: Lawfare Diplomacy: How the U.S. Bullied its Way Back into the Panama Canal
articles.description: The quiet reclamation of the Canal reveals that the Trump administration has more sophisticated tools at its disposal for asserting its hegemony in the Western Hemisphere.

articles.title: U.S. Stifles Clean Energy Independence in Latin America and the Caribbean
articles.description: Renewable energy production brings with it opportunities for social change in Latin America but an intensified U.S. imperialism seeks to prolong the grip of fossil fuels in the region.

articles.title: Ecuadorian Migrants Caught in the “Everywhere War”
articles.description: Fleeing from war at home and targeted by a brutal U.S. immigration crackdown, Ecuadorian migrants are caught in a perpetual cycle of violence.

articles.title: Homeland Empire | Under the Shadow, Season 2, Episode 6
articles.description: We are seeing an unprecedented reorganization of domestic and foreign policy in the United States. Both of them deployed to achieve the same goal — punish the opposition to Trump.

articles.title: Winter in Havana
articles.description: Uncertain prospects for negotiation as Cuba faces a new Special Period.

articles.title: Las continuas batallas de la memoria histórica en Guatemala
articles.description: Por más de diez años, Angélica Macario se ha dedicado a preservar el archivo del Consejo de la Comunidad Étnica Runujel Junam en una lucha por mantener viva la memoria del genocidio.

articles.title: Guatemala’s Ongoing Memory Battles
articles.description: For over ten years, Angélica Macario has sought to preserve the archive of the Council for Ethnic Community Runujel Junam in a struggle to keep the memory of the genocide alive.

articles.title: El auge del rodriguismo en Costa Rica
articles.description: Preocupados por una creciente sensación de inseguridad, los costarricenses eligieron a Laura Fernández, una candidata del partido gobernante cuyo predecesor lideró un proyecto político que rompe con la tradición democrática del país.

articles.title: Mexican Watchdogs: The Rise of a Critical Press since the 1980s (Review)
articles.description: The political and business history of Mexico’s beleaguered critical press, caught between drug traffickers and the state, is the subject of Andrew Paxman’s new book.

articles.title: ¿Cómo está respondiendo América Latina a la Doctrina Donroe?
articles.description: En una entrevista, el político ecuatoriano y experto en política exterior, Guillaume Long, explica cómo la “Doctrina Donroe” podría reconfigurar la política latinoamericana.

articles.title: How is Latin America Responding to the Donroe Doctrine?
articles.description: In an interview, Ecuadorian politician and foreign policy expert Guillaume Long explains how the “Donroe Doctrine” could reshape Latin American politics.

articles.title: The Rise of Rodriguismo in Costa Rica
articles.description: Amidst rising fears of insecurity, Costa Ricans elected Laura Fernández, a continuity candidate whose predecessor presided over a political project that breaks with the country’s democratic traditions.

articles.title: “Luciérnagas en El Mozote” y la batalla por la memoria histórica en El Salvador
articles.description: Una película apoyada por el estado que narra la masacre de El Mozote en 1981 abre preguntas sobre la guerra civil, mientras que las víctimas siguen buscando justicia.

articles.title: The Breach: Iran-Contra and the Assault on American Democracy (Review)
articles.description: Alan McPherson’s book treats the Iran-Contra affair as a turning point in U.S. history, one which normalized the erosion of key democratic principles and helped birth our current (dis)order.

articles.title: An Unbowed Petro Successfully Plays the Diplomat
articles.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro’s positive meeting with Trump stands to benefit not only his coalition, but also the region writ large.

articles.title: “Luciérnagas en El Mozote” And The Battle Over Historical Memory in El Salvador
articles.description: A state-backed film detailing the 1981 massacre at El Mozote raises thorny questions about the country’s civil war as the victims continue fighting for justice.

articles.title: When the Bombs Fell on Caracas | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 5
articles.description: The United States has painted its January 3 invasion of Venezuela as a surgical operation. But for those on the ground it was a disaster. The US killed more than 100 people. It hit civilian targets and left terror and destruction in its wake.

articles.title: Bolivia frente a un nuevo tiempo de luchas
articles.description: Tras una serie de protestas por recortes en los subsidios al combustible que paralizaron el país, los poderosos movimientos sociales de Bolivia abren un nuevo capítulo y se enfrentan a nuevas formas de represión.

articles.title: Driving Terror: Labor, Violence, and Justice in Cold War Argentina (Review)
articles.description: Karen Robert’s book unearths how a deadly collaboration between the Argentine dictatorship and the Ford Motor Company stifled worker dissent.

articles.title: Maduro Was “Captured,” Our Campus Doesn’t Have to Be
articles.description: As a right-wing center harnesses public funds to bolster U.S. imperial power at Florida International University (FIU), a faculty member issues an urgent call to resist.

articles.title: Bolivians Enter a New Era of Popular Struggles
articles.description: After a series of protests over cuts to fuel subsidies brought the country to a halt, Bolivia’s powerful social movements embark on a new chapter and face new forms of repression.

articles.title: Debunking the US narrative on Venezuela | Under the Shadow Season 2, Ep. 4
articles.description: Host Michael Fox looks at the misconceptions, myths, and misinformation that have been spread about Venezuela in the wake of the US invasion.

articles.title: Postcolonial Rhythms in the Andes
articles.description: A.Chal’s new track “Pituko” is a creative disruption of postcolonial geographies across Peru, and a blend of old and new genres that creates a unique postcolonial sound.

articles.title: El significado global del ataque de Estados Unidos a Venezuela
articles.description: Más allá de la apropiación de los recursos petroleros y minerales de Venezuela, la invasión del 3 de enero es parte de la crisis de época del capitalismo global.

articles.title: Hands Off Latin America | Season 2, Ep. 3
articles.description: After the U.S. invasion of Venezuela, the Trump administration has threatened other countries in Latin America, but some of the region’s leaders and people are standing up.

articles.title: A Wide Net of Solidarity: Antiracism and Anti-Imperialism from the Americas to the Globe (Review)
articles.description: Anne Mahler’s history of the Anti-Imperialist League of the Americas recovers the contributions to the study of imperialism made by pre-WWII Latin American thinkers.

articles.title: Las amenazas contra un triunfo LGBTQ+ en República Dominicana
articles.description: A pesar de reciente despenalización de las relaciones sexuales entre personas del mismo sexo en las fuerzas armadas y la policía, la decisión se enfrenta a ataques por parte del gobierno de derecha y sus aliados.

articles.title: An LGBTQ+ Win in the Dominican Republic Comes Under Fire
articles.description: Despite the recent decriminalization of same-sex relationships in the armed forces and police, the ruling is being attacked by the right-wing government and its allies.

articles.title: The Global Meaning of the U.S. Attack on Venezuela
articles.description: Beyond the seizure of Venezuela’s oil and mineral resources, the January 3 invasion is inscribed within the epochal crisis of global capitalism.

articles.title: A Curated Guide to the U.S. Attack on Venezuela
articles.description: NACLA editors selected several articles from decades of coverage to provide you with the information you need to understand the recent events in Venezuela.

articles.title: Contra el Autoritarismo de la Libertad Financiera (Review)
articles.description: Verónica Gago and Lucí Cavallero’s book completes a trilogy that takes debt seriously as a feminist problem—one that operates not as a momentary burden but as a lasting mechanism of dispossession.

articles.title: El régimen dominicano, de paria regional a vanguardia de la ola reaccionaria
articles.description: Cómo el proyecto de apartheid de la República Dominicana se convirtió en un modelo para la extrema derecha en Estados Unidos.

articles.title: In Paraguay, NGOs are Under Attack
articles.description: Echoing a global trend, the country’s ruling Colorado Party has relied on vague accusations of corruption to attack critical NGOs and further their far-right agenda.

articles.title: The Americas Will Never Be the Same | Season 2, Ep. 2
articles.description: Early on January 3, the U.S. government invaded Venezuela and took President Maduro into custody. It marked the first time the United States used its own military to invade a South American country.

articles.title: What’s Really Behind Trump’s Drive for War in Venezuela? | Season 2, Ep. 1
articles.description: How Trump is redefining U.S. foreign policy in Latin America with Monroe 2.0. This is Season 2, Episode 1 of Under the Shadow.

articles.title: Boomerangs of Empire: Latin America as Colonial Laboratory
articles.description: This special issue of the NACLA Report traces the returning and disseminating violence that is shaping the Americas today, from border militarization to algorithmic surveillance and the criminalization of dissent.

articles.title: Chile at a Crossroads: Human Rights and the Environment Under Kast
articles.description: Chile’s new far-right president seeks to recycle the military regime’s political project, risking hard earned advances in human, Indigenous, and environmental rights.

articles.title: Editors’ Photo Picks 2025
articles.description: This year, NACLA’s web articles were accompanied by powerful photos from journalists across the region. Here are the top photos.

articles.title: Books that Breathe the Everyday Life of El Alto, Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: Los Hijos de Goni and No Me Jodas, No Te Jodo, two books from El Alto publishing house Sobre Selectas, invite people to experience the intricacies of the city and its people.

articles.title: Un nuevo capítulo absurdo en la “guerra contra las drogas”
articles.description: Las ejecuciones extrajudiciales de pescadores latinoamericanos y el indulto a un expresidente y narcotraficante hondureño revelan las letales contradicciones de la política estadounidense de la “guerra contra las drogas”.

articles.title: Editors’ Picks 2025: The Best of NACLA’s Online Coverage
articles.description: With so many critical events, stories, and voices covered in our 2025 web content it was hard to choose a favorite, but here are our top 10!

articles.title: An Absurd New Chapter in the “War on Drugs”
articles.description: Extrajudicial executions of Latin American fishermen and the pardon of a former Honduran President and cocaine kingpin reveal the deadly contradictions of the U.S. “War on Drugs.”

articles.title: Barbecue, Empire, and Haiti’s Child Soldiers
articles.description: Haiti’s gang coalition Viv Ansanm is powered by thousands of child combatants drawn by hunger, displacement, and a century of foreign intervention.

articles.title: The Secret Agent (Review)
articles.description: Kleber Mendonça Filho’s latest political thriller, O agente secreto, comes at the end of an exceptional year for Brazilian films set during the military dictatorship.

articles.title: La impunidad y las luchas internas reinan en Bolivia
articles.description: Mientras el país se inclina hacia la derecha y sus nuevos líderes se enfrascan en luchas internas, la justicia y las reparaciones por las masacres de 2019 se detienen.

articles.title: La guerra de Trump contra los venezolanos: de la Operación Aurora a la Operación Lanza del Sur
articles.description: Cómo la mentira de un casero sin escrúpulos en Colorado sobre “pandillas venezolanas” permitió a Trump detener a miles de personas, desplegar buques de guerra, y reescribir la política migratoria.

articles.title: Surgery and Salvation: The Roots of Reproductive Injustice in Mexico, 1770–1940 (Review)
articles.description: Elizabeth O’Brien’s well-sourced book unearths the long-running roots of the gyneco-obstetric violence women in Mexico still face today.

articles.title: Impunity and Infighting Reign in Bolivia
articles.description: As the country shifts to the right and its new leaders fight amongst themselves, justice and reparations for the 2019 massacres come to a halt.

articles.title: Trump’s War on Venezuelans: From Operation Aurora to Operation Southern Spear
articles.description: How a Colorado slumlord’s lie about Venezuelan gangs was leveraged by the Trump administration to detain thousands, deploy warships, and rewrite the rules of immigration enforcement.

articles.title: What Honduras’s Election Means for Próspera, a Billionaire Utopia
articles.description: As Hondurans cast their ballots, they will decide the fate of a libertarian experiment that has challenged the nation and the idea of democracy itself.

articles.title: How Mamdani Won Over Latinos in NYC
articles.description: By building a politics of community and remaining focused on issues of affordability, the mayor-elect won the support of a diverse population increasingly dismissed as too conservative.

articles.title: Searching for Memory: Aluízio Palmar and the Shadow of Dictatorship in Brazil (Review)
articles.description: Jacob Blanc’s new book sheds light on the extraordinary life of a Brazilian hero while pushing forward our understanding of memory and the role of the historian.

articles.title: Ecuador rechaza el giro derechista de Noboa
articles.description: Los ecuatorianos votaron en contra de una  agenda de gobierno alineada con Estados Unidos, defendieron las protecciones constitucionales progresistas y rechazaron el retorno de bases militares extranjeras.

articles.title: Ecuador Rejects Noboa’s Rightward Turn
articles.description: Ecuadorians vote against the government’s U.S.-aligned agenda, defending progressive constitutional protections and opposing foreign military bases.

articles.title: As Detention Surges Under Trump, Organizers Build a Lasting Movement
articles.description: As the president pumps $45 billion into ICE over the next three years, activists and communities must balance rapid response with longer-term resistance.

articles.title: The Last Door: A History of Torture in Mexico’s War Against Subversives (Review)
articles.description: Gladys I. McCormick’s new book is an invaluable contribution to our understanding of torture in Mexico at the hands of the State.

articles.title: “Las Recambistas”: The New Wave of Communist Women in Chile’s Elections
articles.description: Led by presidential hopeful Jeannette Jara, Chile’s elections feature a new generation of young, female politicians who have found a home in the century-old Chilean Communist Party.

articles.title: Narcofemicide in Argentina: When State Absence Meets Patriarchal Violence
articles.description: A brutal triple femicide exposes how women’s bodies are the battlefield of a war the authorities refuse to name.

articles.title: Establishing a Just Transition at COP30 in Belém
articles.description: As false solutions proliferate, communities most affected by the climate crisis must be centered in advancing transformation.

articles.title: Call for Pitches | Desiring Temporalities + Playful Aesthetics
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for our Summer 2026 issue on creative practices that spark critical and dissident imagination. Send us your pitches by December 1, 2025.

articles.title: Bad Bunny and the Politics of Joyful Resistance
articles.description: Through pleasure and language, Bad Bunny challenges erasure and invites us to examine how joy can disrupt, reclaim, and reimagine connection.

articles.title: U.S. Government Eliminates Legal Protections for Honduran Women
articles.description: A series of recent legal decisions on gender-based violence further reveals the Trump administration’s depravity—and sentences vulnerable asylum-seeking women to their death.

articles.title: En la primera línea del Perú, personas de a pie enfrentan violencia extraordinaria
articles.description: Los manifestantes en la “primera línea” luchan por un nuevo Perú y se enfrentan a una represión estatal letal.

articles.title: The Future of Hip Hop is in Indigenous Languages
articles.description: The First International Indigenous Hip Hop Festival brings together artists from across Abya Yala to the birthplace of Hip Hop, the South Bronx.

articles.title: On Peru’s Frontline, Ordinary Citizens Face Extraordinary Violence
articles.description: The dedicated protesters who make up the “primera línea” are fighting for a new Peru—and confronting lethal state repression.

articles.title: Bolivia and its Destiny: A Post-Election Analysis
articles.description: After two decades of MAS rule, the country shifts to the right amid economic turmoil and a crisis of representation.

articles.title: Bolivia y su destino: Una lectura post-electoral
articles.description: Tras dos décadas de gobierno del MAS, el país gira hacia la derecha en medio de la turbulencia económica y una crisis de representación.

articles.title: María Corina Machado’s Nobel Peace Prize: A Reading from the Left Opposition
articles.description: Machado’s support for—and from—the world’s far-right leaders makes a mockery of her prize and complicates the task of the Venezuelan popular struggle.

articles.title: La Voz del Anonimato/The Voice of Anonymity (Review)
articles.description: Layqa Nuna Yawar’s new exhibition carefully crafts an aesthetic uniting Indigenous peoples and Andean immigrants, linking both through Indigenous principles of Andean Philosophy.

articles.title: Antes de las elecciones, corrupción y una crisis económica plagan la administración de Milei
articles.description: Una red de sobornos en la que está involucrada la hermana del presidente argentino, Karina Milei, es solo otro de la serie de escándalos que rodean al gobierno de Milei en vísperas de las elecciones legislativas.

articles.title: Before Elections, Corruption and Economic Crisis Plague Milei
articles.description: A kickback scheme involving the Argentine President’s sister, Karina Milei, is just another in a string of scandals surrounding Milei’s administration in the lead-up to legislative elections.

articles.title: Mexicans Demand a Complete Break with Israel
articles.description: As the country’s progressive government drags its feet, the demand to end political and economic relations with Israel is an ever-growing movement.

articles.title: With Trump, Polarization Among Venezuelans Reaches New Heights
articles.description: Washington’s Venezuela policy has intensified divisions within the opposition, revealing class divides and fears over the fallout from a potential U.S. invasion.

articles.title: A condenação de Bolsonaro e a luta contra anistia no Brasil
articles.description: Um coletivo de descendentes de ex-presos políticos discute o julgamento histórico e a batalha contínua por verdade, justiça, e responsabilização.

articles.title: Brazilian Belonging: Jewish Politics in Cold War Latin America (Review)
articles.description: Rom’s book is an archivally rich account of how Jewish individuals and institutions grappled with rapidly shifting politics—and state violence—before, during, and after the Cold War.

articles.title: La Generación Z del Perú ayuda a derrocar a la presidenta
articles.description: Inspirados por una ola global de protestas, los jóvenes manifestantes de Perú han convertido la indignación en redes sociales en una movilización masiva contra la corrupción y la represión.

articles.title: Peru’s Gen Z Helps Bring Down the President
articles.description: Inspired by a global wave of protest, Peru’s youth protesters have turned social media outrage into mass mobilization against corruption and repression.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Paro Protests Autocracy, Not Diesel
articles.description: President Noboa escalates repression and lawfare against protesters, targeting Indigenous people and water and land defenders.

articles.title: Impunity and a Splintered MAS pave the way for the Right’s Return in Bolivia
articles.description: As the country heads to an October 19 runoff between two conservative candidates, any hope of justice for the victims of right-wing violence fades.

articles.title: El Congreso revoca los vetos de Milei: victoria para la salud y la educación
articles.description: Los vetos de Milei a la financiación de las universidades públicas y la salud han sido revocados por ambas cámaras del Congreso, una victoria histórica para la democracia en Argentina.

articles.title: Congress Overturns Milei’s Vetoes: Victory for Healthcare and Education
articles.description: Milei’s vetoes of funding for public universities and healthcare overturned by both houses of Congress, marking a historic win for democracy in Argentina.

articles.title: Venezuela: entre la “guerra” de Trump y la normalidad cotidiana
articles.description: La retórica belicosa de Trump regresa, pero en las calles de Venezuela el ambiente es de calma más que de alarma.

articles.title: Venezuela: Between Trump’s “War” and Everyday Normality
articles.description: Trump’s belligerent rhetoric has returned, but on the streets of Venezuela the mood is one of calm rather than alarm.

articles.title: Mexico Can do More for Migrants
articles.description: Though constrained by U.S. threats, Mexico’s history demonstrates it can—and must—do more to protect the dignity of migrants at home and abroad.

articles.title: Colombia’s Peace Court Delivers Historic Sentence Against Military Officers
articles.description: The ruling by the transitional justice tribunal, though criticized by some victims, brings hope that the country may soon turn the page.

articles.title: A Flower Traveled in My Blood: The Incredible True Story of the Grandmothers Who Fought to Find a Stolen Generation of Children (Review)
articles.description: Haley Cohen Gilliland’s book powerfully tells the story of the Abuelas de Plaza de Mayo, an organization whose challenge to Argentina’s dictatorship is as relevant now as ever before.

articles.title: Progressive Leaders Honor Mujica, Defend “Democracia Siempre”
articles.description: Presidents and activists looked to the life of former Uruguayan President Pepe Mujica for lessons on how to defeat the Right.

articles.title: Bolsonaro’s Conviction and the Fight Against Amnesty in Brazil
articles.description: A collective of descendants of former political prisoners weighs in on the historic ruling and the ongoing struggle for truth, justice, and accountability.

articles.title: Re-militarization of Puerto Rico Exacerbates Environmental Injustice
articles.description: In the height of hurricane season, U.S. military operations in Puerto Rico worsen environmental racism. Meanwhile, communities fight for rooftop solar access for all.

articles.title: Por qué fracasó el primer laboratorio legal de coca en Colombia
articles.description: Una comunidad indígena Nasa quiso darle a la hoja de coca un uso distinto al narcotráfico. Pero el proyecto se desplomó bajo el peso de la presión estadounidense y la prohibición internacional.

articles.title: Panama’s President Cracks Down on Unions
articles.description: Amidst protests, President José Raúl Mulino holds firm to social security reform while suspending habeas corpus and attacking unions and striking workers.

articles.title: Why Colombia’s First Legal Coca Lab Failed
articles.description: An Indigenous Nasa community set out to offer farmers an alternative to the drug trade, but the project collapsed under the weight of U.S. pressure and  international prohibitions.

articles.title: Jubilados y jubiladas en Argentina lideran la lucha contra el programa de austeridad de Milei
articles.description: La violencia estatal sin precedentes contra los jubilados y sus aliados no ha logrado sofocar el descontento popular por la creciente precariedad de la vida en Argentina.

articles.title: Los Angeles Against ICE: Art, Mutual Aid, and Collective Survival
articles.description: From exhibitions to queer spaces for grief, Angelinos forge networks of care and resistance to confront state violence and nurture collective futures.

articles.title: The Sandinista Revolution: A Global Latin American History (Review)
articles.description: Jarquín’s excellent book details the Sandinista revolution, its regional stakes, and the contradictory effects brought by external involvement.

articles.title: Struggling Pensioners Lead the Charge Against Milei’s Austerity Agenda
articles.description: Unprecedented state violence against retirees and their allies has failed to quash popular discontent at the increasing precarity of life in Argentina.

articles.title: Green Capitalism in the Americas Web Collection
articles.description: Don’t miss the open access and Spanish and Portuguese content from our Fall 2025 issue.

articles.title: Capitalismo verde contra a democracia: o plano de bioeconomia no Pará
articles.description: À medida que a COP30 se aproxima, a estratégia de bioeconomia do Brasil promete crescimento verde. Mas por trás da retórica reside um aprofundamento do desmatamento, a captura pela elite e a marginalização das comunidades locais.

articles.title: De la esperanza a la desilusión: Bolivia tras 20 años del MAS
articles.description: En medio de feroces disputas internas y crisis económica, la izquierda boliviana sufrió una fuerte derrota después de casi dos décadas de gobierno innovador.

articles.title: Green Sacrifice Zones in Antofagasta
articles.description: Chile’s energy transition promises decarbonization, but in the north of the country, a growing extractive frontier threatens water, land, and Indigenous ways of life.

articles.title: Has Belém Turned its Back on the River?
articles.description: COP30 construction projects have reproduced long-standing social inequalities and environmental injustices, giving new momentum to struggles over water and life in the city.

articles.title: Greenwashing Debt in the Galápagos Islands
articles.description: Ecuador’s historic debt-for-nature swap promises to bridge the international funding gap for biodiversity conservation, but island residents say it erodes sovereignty and empowers foreign interests.

articles.title: A colonialidade do discurso climático
articles.description: Desvendando narrativas climáticas hegemônicas que moldam as negociações globais do clima por meio de uma abordagem interseccional e decolonial.

articles.title: La colonialidad del discurso climático
articles.description: Desenmascarando narrativas climáticas hegemónicas que conforman las negociaciones climáticas globales bajo un enfoque interseccional y decolonial.

articles.title: Puerto Rico’s Fight for Energy Independence
articles.description: Amid worsening blackouts and fossil fuel contracts that deepen colonial dependency, Puerto Ricans are building energy sovereignty from the ground up—one rooftop solar panel at a time.

articles.title: Notes from the Palestine-Mexico Border
articles.description: Reflections on empire, genocide, and the violence that has come home to roost.

articles.title: From Hope to Disillusionment: Bolivia After 20 Years of MAS
articles.description: Amidst bitter infighting and economic crisis, Bolivia’s left suffered a major defeat after nearly two decades of groundbreaking governance.

articles.title: Guatemala: Entre los pueblos originarios y un progresismo light
articles.description: El primer gobierno progresista en décadas se muestra impotente frente al “pacto de corruptos”, mientras el movimiento indígena busca frenar a la ultraderecha y empujar a Bernardo Arévalo hacia la izquierda.

articles.title: “Não somos Vale do Lítio!”
articles.description: No Vale do Jequitinhonha, Brasil, a promessa de uma transição verde traz mais violência extrativista, mas as comunidades estão resistindo e reivindicando o direito de moldar o próprio futuro.

articles.title: Dissident Peace: Autonomous Struggles and the State in Colombia (Review)
articles.description: Anthony Dest’s Dissident Peace is a study of political possibilities in Colombia in a period that begins with the signing of the 2016 Peace Accords between the Revolutionary Armed Forces […]

articles.title: Belém virou as costas para o rio?
articles.description: Os projetos de construção da COP30 reproduziram desigualdades sociais e injustiças ambientais de longa data, dando novo impulso às lutas pela água e pela vida na cidade.

articles.title: Zonas de sacrificio verde en Antofagasta
articles.description: La transición energética de Chile promete la descarbonización, pero en el norte del país una creciente frontera extractiva amenaza el agua, el territorio y los modos de vida indígena.

articles.title: A Oaxacan Indigenous Community Fights a New Round of Bogus Criminal Charges
articles.description: The Mazatecan community of Eloxochitlán de Flores Magón has been subjected to more than a decade of judicial persecution for defending their lands.

articles.title: Green Capitalism in the Americas: False Solutions, Real Threats
articles.description: In this special issue of the NACLA Report, we critically examine the rise of green capitalism in the region in the lead-up to COP30 in Belém, Brazil in November.

articles.title: Echoes of the Water War: Legacies of Cochabamba, Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: A newly expanded edition of Oscar Olivera’s book revisits the grassroots uprising that defeated water privatization 25 years later, offering lessons on sovereignty, solidarity, and defense of the commons.

articles.title: Charting a Progressive Path Forward at the Pan American Congress
articles.description: As the far right gains ground across the world, the region’s Left builds a common front in Mexico City.

articles.title: U.S. Sends Troops to Southern Caribbean in New Threat to Venezuela
articles.description: The action was announced by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who called the Venezuelan government a “criminal organization.”

articles.title: Uribe on Trial: Colombia’s Justice System Faces Its Defining Test
articles.description: In a nation long plagued by impunity, the historic conviction of Colombia’s powerful former president is testing the country’s judicial independence and raising the stakes for truth and accountability.

articles.title: The Militarization of Commercial Activity in Mexico
articles.description: After building the Tren Maya, Mexico’s military has expanded its commercial reach in the Yucatán, with no regard for transparency or accountability.

articles.title: Colombia desafía la impunidad israelí desde arriba y desde abajo
articles.description: Mientras el presidente Petro intensifica sus esfuerzos para detener las exportaciones de carbón a Israel, los activistas presionan para que se tomen medidas decisivas.

articles.title: Dictatorship Across Borders: Brazil, Chile and the South American Cold War (Review)
articles.description: Burns’s book brings South American history to the fore, explaining the regional dynamics and economic turmoil that led to Salvador Allende’s downfall and the beginnings of Operation Condor.

articles.title: Colombia Challenges Israeli Impunity from Above and Below
articles.description: As President Petro ramps up efforts to halt coal exports to Israel, activists push for decisive action and chart a new path for Global South solidarity.

articles.title: Suriname Chooses New President, Considers New Paths
articles.description: The election of the country’s first woman president comes after years of corruption and economic insecurity.

articles.title: Yon Sosa. Historia del MR13 en Guatemala y México: Seguida de las Memorias Militares del Comandante Guerrillero (REVIEW)
articles.description: Arturo Taracena’s deep dive into the Guatemalan guerrilla group and its leader, informed by personal experience and decades of research, is social history at its finest.

articles.title: From Colombia to Ukraine’s Front Lines
articles.description: As more Colombians fight and die in foreign wars, their loved ones push the government for help in finding out their fate.

articles.title: The Politics of Care Work: Puerto Rican Women Organizing for Social Justice (Review)
articles.description: The recent book by Emma Amador examines Puerto Rican women social workers’ advocacy for political and social justice under U.S. colonialism throughout the 20th century.

articles.title: Indigenous Communities in Putumayo Resist Canadian Oil
articles.description: The Nasa’s fight against Gran Tierra Energy reveals the links between extraction and armed conflict, and the limitations of the government’s reforms.

articles.title: Panama Fights for Freedom
articles.description: Anger at neoliberal austerity, Canadian mining corruption, and U.S. imperialism have converged into a powerful protest movement.

articles.title: Criminalización y terrorismo judicial contra pueblos indígenas en Guatemala
articles.description: La reciente detención de dos líderes mayas es simbólico de la creciente represión y criminalización de los pueblos indígenas por parte del Estado guatemalteco.

articles.title: Criminalization and Judicial Terrorism Against Indigenous Peoples in Guatemala
articles.description: The recent arrests of two Maya leaders is emblematic of increasing repression and criminalization of Indigenous peoples by the Guatemalan state.

articles.title: Mexico’s Teachers Demand Pensions Over Profit
articles.description: Education workers in Mexico are leading a renewed struggle to reclaim public retirement systems from global finance.

articles.title: México Between Feast and Famine: Food, Corporate Power, and Inequality (Review)
articles.description: Enrique C. Ochoa details the transformation of Mexico’s food economy from a traditional, locally controlled system to one driven by neoliberal ideas of modernity and efficiency.

articles.title: Learning from the Los Angeles Protests
articles.description: In the wake of widespread protests against deportations that began in Los Angeles earlier this month, left and feminist movements must develop a future facing vision to combat fascism.

articles.title: Haiti’s Political Impasse
articles.description: Haiti’s current form of “checkpoint governance” represents a structural transformation in how politics works in the country.

articles.title: The Fourth Invasion: Decolonizing Histories, Extractivism, and Maya Resistance in Guatemala (Review)
articles.description: Giovanni Batz’s carefully researched text examines how the Ixil and K’iche’ Mayas have resisted attacks on their land, state violence, and extraction since Spanish colonization.

articles.title: Please Excuse Our Appearance!
articles.description: NACLA is getting a new website! We are still working out some glitches with the website launch so please be patient as we migrate and update our content.

articles.title: How the Grandson of a Dictator Lobbies for Sanctions Against Brazil in the U.S.
articles.description: Based in Florida and charged by federal prosecutors, Paulo Figueiredo is a close ally of the Bolsonaro family and is campaigning in the U.S. against Brazilian Supreme Court justices.

articles.title: From Anti-War to Anti-Immigrant
articles.description: Once vocal advocates for workers’ rights in war-torn El Salvador, some Albany labor activists turned against Central American migrants at home—fueling exclusionary narratives that persist today.

articles.title: Monuments of Mourning: Remembering the Victims of the Key Bridge Collapse
articles.description: After the Key Bridge collapse in Baltimore, Maryland took the lives of six Central American and Mexican men, a grieving community built a memorial to remember them.

articles.title: El resultado electoral en Ecuador: una mirada desde el ecofeminismo
articles.description: El triunfo de Noboa consolida una extrema derecha misógina, extractivista, y subordinada al poder imperial con efectos graves para mujeres y territorios.

articles.title: Epistemic Violence and the Struggle for Moskitian Historical Memory
articles.description: Through radio broadcasts and digital archives, Moskitian activists are reclaiming their history, resisting settler colonial erasure, and forging a transnational movement for sovereignty across the western Caribbean.

articles.title: Nuestra existencia, nuestra memoria
articles.description: Desde Tegucigalpa a Buenos Aires y Nueva Orleans, el Archivo Honduras Cuir preserva y celebra la vida trans y cuir a través de la memoria, la resistencia y la solidaridad transnacional.

articles.title: Regímenes que le apuestan a la otredad
articles.description: En la Guatemala de la era de la guerra civil, la Policía Nacional recopiló álbumes fotográficos de todas las personas que etiquetó como subversivas. Hoy, ese legado de criminalización sigue vigente.

articles.title: “Prison Bars Will Not Silence the Truth”
articles.description: In El Salvador, the movement of families of people deprived of their liberty is sustaining a struggle for a community stripped of its humanity and reimagining justice in the process.

articles.title: “Las Rejas No Callarán la Verdad”
articles.description: En El Salvador, el movimiento de familiares de personas privadas de libertad sostiene una lucha por una comunidad que ha sido despojada de su humanidad. En el proceso, está reimaginando la justicia.

articles.title: El Legado del Papa Francisco
articles.description: El primer Papa latinoamericano desafió la tradición y el poder católico con una doctrina de humildad e inclusión, volviendo a centrarse radicalmente en los pobres y marginados.

articles.title: Thanks to Life: A Biography of Violeta Parra (Review)
articles.description: The recent biography chronicles the dynamic life and career of Chilean folklorista Violeta Parra.

articles.title: El beso de Barbarella y el inicio de la revolución sexual en Bolivia
articles.description: Cincuenta años después de que una bailarina travesti se atrevió a besar a un dictador, ¿dónde está el movimiento TLGB boliviano ahora?

articles.title: La Horchata and Queer Diasporic Collectivity
articles.description: A zine envisioned by and for Central Americans is reshaping what it means to be Central American in the diaspora through a spirit of acompañamiento, inclusivity, and collective becoming.

articles.title: “Caminxs del Sur”: A Sonic-Collage for the Isthmus
articles.description: Sensing the pluriverse through sound and movement, this audio-visual archive for the future reimagines Central American survival, displacement, and femme worldmaking from below.

articles.title: Can the Youth Speak? The Inter-generational Politics of the Afro-Panamanian Movement
articles.description: Drawing from student resistance in the 1970s, a new generation of Black Panamanian organizers is confronting environmental racism and forging a radical, intersectional movement for justice, identity, and systemic change.

articles.title: On Isthmian Knowing: Bodies, Archives, Futures
articles.description: Our Summer 2025 print issue explores how knowledge has been created, challenged, and imagined on the Central American isthmus and across its vast diasporas.

articles.title: Isthmian Saberes Web Exclusive
articles.description: Don’t miss the web exclusive content from our Summer 2025 issue, Isthmian Saberes, on knowledge production and archival praxis in América Central.

articles.title: Javier Milei’s War on Indigenous Rights
articles.description: As Argentina’s far right administration dismantles Indigenous protections, a wave of land evictions has destabilized life for Patagonia’s Mapuche community.

articles.title: Panamá to Belém: Grassroots Demands for a Real Just Transition
articles.description: United Nations Climate Week in Panamá centered market-based false solutions to the climate crisis, but it’s not too late to change course for the June global climate talks in Bonn.

articles.title: Sovereignty and Extortion:  A New State Form in Mexico (Review)
articles.description: Lomnitz’s book explores the historical construction of the Mexican state and analyzes its failure to adapt successfully to its neoliberal transformation.

articles.title: La motosierra de Milei ataca la salud mental en Buenos Aires
articles.description: 200 trabajadores fueron despedidos del Hospital Laura Bonaparte como parte de la política de recorte de presupuesto público del ultra liberal. La atención en salud mental se desmejoró, afectando a los pacientes.

articles.title: Milei’s Chainsaw Slashes Mental Health in Buenos Aires
articles.description: In March, 200 workers were dismissed from the Laura Bonaparte Hospital as part of Milei’s drastic cuts to the country’s public budget. Mental health care has deteriorated, having dire effects on patients.

articles.title: To Vote or Not to Vote: Venezuelans Face Another Election Choice
articles.description: In the shadow of a contested presidential vote, Venezuela’s upcoming regional elections reveal more about power struggles than democratic representation.

articles.title: Chile in Their Hearts, and Ours (Review)
articles.description: The recent book draws upon extensive investigative reporting by John Dinges, who pursues the untold story behind the killings of two Americans by the Chilean military after the coup.

articles.title: Indigenous Women Survivors of Guatemala’s Rape Camps Seek Justice
articles.description: Forty years after surviving state violence, Maya Achi women return to court to demand justice, challenging a long legacy of impunity for crimes against humanity.

articles.title: The Modern Surveillance State: Mexico and the CIA during the Cold War
articles.description: Newly declassified CIA documents reveal the links between Mexico’s contemporary surveillance state and a uniquely close Cold War collaboration with U.S. intelligence agencies.

articles.title: The Reinvention of the Strike: 10 Years of Feminist Uprising in Argentina
articles.description: This year marks the 10-year anniversary of the Ni Una Menos movement. Understanding the tensions that mark these moments of intense rearrangements and changes in increasingly militarized capitalism is urgent.

articles.title: Debates over TPS for Venezuelans Reveal Diasporic Divide
articles.description: The battle over Temporary Protected Status provides a window into the class, racial, and political divisions within the Venezuelan diaspora in the United States.

articles.title: Honduran Farmers Continue Their Fight for Land and Justice
articles.description: After the murder of their leader, communities in the Aguán Valley have continued their fight against corruption, impunity, and the pollution of their lands.

articles.title: Criminalizing Natural Birth: An Interview with Ricardo Jones
articles.description: May 5th is the International Day of the Midwife. Natural birth and home birth midwives are facing criminalization of their work in countries worldwide. Brazil’s Ricardo and Neusa Jones are the latest.

articles.title: Neofascistas marchan en Punta Cana exigiendo la expulsión de los haitianos
articles.description: Una marcha reciente, violenta y destructiva, llamando a expulsar a las personas haitianas de Punta Cana es el síntoma de un extendido e intenso anti-haitianismo y un creciente movimiento fascista en la República Dominicana.

articles.title: Cuerpx en Vela: Travesti Performance Against the Necro-State
articles.description: Artist Germa Machuca uses her body as an altar and guiding light to illuminate the connections across anti-trans and anti-Indigenous violence in Peru.

articles.title: At the Thresholds: Labor Organizing as Travesti-Trans Formal Workers in Argentina
articles.description: Travesti and trans workers in the formal sector expand imaginaries about the relationship between LGTBQ+ people and work, contributing to broader labor struggles for all of society.

articles.title: For Trans Men in Colombia, All Cops Are Bastards
articles.description: Violence against trans men by state forces is widespread—and little studied. The profiling inherent to mandatory military service only aggravates the problem.

articles.title: Transfeminist Struggles Against Debt and Dispossession
articles.description: In Argentina, sexual dissidents mobilize transversal alliances to denounce indebtedness as a tool of capitalist displacement and violence.

articles.title: Las Maricas No Olvidamos: Dissident Archives Against Deliberate Erasure
articles.description: A sex-dissident archive recovers the memory of the struggle to decriminalize homosexuality in Ecuador through affective and archival work that combats invisibilization and patriarchal erasure.

articles.title: La silvicultura comunitaria aborda la destrucción ambiental en México
articles.description: Las estructuras de gobernanza comunitaria ponen la toma de decisiones sobre los bosques de Ixlán de Juárez en manos locales, generando oportunidades económicas y revirtiendo siglos de deforestación en la localidad.

articles.title: Blue Collar Empire: The Untold Story of US Labor’s Global Anticommunist Crusade (Review)
articles.description: Schuhrke documents the AFL-CIO’s anti-communist campaign and role in weakening left-wing labor unions around the world, between the 1940s and 1990s.

articles.title: Community Forestry Addresses Environmental Destruction in Mexico
articles.description: Community-based governance structures put decision-making about Ixlán de Juarez’s forests into local hands, creating economic opportunity and reversing centuries of deforestation in the town.

articles.title: Neofascist March Calls for the Expulsion of Haitians in Punta Cana
articles.description: A recent violent and destructive march calling for the expulsion of Haitians in Punta Cana demonstrates the vehement anti-Haitianism and rising fascism prevalent in the Dominican Republic.

articles.title: Tren de Aragua: A Gang, Not Terrorist Invaders
articles.description: Trump’s use of the Alien Enemies Act to target Venezuelan migrants relies on a false narrative about Tren de Aragua and the Venezuelan state, and sets a dangerous precedent for immigrant rights.

articles.title: Call For Pitches | Boomerangs of Empire: The Americas as Colonial Laboratory
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for our Winter 2025 issue on the Americas as both site and system of imperial experimentation and return. Send us your pitches by May 2, 2025.

articles.title: Para las transmasculinidades en Colombia, todos los policías son una gonorrea
articles.description: El abuso de autoridad de la Fuerza Pública hacia hombres trans es un fenómeno prevaleciente y poco analizado, agravado por el perfilamiento implícito en el servicio militar obligatorio.

articles.title: Organización TranSindical en el Zagúan
articles.description: Trabajadorxs travestis y trans en el sector formal en Argentina expanden el imaginario sobre la relación entre personas LGTBQ+ y el trabajo, contribuyendo a las luchas laborales más amplias.

articles.title: I’m Still Here (Review)
articles.description: The critically acclaimed film tells the story of the kidnapping and murder of former congressman Rubens Paiva during Brazil’s military dictatorship and his wife Eunice’s struggle to find the truth behind his forced disappearance.

articles.title: El pan de cada día : Trabajo feminizado y crisis económica
articles.description: En Bolivia, donde predomina la economía informal, el mercado popular es un microcosmos de la realidad nacional.

articles.title: Ajuda mútua e saúde trans no Brasil
articles.description: Durante a pandemia, as opções de assistência médica transmasculina foram severamente reduzidas. Em resposta, grupos trans autônomos criaram redes de compartilhamento de conhecimento e ajuda mútua.

articles.title: Destierro y desmar: Bordando experiencias transmasculinas de migración interna y desplazamiento forzoso
articles.description: Para los activistas transmasculinos desarraigados de las tierras y aguas que llamaban hogar, el bordado y la poesía se convierten en prácticas para expresar la nostalgia y construir comunidad.

articles.title: Voces de las Diversidades Ancestrales en el cine
articles.description: En toda Abya Yala, creadores audiovisuales indígenas exploran la disidencia sexual y el deseo en sus obras, impulsando conversaciones en torno a la producción cultural en sus propios términos.

articles.title: Las maricas ni nos vamos, ni olvidamos
articles.description: Un archivo sexo-disidente recupera la memoria de la lucha por despenalizar la homosexualidad en Ecuador, en un trabajo afectivo que combate la invisibilización y el borramiento patriarcal.

articles.title: Cuerpx en Vela: Performance Travesti frente al Necro-Estado
articles.description: La artista Germa Machuca usa su cuerpa como altar y luz de guía para iluminar las conexiones entre la violencia anti-trans y anti-indígena en Perú.

articles.title: Políticas transfeministas contra la expropiación de la deuda
articles.description: En Argentina, colectivas de las disidencias sexuales movilizan alianzas transversales para denunciar el endeudamiento como herramienta de desplazamiento y violencia capitalista.

articles.title: Explosive Report and Exposé on Colombia’s Magdalena Medio Oil Industry
articles.description: After a two-year investigation, a comprehensive report, BBC documentary, and whistleblower dossier reveal alleged widespread contamination, health impacts, and surveillance by Colombia’s state-owned Ecopetrol.

articles.title: Buscadoras in Mexico Under Threat After Grisly Discovery
articles.description: News of a mass grave found by civilian search collectives has reopened an old debate about a lack of political will on the part of authorities to investigate violent crime.

articles.title: ¿Cómo estamos de memoria en Argentina?
articles.description: Ante el ataque de Javier Milei a los derechos civiles, argentinos participaron en manifestaciones en el día de la Memoria, la Verdad y la Justicia para honrar a las víctimas del terrorismo de Estado.

articles.title: “Memory Moves Us”: Argentines Take to the Streets for 24M
articles.description: In the face of Javier Milei’s deepening attack on civil rights, massive mobilizations in Argentina honor the victims of state terrorism on the Day of Remembrance for Truth and Justice.

articles.title: The Amazon in Times of War (Review)
articles.description: Marcos Colón’s book, grounded in the experiences of Indigenous communities, examines how the institutional violence of Jair Bolsonaro’s government resulted in the environmental destruction of the Brazilian Amazon.

articles.title: Hacia las lenguas del corazón/ Towards Languages of the Heart
articles.description: An art collaboration between two activists and friends involves the sharing of blood as an act of diasporic love, colonial reckoning, and collective repair for queer, travesti, and trans people of color.

articles.title: Destierro y Desmar: Embroidering Experiences of Internal Migration and Forced Displacement
articles.description: For transmasculine activists uprooted from the lands and waters they called home, embroidery and poetry become practices for expressing nostalgia and building community in Bogotá.

articles.title: Cuerpos Furiosos: Travesti-Trans Politics for Counterrevolutionary Times
articles.description: The Spring 2025 issue of the NACLA Report explores travesti-trans politics across the Americas, an antifascist and transversal politics with the power to reshape our world.

articles.title: Afro-Feminist Poetics and Trans Life in Cuba: A Conversation with Max Fonseca
articles.description: The poet and activist talks about the precarity that trans, queer, and Afrodescendent people in Cuba face today, and the life sustaining worlds built by Black trans women in Cuba and its diaspora.

articles.title: Bodies on Fire: Toloposungo’s Trans-Marika Abolitionist Performance
articles.description: In Colombia, a trans-marika vogue collective takes to the streets to denounce state violence and call for police abolition.

articles.title: Web Exclusive – Cuerpos Furiosos: Travesti-Trans Politics in Revolt
articles.description: Don’t miss the web exclusive content from our Spring 2025 issue, Cuerpos Furiosos: Trans-Travesti Politics in Revolt.

articles.title: Anti-Racist Transfeminism: Against Adjustment and the Plundering of Rights
articles.description: In the face of discriminatory narratives and laws in Argentina, activists advocate for anti-racist, transfeminist initiatives during this year’s International Women’s Day.

articles.title: Transfeminismo antirracista: contra el ajuste y el saqueo de derechos
articles.description: Frente a las narrativas y leyes discriminatorias en Argentina, activistas abogan por iniciativas transfeministas antirracistas durante el Día Internacional de la Mujer de este año.

articles.title: “Carnival is Always Political”: Keeping Protest Alive in Trinidad
articles.description: Designer Robert Young discusses the political activism woven into the costumes of his band, Vulgar Fraction, which participates annually in Trinidad and Tobago’s Carnival celebrations.

articles.title: Brazil’s Student Movement Resists the Far Right, at Home and Abroad
articles.description: In the days after the Trump inauguration, Brazilian students gathered at the largest student congress in Latin America to debate the future of left resistance.

articles.title: Trump’s Latin America Policy: Inconsistencies and Vacillations
articles.description: The Trump administration’s volatility on foreign policy reveals internal divisions within Trumpism. But when threats and populism lose their momentum, the anti-communist hawks may get their way.

articles.title: Corpos furiosos: Política travesti-trans para tempos contrarrevolucionários
articles.description: Este artigo foi publicado na edição de primavera de 2025 de nossa revista trimestral, a NACLA Report. Read this introduction in English. Leer esta introducción en español. Enquanto escrevo esta introdução de Charlottesville, Virgínia, […]

articles.title: Cuerpos furiosos: Política travesti-trans para tiempos contrarrevolucionarios
articles.description: Este artículo fue publicado en inglés en la edición de primavera de 2025 de nuestra revista trimestral el NACLA Report. Read this introduction in English. Leia este artigo em português. Mientras escribo esta introducción desde Charlottesville, […]

articles.title: Furia archivística: una conversación con archivos trans, travesti y cuir
articles.description: Desde la ternura, amistad y cuidado, una creciente ola de proyectos archivísticos en América Latina y el Caribe desafía los modos hegemónicos de narrar la historia y comprender el presente desde cuerpos y memorias en revuelta.

articles.title: A New No-Man’s-Land: Writing and Art at Guantánamo, Cuba (Review)
articles.description: Esther Whitfield’s book examines how art produced in Guantánamo transcends cultural and linguistic divides to find common ground, reimagining empathy and resistance against political forces.

articles.title: ¿Puede la democracia frenar al extráctivismo y al autoritarismo?
articles.description: El electorado ecuatoriano acudirá a una segunda vuelta en abril en un clima altamente polarizado. Está en juego una lucha política más amplia entre autoritarismo y democracia.

articles.title: The U.S. War on Migrants Gets Help from El Salvador
articles.description: El Salvador’s offer to house deportees and U.S. citizens in its infamous prisons – for profit – signals a new and troubling escalation in the criminalization of migration.

articles.title: Rio’s Samba Parade Spotlights Trans Rights
articles.description: The Tuiuiti samba school uplifts trans identities and highlights the political dimensions of Brazil’s Carnival celebrations.

articles.title: Los legados africanos prohibidos en la República Dominicana
articles.description: A pesar de la continua criminalización y persecución racista de la tradición africana, desde la criminalización del vudú hasta las restricciones contra el gagá, la cultura afrodominicana persiste.

articles.title: Forbidden African Legacies in the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Despite the ongoing criminalization and racist persecution of African tradition, from the criminalization of Vodou to restrictions against Gagá, Afro-Dominican culture persists.

articles.title: The Life of a Trans Mexican Revolutionary, in Drag
articles.description: Drag performers mine the story of the revolutionary hero and trans icon Amelio Robles to better understand the past and shape the future.

articles.title: “Quiero ser libre, pues libre nací”
articles.description: From Reagan to Trump, the fight for sanctuary has deep roots in the United States. The lessons of the 1980s anti-war sanctuary movement are relevant for today’s immigrant rights struggles.

articles.title: Can Democracy Stop Extractivism and Authoritarianism?
articles.description: Ecuador’s electorate will go to a runoff vote in April in a highly polarized climate. At stake is a broader political struggle between authoritarianism and democracy.

articles.title: Despite Regional Opposition, a Canadian Company Pursues Mining in Santander
articles.description: In the face of rifts over securitization issues and calls for conservation, Aris Mining intends to launch the Soto Norte mine in Santander, Colombia, in 2029.

articles.title: In a Polarized Political Climate, Ecuador Heads to a Presidential Runoff
articles.description: Ecuador held its general election on February 9. Defying the polls, Ecuadorians will be heading to a runoff race in April to choose its next president.

articles.title: In the Face of Violence, Catatumbo’s Communities Call for Solidarity
articles.description: Failed peace negotiations and the battle for control over valuable territory have produced an unprecedented humanitarian catastrophe.

articles.title: A Proposal for Plurinational Popular Power Woven from Below
articles.description: Leonidas Iza, Ecuador’s presidential candidate with the Indigenous-aligned Pachakutik political movement, outlines his vision for governing a Plurinational State.

articles.title: Una propuesta de poder popular plurinacional tejido desde abajo
articles.description: Leonidas Iza, candidato presidencial del movimiento político indígena ecuatoriano Pachakutik esboza sus propuestas para gobernar un Estado plurinacional.

articles.title: The Prestes Column: An Interior History of Modern Brazil (Review)
articles.description: Through storytelling and analysis, Blanc’s book tells an interior history of Brazil by recounting the political initiatives and legacy of the Prestes Column.

articles.title: Panama Canal Takeover: An Old Ghost in a Curious Present
articles.description: Trump’s threat to take back the Panama Canal signals a new era of U.S. expansionism and the greatest attack on Central American sovereignty since the 1990s.

articles.title: The Forgotten Victims: Orphans of Femicide in Colombia
articles.description: In the face of the femicide crisis in Colombia, a second crisis persists: hundreds of children are left orphaned each year and without support from the state.

articles.title: Trump and the Threat of Domestic Militarization
articles.description: Mexico’s experience with the militarization of prohibition and migration is defined by violence and displacement.

articles.title: The Disappearance of a Mapuche Elder is an Indictment of Chile’s “Progressive” Government
articles.description: Activists link the disappearance of land defender Julia Chuñil to the state’s militarization of the region and its deep ties to rapacious forestry companies.

articles.title: Debí TiRAR MáS FOToS (Review)
articles.description: Bad Bunny’s new album, which elevates the artists and musical traditions of Puerto Rico and its diaspora, comes at a time of intense concern over the effects of climate crises and gentrification.

articles.title: Forensic Crisis Reveals Institutional Responsibility in Mexico City’s Disappearance Crisis
articles.description: In Mexico City, families of the disappeared mobilize for justice for their loved ones and the accountability of forensic authorities.

articles.title: Mass Grave in Medellín Tourist Hotspot Sparks War of Words—and Walls
articles.description: The discovery in historic neighborhood Comuna 13, a neighborhood known for its art as much as its dark past, has reignited the debate over paramilitarism in the country.

articles.title: This Spanglish Bookstore In New York Is Reclaiming Bushwick’s Sense Of Latinidad
articles.description: Mil Mundos began as a means for María Herrón to reclaim her identity. Now, this bookstore’s founder is building a community among Latines in Brooklyn, New York.

articles.title: Complicating Humanitarianism in a World Scarred by an Empire of Borders (Review)
articles.description: Two recent books offer nuanced explanations behind the increased violence and militarization toward criminalized immigration in the U.S.-Mexico borderlands.

articles.title: Call For Pitches | Green Capitalism in the Americas
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for our Fall 2025 issue on Green Capitalism in the lead-up to COP30 in Belém, Brazil in November. Send us your pitches by February 3, 2025.

articles.title: Our Comrades in Havana: Cuba, the Soviet Union, and Eastern Europe, 1959-1991 (Review)
articles.description: Yordanov’s new book fills a gap in the historiography of the Cuban Revolution by centering Cuba’s post-revolutionary relations with its Eastern European allies.

articles.title: A New Mall for the Village: How Carbon Credits Impact Indigenous People in Guyana
articles.description: In 2022, Guyana became the first country in the world to issue carbon credits on a national scale. Indigenous people say they were excluded from the negotiations and criticize the loss of autonomy in their territories.

articles.title: The U.S. Is Helping Brazilian Police Kill
articles.description: Brazil’s highly militarized policing disproportionately impacts poor and racialized communities. By providing funding and training, the United States has helped exacerbate the crisis.

articles.title: Editors’ Picks 2024: The Best of NACLA’s Online Coverage
articles.description: Spanning ten countries and covering themes of extractivism, youth organizing, U.S. intervention, paramilitarism, and more, join us in taking a look back at the most memorable web articles of 2024.

articles.title: NACLA Editors’ Photo Picks 2024
articles.description: Ten examples of the best original photography published by NACLA online this year.

articles.title: Hermanos fi al-Muqawama, Brothers in the Resistance
articles.description: In the heat of parallel struggles against imperial oppression, one of the most prominent historical ideologues of El Salvador’s revolutionary movement played a key role in forging solidarity with the Middle East.

articles.title: A Genocidal Special Relationship
articles.description: Guatemala and Israel boast a long friendship dating to the formation of the Zionist state. Their shared histories of violence against Mayas and Palestinians bely each state’s claims to liberation.

articles.title: In the U.S. South, Latin American Diaspora Organizes for Palestinian Liberation
articles.description: From Texas to North Carolina, Latine solidarity organizers connect the dots between U.S. imperialism in the Americas and Israel’s colonization of Palestine.

articles.title: For Palestine, from Ayiti
articles.description: Apartheid and genocide in the occupied territories hold up a mirror to the racist exclusion of Haitians and Black people in the Dominican Republic. Anti-imperialist solidarity is imperative.

articles.title: Portable Postsocialisms: New Cuban Mediascapes after the End of History (Review)
articles.description: Paloma Duong’s book explores Cuba’s changing media landscape’s effect on local and global perceptions of the country by analyzing popular Cuban music and recent leftist travel literature on Cuba.

articles.title: The Campus Rebellion for Palestine
articles.description: Across the hemisphere, students demanded an end to the genocide in Gaza. How they navigated repression and resistance offers lessons for the solidarity movement.

articles.title: El campus en rebelión por Palestina
articles.description: A lo largo del hemisferio, estudiantes se levantaron para exigir el cese del genocidio en Gaza. Cómo navegaron la resistencia nos da lecciones para la solidaridad.

articles.title: ¿Quiénes Somos Todos? Libertad, igualdad y fraternidad en Cuba (Review)
articles.description: Julio César Guanche’s book offers a reinterpretation of Cuba’s enduring democratic-republican tradition, unearthing its popular and egalitarian historical roots.

articles.title: Trump y la izquierda latinoamericana
articles.description: Los gobiernos progresistas de América Latina no tiene el mismo grado de cohesión que durante la década anterior. ¿Lograrán contener la avalancha de una segunda travesía trumpista?

articles.title: Trump and the Latin American Left
articles.description: Progressive governments in Latin America are not as united today as they were a decade ago. How prepared are they to navigate the onslaught of a second Trump administration?

articles.title: Sitios de memoria bajo amenaza
articles.description: A medida que aumenta la extrema derecha en el Cono Sur y más allá, el negacionismo de la dictadura amenaza con hacer retroceder décadas de progreso hacia la verdad y la justicia para los desaparecidos.

articles.title: A Narrow Frente Amplio Victory in Uruguay
articles.description: A former teacher and provincial mayor won a close runoff vote in Uruguay, signaling a return to the social welfare politics of the center-left Frente Amplio coalition.

articles.title: Resistencias a los avances de Israel desde arriba y abajo
articles.description: Los gobiernos progresistas de toda América Latina han pedido el fin del genocidio en Gaza. Pero muchos movimientos exigen acciones más profundas: cortar los lazos militares y económicos.

articles.title: To Exist is to Resist ¡Viva Palestina Libre!
articles.description: The Winter 2024 issue of the NACLA Report explores transcontinental encounters between the land of historical Palestine and the land we know as the Americas.

articles.title: Call For Pitches | Central America(s): Sites of Knowledge Production
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for an issue on sites of knowledge production in Central America and the diaspora. Send your pitches by December 13.

articles.title: Paraguayan Sorrow: Writings of Rafael Barrett, a Radical Voice in a Dispossessed Land (Review)
articles.description: Translated for the first time into English by William Costa, Rafael Barrett’s text, originally published in 1911, offers political and spiritual solutions to Paraguay’s social injustices following the War of the Triple Alliance.

articles.title: “If we Fall, Others Will Follow”: Organizing to Combat Anti-Haitianism Tomorrow and Beyond
articles.description: The quest for white dominance has required undermining Haiti’s freedom and demonizing its people. A transnational response is necessary to foster solidarity and challenge the notion of U.S. exceptionalism.

articles.title: Life Beyond the Darién for Children on the Move
articles.description: Children now represent 1 in 5 people traversing the Darién Gap. The repercussions of crossing the jungle on children’s mental health have become a major challenge for humanitarian organizations.

articles.title: Global Guyana’s Call for Reckoning (Review)
articles.description: Oneka LaBennett’s recent book excavates layers of myth-making and storytelling in Guyana to examine the gendered dimensions of environmental extractivism and global capitalism.

articles.title: Bolivia en su laberinto
articles.description: La crisis económica y política en Bolivia es un síntoma de la decadencia moral del partido Movimiento al Socialismo, que pone en cuestión sus otrora promesas y horizontes revolucionarios.

articles.title: Bolivia in its Labyrinth
articles.description: Bolivia’s economic and political crisis is a symptom of the moral decay of the Movement Towards Socialism party, putting into question its once revolutionary promises and horizons.

articles.title: The Multiplication of Autonomies in Latin America
articles.description: From food production to health and community justice, practices and experiments in autonomy across Latin America cultivate a thirdway alternative to electoral reform and revolutionary struggle.

articles.title: Freedom Corner: Inside a Buenos Aires Prison Cooperative
articles.description: In the face of Javier Milei’s dramatic cuts to public funding, a prison cooperative fights to keep supporting free education for incarcerated people.

articles.title: Esquina Libertad: Dentro de una cooperativa penitenciaria en Buenos Aires
articles.description: Frente a los drásticos recortes que Javier Milei hizo a la financiación pública, una cooperativa penitenciaria lucha por seguir apoyando la educación gratuita de las personas privadas de la libertad.

articles.title: On Cuba: Reflections on 70 Years of Revolution and Struggle (Review)
articles.description: The recent book by Noam Chomsky and Vishay Prashad provides a critical analysis of the U.S. empire’s treatment of Cuba, from the Cuban Revolution to the present.

articles.title: Evo Morales at the Center of a Firestorm
articles.description: Bolivia enters a multi-sided and violent crisis as the former president, facing sexual abuse charges and gunfire, presses to appear on the ballot again.

articles.title: Despertando el gigante dormido: dentro del movimiento estudiantil en Argentina
articles.description: Tras la ola de tomas universitarias en Argentina, estudiantes se disputan la trayectoria del movimiento.

articles.title: Awakening the Sleeping Giant: Inside Argentina’s Student Movement
articles.description: Behind Argentina’s tide of university occupations, student groups spar over the movement’s trajectory.

articles.title: The Haitian Community Needs Another March on Brooklyn Bridge
articles.description: Renewed attacks on Haitian immigrants in the lead-up to the U.S. presidential election have created fertile ground for mass mobilization.

articles.title: The Struggle for Energy Sovereignty in Puerto Rico’s Gubernatorial Elections
articles.description: In Puerto Rico, debates over energy sovereignty and the political struggle for self-determination take center stage in the upcoming gubernatorial election.

articles.title: “Arrasar con la universidad pública es arrasar con un sueño”
articles.description: El acceso gratuito a la educación superior ha sido durante mucho tiempo una promesa de movilidad ascendente en Argentina. Con los severos recortes presupuestarios de Javier Milei, esa promesa puede estar llegando a su fin.

articles.title: Destroying Dreams by Dismantling the Public University
articles.description: Free access to higher education has long held the promise of upward mobility in Argentina. Under Javier Milei’s severe budget cuts, that promise may be coming to an end.
