articles.title: Anti-Haitianism Beyond Borders: Toward a Hemispheric Approach
articles.description: This series brings together perspectives on Haitian migration and experiences of anti-Haitianism across the continent, from the Midwest to the Dominican Republic and beyond.

articles.title: Propuestas de regulaciones amenazan el futuro de la partería en México
articles.description: Un proyecto pone en riesgo el trabajo y los medios de vida de las parteras tradicionales. Una red de parteras autónomas exige un enfoque intercultural e interseccional para la atención de la salud reproductiva.

articles.title: Murder of Liberation Theology Priest Sends Chill Through Chiapas
articles.description: Violence is on the rise in Zapatista territory. As an Indigenous “peacemaker” becomes the latest victim, communities call for justice, not militarization.

articles.title: The Myth of American Idealism: How U.S. Foreign Policy Endangers the World (Review)
articles.description: Chomsky and Robinson’s new critical survey of American foreign policy seeks to dispel the myth that the United States is devoted to promoting democracy and human rights.

articles.title: In a Climate of Slander and Hate, Haitian Immigrants Organize Across Borders
articles.description: In the face of mounting attacks on Haitian diaspora communities from Springfield to Santo Domingo, immigrants across the hemisphere are coming together to demand protection.

articles.title: El nuevo Caucus de El Salvador parece dispuesta a cumplir los objetivos de Bukele en el Congreso
articles.description: Mientras siguen acumulándose las violaciones de derechos humanos bajo el estado en El Salvador, un grupo bipartidista de legisladores estadounidenses intenta encubrir los abusos.

articles.title: Remembering Juan López: The Deadly Fight for Environmental Justice in Honduras
articles.description: The assassination of Honduran water defender Juan López offers a chilling reminder of the threats local leaders face in the most dangerous region in the world for environmental activists.

articles.title: Proposed Regulations Threaten the Future of Midwifery in Mexico
articles.description: A draft health standard puts the labor and livelihoods of traditional midwives at risk. A network of autonomous midwives calls for an intercultural and intersectional approach to reproductive health care.

articles.title: The Force of Witness: Contra Feminicide (Review)
articles.description: Rosa-Linda Fregoso’s book invites readers to become collective witnesses and develop radical hope to respond to feminicide and gender violence.

articles.title: El sionismo cristiano en El Salvador bajo Bukele
articles.description: La formulación del presidente Nayib Bukele de la seguridad como una batalla espiritual entre el bien y el mal ayuda a explicar su popularidad y su apoyo a Israel.

articles.title: Christian Zionism in Bukele’s El Salvador
articles.description: President Nayib Bukele’s framing of security as a spiritual battle between good and evil helps to explain his popularity and his support for Israel.

articles.title: Brazil Is Burning and Agribusiness Must Be Held Liable
articles.description: As blazes set new records, it is important to denormalize the framing of forest destruction as a simple natural cycle, detached from criminal activity, intentional deforestation, economic interests, and climate change.

articles.title: Anti-Haitianism: A Hemispheric Rejection of Revolutionary Blackness
articles.description: From the United States to the Dominican Republic to the Bahamas, the collective scapegoating and mass deportation of Haitians for political gain lays bare a particular kind of anti-Blackness.

articles.title: Cash, Clothes, and Construction: Rethinking Value in Bolivia’s Pluri-economy (Review)
articles.description: By centering Indigenous women’s lived experiences, Maclean’s book presents a gendered analysis of the pluri-economy under MAS leadership in Bolivia.

articles.title: Coca without Borders
articles.description: Long associated with the Andean region, coca cultivation is expanding rapidly in Central America. As eradication efforts intensify, local communities may be in the crossfire.

articles.title: “Pinche Estado, Count Them Well”: In Mexico, the Struggle for Truth and Memory Continues
articles.description: A decade after 43 students were forcibly disappeared from Iguala, Mexico, demands to uncover the truth of the Ayotzinapa case persist.

articles.title: Organizing Against Anti-Haitianism Beyond Borders
articles.description: As anti-Haitianism surges in the lead-up to the U.S. presidential election, confronting the rise in xenophobia and hate requires a hemispheric approach to U.S. imperialism.

articles.title: Love and Despair: How Catholic Activism Shaped Politics and the Counterculture in Modern Mexico (Review)
articles.description: The recent book by Jaime Pensado explores how the social, cultural, and political engagement of Mexican Catholics shaped the country’s long Sixties.

articles.title: Ayotzi Vive, La Lucha Sigue
articles.description: On the 10th anniversary of the disappearance of 43 students in Iguala, Mexico, this collection of NACLA coverage contextualizes the eventand the government’s ongoing role in obscuring the truth.

articles.title: Ten Years of Impunity: AMLO and the Betrayal of Ayotzinapa
articles.description: Last month, nine years and 11 months after their children were disappeared, the parents of the 43 students ended their relationship with Mexico’s current government. In the absence of justice, the state’s mask has fallen.

articles.title: “Violence Is Deeply Intertwined with the State”
articles.description: Ten years after the Ayotzinapa case blew the lid off Mexico’s ongoing disappearance crisis, anthropologist Claudio Lomnitz unpacks the much bigger picture surrounding the 43 students.

articles.title: La última revolución: La insurrección sandinista y la guerra fría interamericana (Reseña)
articles.description: El libro de Gerardo Sánchez Nateras utiliza diversos archivos centroamericanos para presentar alternativas a las narrativas dominantes sobre la revolución nicaragüense.

articles.title: The Last Revolution: The Sandinista Insurrection and the Inter-American Cold War (Review)
articles.description: Gerardo Sánchez Nateras’s book uses a variety of Central American archives to present alternatives to the dominant narratives about the Nicaraguan revolution.

articles.title: Somos Pluri: Embracing the Diversity of Abya Yala’s Feminist Movements
articles.description: A continental feminist network enshrined its commitment to plurinationalism. What comes next in the collective struggle of women and trans, travesti, and nonbinary people across the hemisphere?

articles.title: “Somos una alternativa para revitalizar al movimiento indígena”
articles.description: Desde la amazonia peruana, comunidades indigenas estan constuyendo gobiernos territoriales autónomas priopios, sin el apoyo del estado. Una líder de la Nación Chapra relata su experiencia.

articles.title: A Multinational, Multiethnic Alternative in Chile’s Migrant Settlements
articles.description: Grassroots migrant communities in the northern border region have organized plurinational politics from below, pushing back against criminalization by asserting plurality on their own terms.

articles.title: From Bolivia, A Vision for a Grassroots Feminist Constitution
articles.description: For anarchist feminist María Galindo, the plurinational state was always doomed to reproduce oppression. A radical, anticapitalist alternative calls for rethinking colonial and patriarchal relations.

articles.title: The Fate of Peruvian Democracy: Political Violence, Human Rights, and the Legal Left (Review)
articles.description: Tamara Feinstein’s carefully researched text offers a comprehensive map of the rise and demise of the Left in Peru.

articles.title: As Guatemalan Democracy Falters, Indigenous Communities Stand Their Ground
articles.description: Ancestral authorities played a decisive role in counteracting a right-wing backlash. In their vision for liberation, alternatives are built from the grassroots, regardless of the government in power.

articles.title: Splinter FARC Groups Dispute Historical Guerrilla Heartland
articles.description: In Colombia’s Pato River valley and wider Caguán basin, former combatants are caught in the crosshairs as peacebuilding efforts clash with dissident groups in the struggle to define the region’s legacy.

articles.title: La violencia de la ausencia
articles.description: La desaparición atraviesa las sociedades de las Américas. El número del NACLA Report “¿Dónde están?” aborda el dolor profunda, la pena y la vida emocional de las luchas dignas en la región por la justicia.

articles.title: Tracing the Anti-Haitianism Behind the Springfield Scapegoating
articles.description: Old racist tropes demonizing Haitians as uncivilized practitioners of barbaric or mysterious rituals have been revived, once again casting immigrants from Haiti as dangerous outsiders unworthy of protection or empathy.

articles.title: “Whose Side Are You On?” Building Palestine Solidarity Through Music
articles.description: In the tradition of music as a medium for struggles for freedom, Rod Starz of hip hop duo Rebel Diaz insists on wielding dissident cultural resistance to strengthen solidarity between the Americas and Palestine.

articles.title: The Assassin of Illusions: Alberto Fujimori’s Long-Lasting Legacy
articles.description: The former strongman’s political project lives on in his ideology of Fujimorismo, championed by his polarizing daughter. Peru’s collective memory will be the judge in the face of enduring impunity.

articles.title: Las disputas por lo plurinacional de las mujeres indígenas en el Estado
articles.description: Funcionarias de Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, y Panamá relatan sus experiencias y los desafíos de navegar instituciones estatales coloniales, patriarcales y racistas.

articles.title: Desde Bolivia, una visión para una constitución feminista de base
articles.description: Para la feminista anarquista María Galindo, el Estado plurinacional siempre estuvo condenado a reproducir la opresión. Una alternativa radical y anticapitalista exige repensar las relaciones coloniales y patriarcales.

articles.title: New El Salvador Caucus Appears Set to Do Bukele’s Bidding in Congress
articles.description: As human rights violations under El Salvador’s more than two-year-long state of exception continue to mount, a bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers seeks to whitewash the abuses.

articles.title: Una perspectiva indianista critica del Estado Plurinacional en Bolivia
articles.description: Indianismo, una ideología surgida del pensamiento aymara y quechua, ayudó a llevar al poder a Evo Morales. ¿Qué es el balance del proyecto de descolonización de su gobierno?

articles.title: El Estado lumpen: Radiografía de la extrema derecha ejerciendo el poder en el Perú
articles.description: Mientras el gobierno represivo de Perú se vuelve cada vez más autoritario, los movimientos sociales y otros bastiones de la resistencia deben persistir en crear espacios para la democracia.

articles.title: Una propuesta desde los Andes para la sostenibilidad de la vida
articles.description: La constitución plurinacional del Ecuador marcó la historia al consagrar el compromiso al “Buen Vivir” reconocer los derechos de la Pachamama. Aún no se ha hecho realidad este cambio radical de paradigma.

articles.title: Remembering the U.S. Invasion of Panama, 35 Years Later | Under the Shadow, Ep. 13
articles.description: The 1989 invasion left deep wounds in Panama, including an untold number of people killed. Locals continue to demand justice.

articles.title: The Lumpen State: X-ray of Peru’s Far Right in Power
articles.description: As Peru’s repressive government slides deeper into authoritarianism, social movements and other bastions of resistance must persist in carving out space for democracy.

articles.title: In Patagonia, Green Energy Projects Raise Fears of a New Colonialism
articles.description: Green hydrogen in southern Chile elicits glowing rhetoric from energy advocates. But unless benefits are shared with locals, the project could replicate harmful inequalities.

articles.title: Remembering the U.S. Invasion of Panama, 35 Years Later | Under the Shadow, Ep. 13
articles.description: The 1989 invasion left deep wounds in Panama, including an untold number of people killed. Locals continue to demand justice.

articles.title: La batalla contra la memoria avanza en Argentina
articles.description: El gobierno toma medidas contra las políticas de memoria sostenidas durante 40 años de democracia, mientras diputados oficialistas visitan a presos condenados por crímenes de lesa humanidad.

articles.title: Battle Against Memory Moves Forward in Argentina
articles.description: As the government takes aim at memory policies upheld during the past 40 years of democracy, pro-government lawmakers visit prisoners convicted of crimes against humanity.

articles.title: War in Words: An Intellectual History of the “Narco” in Mexico (Review)
articles.description: Oswaldo Zavala’s book explores the myth of the narco in Mexico and examines the change in narco-discourse, prompted by the United States.

articles.title: “It’s Not Drought, It’s Plunder”: Water Conflicts Ahead of Uruguay’s Presidential Election
articles.description: Uruguay was the first country in the world to enshrine water as a human right. Today an extractivist model threatens water sustainability and sovereignty.

articles.title: Abya Yala to Palestine
articles.description: Tracing support for Palestinian liberation allows us to see how communities in struggle can establish a basis of global politics beyond power and domination.

articles.title: Women are Rebelling in Bolivia: A Conversation with Julieta Ojeda of Mujeres Creando
articles.description: Feminist organizer Julieta Ojeda talks about her grassroots work in Bolivia for abortion access and empowering feminist struggles from below.

articles.title: Plurinationalism from Below
articles.description: The lofty promises of plurinationalism remain an unfinished project. The Fall 2024 NACLA Report explores how plurinational politics are organized and articulated at the margins of state power.

articles.title: Palestine and Latin America: Deep Ties Rooted in Shared Experiences
articles.description: Unraveling the multiple solidarities found across our continent that have converged in support of Palestinian life and dignity.

articles.title: Palestina y América Latina: Lazos profundos por experiencias comunes
articles.description: Desenvolviendo las múltiples solidaridades que se encuentran en nuestro continente y que han confluido en apoyo de la vida y la dignidad palestina.

articles.title: How Community Radio Supports Bolivia’s Campesino Movements
articles.description: Journalist José Aramayo discusses how radio plays a central role in the political and social life of Bolivia and its campesino movements.

articles.title: Weaving Solidarities Across Abya Yala and Palestine
articles.description: In Latin America, Palestine not only represents the struggle of a people refusing to submit to subjugation and dispossession, but also evokes the spirit of perseverance, resistance, and the desire to live a dignified life.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Diverse Lefts Need Internationalist Solidarity
articles.description: Amid the polarized conflict between the government and opposition, critical leftist voices struggle to advance their own political demands.

articles.title: Faces of Water: Women Water Defenders in Mexico and Uruguay
articles.description: From Mexico City to Montevideo, women are leading the fight to protect their communities’ water from extractive projects.

articles.title: Sexual Political Violence and State Terrorism in Chile (Review)
articles.description: Maldonado Garay’s book explores the connection between neoliberalism and sexual political violence under Pinochet’s dictatorship.

articles.title: Un pueblo colombiano resiste a las empresas del carbón
articles.description: El pueblo de Cañaverales es el primero en beneficiarse de un programa destinado a proteger a las comunidades campesinas del desarrollo industrial, pero el poder empresarial sigue siendo un gran obstáculo para la justicia.

articles.title: A Town in Colombia Fights to Keep Coal Companies Out
articles.description: Cañaverales is the first to benefit from a new government program aimed at protecting campesino communities from industrial development, but corporate power remains a major obstacle to justice and dignity for its people.

articles.title: The Indigenous and Feminist Media Speaking Truth to Power in Bolivia
articles.description: In this multi-part interview series, journalist and scholar Benjamin Dangl speaks with veteran and emerging Indigenous and feminist media makers and community organizers in Bolivia.

articles.title: Indigenous Journalist Rosa Jalja Builds Community through Bolivia’s Radio Copacabana
articles.description: On the shores of Lake Titicaca, the Aymara journalist has spent over 50 years informing and strengthening community through the airwaves.

articles.title: The Legacy of the Monroe Doctrine | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 3
articles.description: Historian Greg Grandin examines the long shadow of the Monroe Doctrine, which continues to shape U.S. imperialism in Latin America.

articles.title: The Legacy of the Monroe Doctrine | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 3
articles.description: Historian Greg Grandin examines the long shadow of the Monroe Doctrine, which continues to shape U.S. imperialism in Latin America.

articles.title: 15 Years of Land Defense and Community Building in Xayakalan
articles.description: Indigenous Nahua community members recovered their ancestral land in Michoacan on Mexico’s Pacific coast in 2009. Amid legal and criminal violence, the struggle continues.

articles.title: The Faces of Feminist Resistance, from Palestine to Puerto Rico
articles.description: A long legacy of women freedom fighters connects liberation struggles across space and time, animating solidarity movements between occupied and colonial territories today.

articles.title: Bolivia’s Jichha Media Builds Indigenous Power
articles.description: Indigenous thinker and writer Wilmer Machaca talks about the political vision behind Jichha, a digital space for exploring and building Indianist social change across the Andes.

articles.title: Bolivia’s Muy Waso Is Making Feminist Journalism in the Streets
articles.description: The editors of the feminist media outlet speak about making grassroots journalism that reflects the realities and social movements of the country.

articles.title: Has the Wolf Arrived in Venezuela?
articles.description: Maduro’s rushed victory proclamation without a definitive vote count thrusts Venezuela back into crisis, while the opposition presents contradicting results.

articles.title: ¿Llegó el lobo a Venezuela?
articles.description: La apresurada proclamación de victoria de Maduro sin un recuento definitivo de votos vuelve a sumergir a Venezuela en crisis, mientras la oposición presenta resultados contradictorios.

articles.title: El resurgimiento paramilitar provoca desplazamientos masivos en Chiapas
articles.description: En junio, más de 12.000 personas se vieron obligadas a abandonar sus hogares en el pueblo de Tila. A medida que se intensifican las disputas históricas por la tierra, varios grupos armados luchan por el control territorial.

articles.title: A Permanent State of Mal Gobierno: Analyzing Peruvian State Violence on its Own Terms
articles.description: Violence against protesters, anti-LGBTQ policies, and a submission to the International Criminal Court accusing President Dina Boluarte of human rights violations reflect the need for Peru to confront its own political violence.

articles.title: In Venezuelan Elections, Bringing Migrants Back Home Is on the Ballot
articles.description: Like in the United States, migration is a big talking point in Venezuela’s election. After an exodus of 7.7 million in the last decade, candidates are promising to help them return.

articles.title: Call for Pitches | Cuerpos furiosos: Travesti, Trans, Cuir Politics in Revolt
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for an issue on travesti, trans, and queer activisms in the Americas. Send us your pitches by August 21, 2024.

articles.title: Venezuela’s  Elections: ¿Qué pasará? (Review)
articles.description: Venezuela heads to the polls on July 28. Ociel Alí López’s new book offers a timely look at the context, key players, and what’s at stake.

articles.title: Indigenous Journalist Yolanda Mamani Speaks Out
articles.description: The Aymara radio broadcaster and YouTuber produces feminist media in Bolivia to amplify the struggles of women workers and promote solidarity and mutual aid.

articles.title: The Panama Canal Zone, a U.S. Enclave in Latin America | Under the Shadow, Ep. 12
articles.description: A strategic shipping corridor, Panama became home to Washington’s most important asset in the region and the base of its military training apparatus.

articles.title: The Panama Canal Zone, a U.S. Enclave in Latin America | Under the Shadow, Ep. 12
articles.description: A strategic shipping corridor, Panama became home to Washington’s most important asset in the region and the base of its military training apparatus.

articles.title: Paramilitary Resurgence Drives Mass Displacement in Chiapas
articles.description: In June, more than 12,000 people were forced out of their homes in the former Zapatista stronghold of Tila. As historic land disputes escalate, several armed groups fight for territorial control.

articles.title: Dando a luz al futuro: Las parteras logran un gran avance en México
articles.description: Las parteras tradicionales lograron el derecho a expedir certificados de nacimiento, un paso clave para subsanar las deficiencias de salud materna en comunidades indígenas. Pero su lucha por la protección y la autonomía continúa.

articles.title: A Tapestry of Missing Migrants Stitches a Memory of the Disappeared
articles.description: More than 5,000 migrants have died or disappeared attempting to cross the U.S.-Mexico border over the last decade. Families embroider the names of their missing loved ones as a way of telling their stories.

articles.title: What’s Left of Morena?
articles.description: Caught between conflicting notions of “democracy,” leveled on one hand by a fearmongering right and on the other by a critical grassroots left, Mexico’s government must grapple with its undemocratic condition of global dependence.

articles.title: Three Years After Cuba’s 11J, Discontent and Persecution Continue
articles.description: Since the outbreak of the largest protests seen in nearly three decades, the Cuban government has tightened its grip on the economy, further reducing citizens’ freedoms.

articles.title: The U.S. Shadow Over Costa Rica The Panama Canal Zone, a U.S. Enclave in Latin America | Under the Shadow, Ep. 11
articles.description: The abolition of Costa Rica’s military 75 years ago has been highly celebrated at home and abroad. Yet the story is more complicated than the myth lets on.

articles.title: The U.S. Shadow Over Costa Rica | Under the Shadow, Ep.  11
articles.description: The abolition of Costa Rica’s military 75 years ago has been highly celebrated at home and abroad. Yet the story is more complicated than the myth lets on.

articles.title: Delivering the Future: Midwives Win Breakthrough in Mexico
articles.description: Traditional midwives have won the right to issue birth certificates, a key step toward addressing maternal health gaps in Indigenous communities. But their fight for protections and autonomy continues.

articles.title: What the Mainstream Media Isn’t Saying About Venezuela’s María Corina Machado
articles.description: Machado is not the godsend for the opposition portrayed by the media and her close supporters. But opposition leaders have more cause for hope than in the past.

articles.title: The Rebirth of Omar García Harfuch
articles.description: Mexico City’s former chief of police is believed to have participated in the forced disappearance of the 43 Ayotzinapa students in 2014. A decade later, president-elect Claudia Sheinbaum is poised to make him Mexico’s most powerful policeman.

articles.title: Spectacles of Crisis and Political Power Plays in Bolivia
articles.description: A general and his troops stormed the seat of government in an apparent coup attempt. Three theories have emerged in a context of economic turmoil and political infighting within the ruling party.

articles.title: Reappearing the Memory of Mexico’s Twice Disappeared
articles.description: As the government hides the staggering proportions of Mexico’s forensic crisis, the searching families of El Bosque de la Esperanza take control of their own narratives to resist stigmatization and erasure.

articles.title: Tracking Absence Through Presence at Colombia’s Palace of Justice
articles.description: Among the unanswered questions about the military’s response to the 1985 attack on the seat of the judiciary is what happened to the disappeared victims. New research sheds light on the role of an unassuming museum.

articles.title: “You’re One of the Disappeared?” How I Came to Accept My Place in History
articles.description: A transnational adoptee born in El Salvador and raised in the United States shared his journey to uncovering his family’s truth and finding his voice as a desaparecido.

articles.title: Lineages of Resistance, from the Mothers and Grandmothers to Mass Feminist Struggles
articles.description: As a far-right, denialist government threatens to roll back hard-won gains, Argentine feminists and the mothers and grandmothers fighting for justice for the disappeared remain linked in a decades-old friendly bond of struggle.

articles.title: The New Colonialism of Milei’s Investment Plan
articles.description: Approved amid harsh repression of protesters, the economic scheme at the heart of the Argentine government’s flagship legislation hands big perks to multinationals at the expense of territories, peoples, and the environment.

articles.title: El nuevo colonialismo del plan de inversiones de Milei
articles.description: Aprobado en medio de una dura represión, el esquema económico en el corazón de la legislación emblemática del gobierno les da grandes beneficios a las multinacionales a costo de los territorios, pueblos y el medio ambiente.

articles.title: Chomsky on U.S. Intervention in Central America | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 2
articles.description: Under the Shadow looks back on Chomsky’s 1983 lecture at the University of Colorado, as some of the worst aspects of the Reagan administration’s Cold War-era foreign policy ravaged Central America.

articles.title: Desaparición feminicida en Perú
articles.description: En las luchas por la justicia, desde el conflicto armado interno hasta la violencia de género hoy, la impunidad de estos crímenes es constante. Callarse es también una forma violenta de desaparecer a las víctimas.

articles.title: De estado de guerra a régimen de excepción
articles.description: La detención arbitraria en el contexto de una mano dura severa en El Salvador evoca la memoria dolorosa de la desaparición forzada. Defensores de derechos humanos y activistas conectan los puntos del pasado y el presente.

articles.title: “No nos callaremos ante nuestros verdugos”
articles.description: En una comunidad colombiana dedicada durante mucho tiempo a la no violencia y la defensa ambiental, dos asesinatos recientes subrayan los desafíos que enfrentan los planes del gobierno de izquierda para una paz duradera.

articles.title: Chomsky on U.S. Intervention in Central America | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 2
articles.description: Under the Shadow looks back on Chomsky’s 1983 lecture at the University of Colorado, as some of the worst aspects of the Reagan administration’s Cold War-era foreign policy ravaged Central America.

articles.title: Linajes de resistencia, de las Madres y Abuelas hasta las luchas feministas masivas
articles.description: Ante un gobierno anti derechos, negacionista y apologista de la dictadura, los feminismos argentinos y las mujeres de la lucha por los desparecidxs sostienen un vínculo amistoso de militancia.

articles.title: Arte contra el olvido en México
articles.description: Cuando la historia de la desparición forzada en México se cuenta en cifras, carece de un principio o un fin. Prácticas artísticas de resistencia buscan hacer visible la ausencia.

articles.title: Defensores territoriales garífunas luchan contra una política de exterminio
articles.description: Según lideres comunitarios, la violencia y desplazamiento que facilita el acaparamiento de tierras con fines de lucro en toda la costa caribeña de Honduras es nada menos que un genocidio contra el pueblo Garífuna.

articles.title: Hollywood and Empire | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 1
articles.description: British director Alex Cox’s 1987 film Walker, starring Ed Harris, laid an uncompromising critique of U.S. imperialism in Nicaragua. For his “a revolutionary film in a revolutionary context,” Cox was blacklisted from the industry.

articles.title: Reagan’s Contra War on Nicaragua | Under the Shadow, Ep. 10, Part II
articles.description: In the 1980s, the Reagan administration illegally funded counter-revolutionary warfare in Nicaragua. As scandal engulfed Washington, the solidarity movement pushed back against intervention.

articles.title: Reagan’s Contra War on Nicaragua | Under the Shadow, Ep. 10, Part II
articles.description: In the 1980s, the Reagan administration illegally funded counter-revolutionary warfare in Nicaragua. As scandal engulfed Washington, the solidarity movement pushed back against intervention.

articles.title: Hollywood and Empire | Under the Shadow, Bonus Episode 1
articles.description: British director Alex Cox’s 1987 film Walker, starring Ed Harris, laid an uncompromising critique of U.S. imperialism in Nicaragua. For his “a revolutionary film in a revolutionary context,” Cox was blacklisted from the industry.

articles.title: NACLA Seeks Co-Executive Editor (Non-remunerated)
articles.description: NACLA seeks a new co-Executive Editor specializing in fundraising, outreach, and administration (remote or in-person).

articles.title: Rompiendo barreras: El empoderamiento político de las mujeres indígenas de Bolivia
articles.description: La inclusión política de uno de los grupos más marginados de América Latina refleja un cambio sin precedentes, pero aún quedan muchos retos.

articles.title: Breaking Barriers: The Political Empowerment of Bolivia’s Indigenous Women
articles.description: The political inclusion of one of Latin America’s most marginalized groups reflects an unprecedented change, but many challenges remain.

articles.title: Venezuela’s D-Day: Between Civic Celebration and Worsening Conflict
articles.description: After years of boycotting elections, Venezuela’s opposition has united behind a presidential candidate. The result of the July 28 vote could give way to stabilityor still deeper polarization.

articles.title: El Día D en Venezuela: entre la fiesta cívica y la agudización del conflicto
articles.description: Tras años de abstencionismo, la oposición se ha unificado en torno a una candidatura. Los presidenciales el 28 de julio podrían abrir una estabilidad, o una polarización aún más profunda.

articles.title: NACLA 2023 Annual Report
articles.description: If you support NACLA’s mission, and want to help us work toward our strategic goals and ensure NACLA’s long-term sustainability, please consider donating to support NACLA in 2024.

articles.title: A Boricua in Gaza: “There Is a Direct Connection with Our Struggle”
articles.description: A conversation with Libre Sankara, a Puerto Rican aid worker providing humanitarian assistance in Gaza. Sankara discusses the situation in Rafah and the parallels between the Puerto Rican and Palestinian liberation struggles.

articles.title: Victims Win Historic Victory Against Chiquita in Colombia Paramilitary Case
articles.description: The ruling against the banana giant formerly known as United Fruit makes history in holding a U.S. company liable for abuses committed abroad. Lawyers say the case is just the beginning.

articles.title: The Iron Fist of José Raúl Mulino, Panama’s New President
articles.description: A former law enforcement chief under disgraced ex-President Ricardo Martinelli, Panama’s president-elect has a dubious human rights and criminal justice record.

articles.title: The Mexico of Claudia Sheinbaum
articles.description: Sheinbaum’s triumph marks a profound reconfiguration of the political system that has shaped Mexican public life since the 1990s, and a powerful bulwark against the rise of the far right.

articles.title: ¿Dónde están? Web Exclusive
articles.description: Don’t miss the web exclusive content of our Summer 2024 issue, ¿Dónde están?, on disappearance across the Americas.

articles.title: Narrating the Distance of Transnational Adoption
articles.description: For transnational adoptees wrenched from El Salvador and Guatemala in the throes of civil war, storytelling and art are powerful tools for navigating identity, dislocation, haunting, and healing.

articles.title: Paraguay, A Node in Operation Condor’s Global Disappearance Network
articles.description: ¿A dónde están? Where are they? In Paraguay, the answer to the question of those seeking justice for the disappeared is blunt: they are in the backyard of the elite police headquarters 15 minutes from downtown Asunción.

articles.title: The Double Disappearance Inside Chile’s Patio 29
articles.description: Exhumation of a mass grave in Santiago finally promised answers, but botched DNA testing left the families of victims reeling. Five decades later, the search for truth continues.

articles.title: Repertorios de sanación y disenso
articles.description: Para las Madres de Soacha, sus murales, performances y cuerpos tatuados crean un monumento vivo a través del que elaboran sus duelos y activan estrategias para revelar la historia de los “falsos positivos”.

articles.title: Repertoires of Healing and Dissent
articles.description: For the Mothers of Soacha, murals, performances, and tattoos create a living monument of grief and defiance in their search for the truth about the thousands of “false positives” killed and disappeared in Colombia.

articles.title: The Violence of Absence
articles.description: Disappearance cuts through the Americas. The pain, grief, and resilience of the region’s struggles for justice is the focus of our Summer 2024 NACLA Report, “¿Dónde están?”

articles.title: Crisis de la justicia del agua en México: no hay solución electoral
articles.description: Los candidatos a las elecciones generales en México prometían solucionar la escasez de agua, pero el problema corre más profundo que las urnas.

articles.title: México acude a las urnas en medio de violencia sin parangón
articles.description: El 2 de junio los mexicanos votarán en las elecciones más violentas de la historia del país. El temor a represalias por parte de grupos criminales y la falta de una respuesta estatal eficaz debilitan el proceso democrático.

articles.title: Reseña de Cinco días en Moscú: Mario Vargas Llosa y el socialismo soviético (1968)
articles.description: El reciente libro de Carlos Aguirre y Kristina Buynova aborda la trayectoria del famoso escritor peruano y su notorio quiebre con Cuba y Rusia.

articles.title: Controversial Maya Train Up and Running as Mexicans Cast Their Ballots
articles.description: Mexico’s new tourist train has been hailed as the Yucatán’s salvation, but experts and activists warn of the potentially devastating environmental and cultural consequences of the outgoing president’s flagship megaproject.

articles.title: Mexico Heads to Polls Amid Record Violence
articles.description: On June 2, Mexicans will vote in the most violent elections in the country’s history. Fears of reprisal from criminal groups, and the lack of an effective state response, weaken the democratic process.

articles.title: La reelección de Abinader, el empresario que se compara con el último dictador dominicano
articles.description: Luis Abinader ha transmitido un imagén de moderación, pero sus políticias en contra de las personas negras y migrantes ponen en evidencia una agenda de ultra derecha.

articles.title: Dominicans Reelect Abinader, the Businessman who Compares Himself to the Country’s Last Dictator
articles.description: Luis Abinader has cultivated an image as a moderate, but his anti-Black and anti-immigrant policies lay bare a far-right agenda.

articles.title: A Review of Five Days in Moscow: Mario Vargas Llosa and Soviet Socialism (1968)
articles.description: The recent book by Carlos Aguirre and Kristina Buynova deals with the trajectory of the famous Peruvian writer and his notorious break with Cuba and Russia.

articles.title: No Electoral Solution in Sight for Mexico’s Water Justice Crisis
articles.description: Candidates in Mexico’s upcoming general election promise to fix water scarcity, but the problem runs deeper than the polls.

articles.title: Los paramilitares neofascista de República Dominicana fortalecen la represión derechista
articles.description: Las nuevas expresiones de violencia ultranacionalista que censuran a las mujeres negras y a los migrantes evocan a la dictadura. Cualquier persona considerada una amenaza para los valores dominicanos es un objetivo potencial.

articles.title: The Case for Open Borders: A Book Review from the Banks of the Rio Grande
articles.description: John Washington’s new book attempts to break open the political discourse on borders, showing us that another world is possible.

articles.title: Why the Nicaraguan Revolution Matters Today | Under the Shadow, Ep. 10, Part I
articles.description: The 1979 Sandinista victory over the Somoza dictatorship sparked hope across Central America and beyond. Nicaragua quickly became ground zero of a violent U.S.-backed counterrevolutionary war.

articles.title: Why the Nicaraguan Revolution Matters Today | Under the Shadow, Ep. 10, Part I
articles.description: The 1979 Sandinista victory over the Somoza dictatorship sparked hope across Central America and beyond. Nicaragua quickly became ground zero of a violent U.S.-backed counterrevolutionary war.

articles.title: “They Underestimated the Student Movement”: Paraguayan Youth Fight to Defend Affordable Education
articles.description: Education subsidy funding changes sparked mass protests. A weeks-long occupation of Paraguay’s largest university forced the right-wing government to the table.

articles.title: Awakening the Ashes: An Intellectual History of the Haitian Revolution (Review)
articles.description: A new book recovers the intellectual contribution of Haitian revolutionaries to ideas of freedom, equality, and sovereignty in the Atlantic World.

articles.title: “They’re Making It up as They Go”: Inside the Response to Brazil’s Deadly Floods
articles.description: Climate change and poor disaster preparedness have exacerbated the impacts of historic floods that have left parts of southern Brazil underwater.

articles.title: Viva Palestina! NYC Student Encampments Spark National Rebellion
articles.description: Latinx students are standing with Palestine. For them, the occupation and oppression of Palestine are inseparable from the U.S.- and Israel-backed militarization of Latin America.

articles.title: ¡Viva Palestina! Acampamentos universitarios de Nueva York provocan rebelión nacional
articles.description: Estudiantes latinxs están con Palestina. Para ellxs, la ocupación y la opresión de Palestina son inseparables de la militarización de Latinoamérica respaldada por Estados Unidos e Israel.

articles.title: Mujeres argentinas proponen soluciones colectivas a un sistema punitivista
articles.description: Yo No Fui es un colectivo artístico feminista y anti-carcelario que apoya y defiende a las mujeres y personas LGBTQ+ dentro y fuera de los complejos penitenciarios de Buenos Aires.

articles.title: Argentine Women Find Collective Solutions to a Punitive Prison System
articles.description: Yo No Fui is a feminist and anti-prison artistic collective that supports and advocates for women and LGBTQ+ people inside and outside penitentiary complexes in Buenos Aires.

articles.title: Nicaragua Must Survive: Sandinista Revolutionary Diplomacy in the Global Cold War (Review)
articles.description: Eline van Ommen’s new book presents a nuanced exploration of Sandinista international relations that weaves together impressive archival research.

articles.title: La energía comunitaria en Colombia: Un nuevo paradigma desde el territorio
articles.description: Para un investigador y miembro del movimiento ambiental colombiano, enfrentar la crisis climática y las falsas soluciones propuestas para abordarla implica transformar la sociedad tal y como la conocemos.

articles.title: Community Energy in Colombia: A New Paradigm from the Grassroots
articles.description: For one researcher and member of the Colombian environmental movement, confronting the climate crisisand the false solutions proposed to address itmeans transforming society as we know it.

articles.title: “¡Viva la universidad, carajo!” Argentinos marchan en defensa de la educación pública
articles.description: Cientos de miles de personas salieron a la calle en una movilización inédita en rechazo de recortes del presupuesto de universidades públicas del presidente Javier Milei.

articles.title: “¡Viva la universidad, carajo!” Argentines March in Defense of Public Education
articles.description: Hundreds of thousands took to the streets in an unprecedented mobilization to reject President Javier Milei’s budget cuts to public universities.

articles.title: The Low-Carbon Contradiction: Energy Transition, Geopolitics, and the Infrastructural State in Cuba (Review)
articles.description: Gustav Cederlöf’s book poses challenging questions about energy transition and energy justice from an often-overlooked Caribbean and socialist vantage point.

articles.title: Milei Moves Forward with the Gutting of Public Media
articles.description: The shutdown of a state-owned media agency reinforces the concentration of content production and threatens the right to information in Argentina.

articles.title: Milei avanza con el vaciamiento de los medios públicos
articles.description: El cierre de una agencia de medios estatal refuerza la concentración de producción de contenidos y amenaza el derecho a la información en Argentina.

articles.title: Honduras: Un narco-estado hecho en Estados Unidos
articles.description: Aunque Juan Orlando Herna?ndez fue condenado por tra?fico de drogas en un tribunal de Nueva York, EE.UU. au?n no ha reconocido su papel en fomentar el tra?fico de droga patrocinado por el Estado en Honduras.

articles.title: Remembering the Longest U.S. Occupation in Latin American History | Under the Shadow, Ep. 9
articles.description: Augusto Sandino is celebrated as a Nicaraguan revolutionary and liberator. The U.S invasion he resisted set the stage for dictatorship and, later, revolution.

articles.title: Remembering the Longest U.S. Occupation in Latin American History | Under the Shadow, Ep. 9
articles.description: Augusto Sandino is celebrated as a Nicaraguan revolutionary and liberator. The U.S invasion he resisted set the stage for dictatorship and, later, revolution.

articles.title: Solidarity with El Salvador’s Santa Marta 5 Grows Across Borders
articles.description: The Bukele administration’s case against five community leaders, environmental activists, and former FMLN members sparks international outcry.

articles.title: Honduras: A Narco-State Made in the United States
articles.description: Although Juan Orlando Hernández was convicted of drug trafficking in a New York court, the United States has yet to own up to its role in fostering state-sponsored drug trafficking in Honduras.

articles.title: Gustavo Petro se mantiene firme respecto a Palestina
articles.description: A pesar de los lazos históricos de Colombia con Israel y Estados Unidos, Petro ha sido uno de los líderes más vocales en su apoyo a Palestina, haciendo un llamado a la paz en su país y en el extranjero.

articles.title: William Walker: The Gringo Who Tried to Rule Central America | Under the Shadow, Ep. 8
articles.description: In the 19th century, U.S. filibusters invaded and annexed Latin American territories in the name of Manifest Destiny. One man’s quest to conquer Nicaragua shows the deep roots of U.S. efforts to “spread democracy” abroad.

articles.title: William Walker: The Gringo Who Tried to Rule Central America | Under the Shadow, Ep. 8
articles.description: In the 19th century, U.S. filibusters invaded and annexed Latin American territories in the name of Manifest Destiny. One man’s quest to conquer Nicaragua shows the deep roots of U.S. efforts to “spread democracy” abroad.

articles.title: Through the Night Like a Snake: Latin American Horror Stories (Review)
articles.description: A new collection from Two Lines Press presents 10 translated stories from contemporary Latin American writers that explore the unsettling, unusual, and unspoken.

articles.title: Cuando la tierra pierde a sus guardianes
articles.description: Más de 1 millón de colombianos han sido obligados a huir de sus territorios desde los acuerdos de paz de 2016. A medida que las industrias extractivas y los grupos armados capitalizan el desplazamiento, la biodiversidad sufre.

articles.title: Rio’s Street Poets Have Something to Say
articles.description: The artists who sell their poems on the streets of Rio de Janeiro see the occupation of public space as a necessary effort to expand self-expression and access to art.

articles.title: The New Public Art: Collectivity and Activism in Mexico since the 1980’s (Review)
articles.description: Mara Polgovsky Ezcurra’s edited volume traces the emergence of new, non-state-led forms of public art in Mexico in the age of neoliberalism.

articles.title: When the Earth Loses its Stewards
articles.description: More than 1 million Colombians have been forced to flee their territories since the 2016 peace accords. As extractive industries and armed groups capitalize on displacement, biodiversity suffers.

articles.title: Las ultra derechas latinoamericanas reencarnadas
articles.description: Contenidos exclusivos en español sobre la nueva ola ultaderechista, publicados en inglés en la edición de primavera de 2024 de la revista trimestral NACLA Report.

articles.title: Extreme Rights 2.0, A Big Global Family
articles.description: From Spain’s Vox to Argentina’s Javier Milei, the forces of the new far right don’t resurrect historical fascism. But they are the greatest threat to democracy today.

articles.title: Mario Vargas Llosa: El vendedor de ideas neoliberales más influyente de América Latina
articles.description: ¿Cómo se convirtió uno de los escritores más célebres de la literatura latinoamericana del siglo XX en impulsor y mentor de la nueva derecha reaccionaria de la región?

articles.title: Anticomunismo del siglo XXI en Brasil
articles.description: Las fuerzas conservadoras han fusionado su rechazo visceral al Partido de los Trabajadores con tropos anticomunistas tradicionales, alimentando el ascenso de la extrema derecha autoritaria.

articles.title: Bolsonarismo, um fenômeno criado pelo próprio Brasil?
articles.description: O ex-presidente Jair Bolsonaro chegou ao poder em diálogo com forças globais de extrema direita. Mas as raízes históricas do movimento associado a ele são exclusivamente brasileiras.

articles.title: Cazar a Drácula, incubar monstruos
articles.description: Una red transnacional conservadora ha ayudado a impulsar el ascenso de la ultraderecha. En primera fila de la ofensiva se encuentra la Fundación Internacional para la Libertad.

articles.title: La ola de ultraderecha llega a Uruguay
articles.description: Una nueva derecha radical vinculada con la dictadura ha logrado avances electorales inéditos. Hasta ahora, una institucionalidad y democracia solída ha atenuado sus peores impactos.

articles.title: The Far-Right Tide Reaches Uruguay
articles.description: A new radical right with links to the dictatorship has made unprecedented gains. So far, the country’s strong democracy has tempered its worst impacts.

articles.title: Bukele acumula poder con mano dura
articles.description: Aprovechando los resultados de su “guerra contra las pandillas”, el presidente de El Salvador desafió la constitución y logró le reelección. A lo largo de la región, su modelo represivo inspira a ultraderechistas.

articles.title: La metamorfosis de las derechas paraguayas
articles.description: Tras más de medio siglo de gobiernos casi ininterrumpidos del Partido Colorado, los resultados de un candidato extremista muestran señales de que el poder de los partidos tradicionales se podría erosionar.

articles.title: Los neopatriotas latinoamericanos
articles.description: Unidas por enemigos difusos, las nuevas extremas derechas están altamente interconectados a ambos lados del Atlántico. Sin embargo, el campo de acción local y regional es donde ejercen sus batallas ideológicas.

articles.title: The Young Readers of the Argentine Right
articles.description: As the world of “politically incorrect” books moves from niche to mainstream, the radicalized right’s culture war attracts a new generation of followers.

articles.title: Jóvenes lectores de la derecha argentina
articles.description: Mientras el mundo del libro de los “políticamente incorrectos” pasa de nicho a la masificación, la batalla cultural de la derecha radicalizada atrae una nueva generación de seguidores.

articles.title: Dominican Republic’s Neofascist Paramilitaries Double Down on Right-Wing Repression
articles.description: New expressions of ultranationalist violence censoring Black women and migrants harken back to the Trujillo dictatorship. Anyone deemed a threat to Dominican values is a potential target.

articles.title: Narratives of Carceral Collective Resistance (Review)
articles.description: Kristina Shull’s book Detention Empire shines a light on the links between U.S. repressive counterinsurgency abroad and debilitating immigrant detention policies at home.

articles.title: Beyond Transportation, Panama’s Diablos Rojos Are Treasured Rolling Artwork
articles.description: Panama City’s iconic red devil buses are beloved by local communities, but face competing visions of modernity.

articles.title: 30.000 razones: Argentinos sostienen la memoria en las calles
articles.description: El 24 de marzo recuerda a las víctimas del terrorismo de estado en Argentina. Este año hacer memoria se convierte en una forma de resistir a las políticas negacionistas de extrema derecha del gobierno de Javier Milei.

articles.title: 30,000 Reasons: Argentines Uphold Memory in the Streets
articles.description: March 24 commemorates the victims of state terrorism in Argentina. As President Javier Milei defends perpetrators of genocide, remembering becomes a form of resisting far-right and denialist policies.

articles.title: U.S. Role in Honduras Narco-State Absent from President’s Drug Trial | Under the Shadow, Update 3
articles.description: Former president Juan Orlando Hernández has been convicted of drug trafficking. The United States and Canada remain unaccountable.

articles.title: U.S. Role in Honduras Narco-State Absent from President’s Drug Trial | Under the Shadow Update 3
articles.description: Former president Juan Orlando Hernández has been convicted of drug trafficking. The United States and Canada remain unaccountable.

articles.title: Israel y genocidio: No sólo en Gaza
articles.description: Israel y los Estados Unidos son responsables de perpetuar el Holocausto silencioso en Guatemala.

articles.title: Israel and Genocide: Not Only In Gaza
articles.description: Israel and the United States share responsibility in perpetuating Guatemala’s military-sponsored Silent Holocaust.

articles.title: 8M en Bolivia: “Nos dijeron que no andemos solas en la calle, así que salimos todas”
articles.description: Una mirada a las marchas por el Día Internacional de la Mujer en seis ciudades del país.

articles.title: 8M in Bolivia: “They Told Us Not to Walk Alone in the Streets, so We Marched All Together”
articles.description: A look at International Women’s Day marches in six cities across the country.

articles.title: Haiti: Trapped Between U.S. Guns, Death Squads,  and the Next Colonial Invasion
articles.description: Jake Johnston’s carefully investigated Aid State: Elite Panic, Disaster Capitalism and the Battle to Control Haiti sheds light on the geopolitical origins of the paramilitary death squads currently wreaking havoc on Port-au-Prince.

articles.title: Milei pone a prueba el poder del sistema político argentino
articles.description: El presidente Javier Milei fomenta conflictos y divisiones dentro de la oposición dialoguista en el Congreso argentino.

articles.title: Las nuevas derechas latinoamericanas: cambios ideológicos y circulación transnacional
articles.description: Las nuevas derechas representan una amanaza creciente a la democracia en todo América Latina y el Caribe. Conocer más en el número de primavera del NACLA Report on the Americas.

articles.title: Latin America’s New Right Wings: Shifting Ideologies, Transnational Ties
articles.description: New far-right forces pose a growing threat to democracy across Latin America and the Caribbean. Read more in the Spring 2024 issue of the NACLA Report on the Americas.

articles.title: “Our Struggle Is in Defense of Mother Earth and Self-Determination”
articles.description: Respect for Indigenous sovereignty and self-government are essential to a future where peoples are not forced to migrate. Will Guatemala’s new government work with communities to make this a reality?

articles.title: “Nuestra lucha es en defensa de la Madre Tierra y la libre determinación”
articles.description: El respeto a la soberanía y autogobierno de los pueblos originarios hace posible que algún día no sean forzados a migrar. ¿Trabajará la nueva administración junto a los pueblos para hacer esto una realidad?

articles.title: 2009 Honduras: Legacy of a Coup | Under the Shadow, Ep. 7, Part II
articles.description: The 2009 coup ratcheted up the sell-off of land and resources, enabled state-sponsored drug trafficking and corruption, and fueled a migrant exodus—all with U.S. and Canadian support.

articles.title: 2009 Honduras: Legacy of a Coup | Under the Shadow, Ep. 7, Part II
articles.description: The 2009 coup ratcheted up the sell-off of land and resources, enabled state-sponsored drug trafficking and corruption, and fueled a migrant exodusall with U.S. and Canadian support.

articles.title: El Salvador’s Social Media-Savvy President Receives Boost from Vote Abroad
articles.description: A controversial new system for Salvadorans voting abroad helped President Nayib Bukele secure his unconstitutional second term in office.

articles.title: Milei Tests the Power of Argentina’s Political System
articles.description: President Javier Milei stokes conflict and division within the pro-dialogue opposition of Argentina’s congress.

articles.title: El partido de Bukele impone su hegemonía El Salvador
articles.description: El gobernante consiguió ser reelegido inconstitucionalmente y obtener 54 de 60 escaños en el Legislativo. En el nuevo mapa electoral, el pluralismo político es el gran perdedor.

articles.title: Bukele’s Party Imposes Its Dominance in El Salvador
articles.description: The president achieved an unconstitutional reelection while his party obtained 54 of 60 seats in the legislature. In the new electoral map, political pluralism is the big loser.

articles.title: Is Hugo Chávez to Blame for Venezuela’s Collapse?
articles.description: The country’s current economic and democratic crisis should not be used to erase Chávez’s impressive accomplishments in working to build 21st-century socialism.

articles.title: 2009 Honduras: La Resistencia | Under the Shadow, Ep. 7, Part I
articles.description: The 2009 U.S.-backed coup ruptured Honduras’s three-decade-old democracy. Despite a media blockade, militarization, and deadly repression, the people took to the streets—and refused to back down.

articles.title: 2009 Honduras: La Resistencia | Under the Shadow, Ep. 7, Part I
articles.description: The 2009 U.S.-backed coup ruptured Honduras’s three-decade-old democracy. Despite a media blockade, militarization, and deadly repression, the people took to the streetsand refused to back down.

articles.title: A Strategic Cross-Border Labor Alliance
articles.description: A relationship between a U.S. and a Mexican union, forged in the face of NAFTA, has borne fruit over decades of struggle. Two leaders reflect on the importance of international solidarity.

articles.title: El trasfondo racista del movimiento ecologista boliviano
articles.description: Read this article in English. En Asunción de Quiquibey, una comunidad indígena mosetén del departamento de Beni, al noreste de Bolivia, los incendios forestales ardieron sin control durante meses el año […]

articles.title: Resistencia cultural frente a las políticas de Javier Milei
articles.description: El sector cultural se destaca en la resistencia ante las políticas de derecha en Argentina. Más allá de disciplinas, en dos años se conformó un movimiento unificado que se hace escuchar.

articles.title: Cultural Resistance in the Face of Javier Milei’s Policies
articles.description: The cultural sector stands out in the resistance to right-wing austerity policies in Argentina.

articles.title: The Racist Undertones of Bolivia’s Environmental Movement
articles.description: As massive wildfires swept across Bolivia in late 2023, a classist, racist, and capitalist public outcry deflected from the primary drivers of drought and deforestation.

articles.title: “I Would Rather Die than Live in this Institution”
articles.description: As Peru’s elderly population grows, long-term care facilities are failing older adults dependent on their services in Lima.

articles.title: The Settlers: A Necessary Anatomy of a Genocide
articles.description: Felipe Gálvez’s award-winning film Los Colonos delves into Chile’s brutal settler-colonial past, exposing the consequences of cultural extermination and resonating with Latin America’s contemporary Indigenous struggles.

articles.title: USS Honduras | Under the Shadow, Ep. 6
articles.description: Often overlooked in the story of U.S. imperialism in Central America, Honduras has served as a training base and staging ground for interventions throughout the region. In the 1980s, the impacts were devastating.

articles.title: “Petróleo para alguns poucos” nos portos de exportação do Brasil
articles.description: Para um pequeno agricultor no estado do Rio de Janeiro, um porto privado que serve a indústria de combustíveis fósseis trouxe uma luta de uma década para permanecer na terra.

articles.title: “Oil for the Few” at Brazil’s Export Ports
articles.description: For a small farmer in Rio de Janeiro state, a private port catering to the fossil fuel industry has brought a decade-long struggle to remain on the land.

articles.title: USS Honduras | Under the Shadow, Ep. 6
articles.description: Often overlooked in the story of U.S. imperialism in Central America, Honduras has served as a training base and staging ground for interventions throughout the region. In the 1980s, the impacts were devastating.

articles.title: Brazil at a Crossroads: The Environment or Oil and Gas
articles.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s administration brought high hopes of reversing devastating environmental destruction. Will a new fossil fuel boom undermine promises for change?

articles.title: Call for Pitches: Plurinationalism from Below / Convocatoria: Plurinacionalismo desde abajo
articles.description: CLOSED. NACLA is accepting proposals for an issue on exploring plurinationalism in Latin America. Send us your pitches by February 23, 2024.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Tense Peace Won’t Last
articles.description: President Daniel Noboa’s tough stance on crime managed to reduce rates of violence and instability. But militarization is not enough.

articles.title: Bukele, Presidente | Under the Shadow, Update 2
articles.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele has been reelected in a landslide. Supporters praise his security gains despite widespread human rights abuses, and leaders across the region are looking to emulate his model.

articles.title: Bukele, Presidente | Under the Shadow Update 2
articles.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele has been reelected in a landslide. Supporters praise his security gains despite widespread human rights abuses, and leaders across the region are looking to emulate his model.

articles.title: Informal Settlements on the Front Lines of Wildfire Risk in Bogotá
articles.description: Vulnerable dwellers were disproportionately affected by recent wildfires in Colombia’s capital. As climate change exacerbates El Niño’s impact, affected families regroup amid the threat of additional blazes.

articles.title: A New Life for Mexico’s Oldest Union
articles.description: “Our vision is defending the interests of workers and a democratic union life.” In conversation with a longtime labor journalist, a Mexican union leader puts current worker struggles in context.

articles.title: Freedom of Expression Under Attack in Bukele’s El Salvador
articles.description: President Nayib Bukele’s slide toward authoritarianism has culminated in an unconstitutional reelection bid. His consolidation of power has cracked down on independent press.

articles.title: Arévalo, Presidente | Under the Shadow, Update 1
articles.description: Guatemala’s new president Bernardo Arévalo is now in office. But the struggle to defend democracy against the forces fixated on blocking his rise to power isn’t over yet.

articles.title: Arévalo, Presidente | Under the Shadow, Update 1
articles.description: Guatemala’s new president Bernardo Arévalo is now in office. But the struggle to defend democracy against the forces fixated on blocking his rise to power isn’t over yet.

articles.title: The Queer Women Transforming Brazil’s Funk Music Scene
articles.description: Meet the artists pushing for a wave of social change through sapafunk, a subgenre that celebrates lesbian desire and sexuality.

articles.title: Art and Resistance in Chile 50 Years After the Coup
articles.description: Todavía somos el tiempo, an exhibition commissioned by the Chilean government and featuring material from NACLA’s archives, is now on view at the National Center of Contemporary Art in Santiago.

articles.title: “They Tried to Wipe Us Out. We Rise Up to Seek Justice.”
articles.description: As a new government takes office, Indigenous leaders are pragmatic about their hopes in Guatemala. In the struggle against a corrupt judicial system, the arc toward reconciliation is long.

articles.title: “Nos quisieron acabar. Nosotros nos levantamos para buscar justicia”
articles.description: Al tomar posesión un nuevo gobierno, líderes indígenas son pragmáticos sobre lo que esperan en Guatemala. En la lucha ante un sistema judicial corrupto, el camino hacia la reconciliación es largo.

articles.title: Argentina: Shock Therapy, Resistance, and the Role of the Left
articles.description: With Congress likely to approve Milei’s neoliberal agenda, the only limits to his onslaught will come through mass mobilization, coordinated strike activity, and other forms of social unrest.

articles.title: El Salvador: Rebel Radio | Under the Shadow, Ep. 5
articles.description: Broadcasting the Salvadoran government’s atrocities throughout the armed conflict, the guerrilla radio station Venceremos had a clear goal: bringing down the U.S.-backed dictatorship.

articles.title: “Lula III:” A Year of Mixed Results
articles.description: Brazil’s Workers’ Party has been back in power for a year. Despite some progress, concessions to the far right continue.

articles.title: El Salvador: Rebel Radio | Under the Shadow, Ep. 5
articles.description: Broadcasting the Salvadoran government’s atrocities throughout the armed conflict, the guerrilla radio station Venceremos had a clear goal: bringing down the U.S.-backed dictatorship.

articles.title: Las múltiples caras de la militarización en América Latina
articles.description: Protegiendo los proyectos extractivos en los territorios rurales y habilitando el control de los paramilitares en las zonas urbanas, el poder militar facilita la acumulación por desposesión.

articles.title: Las lealtades cambiantes del poder militar en Bolivia
articles.description: Las Fuerzas Armadas de Bolivia siempre adecuan a los caprichos del partido del turno. La ausencia de una reforma estructural de la institución militar es fundamentalmente contraria al proyecto plurinacional.

articles.description: Despite the Mexican American music trio’s success in the United States, the group faced backlash in Mexico for “not being Mexican enough.” What does this say about how Mexicanness is perceived within and outside the country?

articles.title: Crossing the Storm: EZLN Marks 30 Years with a 120-year Plan
articles.description: The Zapatista liberation movement gathered in Chiapas to honor three decades of struggle and prepare for an uncertain future.

articles.title: Venezuela’s War of All People
articles.description: Under the Bolivarian revolution, militarization has permeated all facets of politics and society, capturing institutional power as the ambit of the Armed Forces repeatedly expands.

articles.title: Indigenous Miskitu Migrants Are Fleeing for their Lives
articles.description: The Miskitu community along Nicaragua’s coast has long faced persecution and invisibilization. A recent increase in migration highlights the need for asylum protections for Miskitu youth.

articles.title: El Salvador: The Innocent Victims | Under the Shadow, Ep. 4
articles.description: In the 1980s, the Reagan administration poured billions of dollars into El Salvador’s military to crush the left-wing FMLN, littering the country in mass graves in the process.

articles.title: “Condemned to Sacrifice” in the Shadow of Argentina’s Vaca Muerta
articles.description: From Patagonia, an environmental activist discusses her community’s struggle against a new oil pipeline and the threats of expanding extractivism in their territory.

articles.title: “Condenados al sacrificio” en la sombra de Vaca Muerta
articles.description: Desde Patagonia, una activista ambiental habla de la lucha contra un nuevo oleoducto y la expansión del extractivismo en sus territorios.

articles.title: El Salvador: The Innocent Victims | Under the Shadow, Ep. 4
articles.description: In the 1980s, the Reagan administration poured billions of dollars into El Salvador’s military to crush the left-wing FMLN, littering the country in mass graves in the process.

articles.title: Argentina’s Vaca Muerta: 10 Years of Fracking and Local Resistance
articles.description: With another oil and gas bonanza brewing and a new far-right president in the Casa Rosada, people-led proposals for a just energy transition are more urgent than ever.

articles.title: “The People Are Willing to Continue”
articles.description: For leaders from the Xinka Parliament, one of the Indigenous authorities behind an ongoing national strike in Guatemala, the new government will provide a foundation to continue the struggle for democracy.

articles.title: “El pueblo está dispuesto a seguir”
articles.description: Para los líderes del Parlamento Xinka, una de las autoridades indígenas detrás de un paro nacional actualmente en desarrollo en Guatemala, el nuevo gobierno proporcionará una base para continuar la lucha por la democracia.

articles.title: Semillas de vida: Guatemala más allá de las elecciones
articles.description: Mientras el nuevo gobierno toma posesión a pesar de repetidos intentos de socavar su ascenso, líderes de los pueblos originarios reflexionan sobre el paro nacional indefinido en defensa de la democracia y las luchas que quedan por delante.

articles.title: Seeds of Life: Guatemala Beyond Elections
articles.description: As a new government takes office despite repeated attempts to undermine its ascent, Indigenous leaders reflect on the indefinite national strike in defense of democracy and the struggles that still lie ahead.

articles.title: New York City is Evicting Migrant Families: “This is a Humanitarian Crisis”
articles.description: As the city’s mayor implements a 60-day limit on shelter for asylum seeking families, a winter storm prompts the emergency evacuation of 2,000 migrants from a temporary shelter in South Brooklyn.

articles.title: “Without Transgender People, There is No Peace”
articles.description: In a historic move, Colombia’s Special Jurisdiction hears trans people’s life experiences during the armed conflict in hopes of bringing greater social and political inclusion.

articles.title: Peruvian Indigenous Communities Continue to Seek Justice a Year On
articles.description: A year after the Juliaca massacre killed 19 protesters, no official has been charged with the crimes. In Lima, corruption allegations have become a form of coercion to preserve the status quo.

articles.title: Guatemala: The Disappeared | Under the Shadow, Ep. 3
articles.description: U.S.-trained and sponsored state forces killed 200,000 mostly Indigenous Guatemalans in a genocide in the 1980s. Forty years later, justice remains elusive.

articles.title: Guatemala: United Fruit | Under the Shadow, Ep. 2
articles.description: The dreams of a democratic Guatemala were dashed by a 1954 CIA coup against President Jacobo Arbenz spurred by the landed interests of the United Fruit Company.

articles.title: The Beginning: Monroe Doctrine and Migration | Under the Shadow, Ep. 1
articles.description: Coups, destabilization, and decades of other forms of U.S. military and economic intervention have driven millions of Central Americans to flee their homes.

articles.title: Guatemala: The Disappeared | Under the Shadow, Ep. 3
articles.description: U.S.-trained and sponsored state forces killed 200,000 mostly Indigenous Guatemalans in a genocide in the 1980s. Forty years later, justice remains elusive.
