articles.description: Coups, destabilization, and decades of other forms of U.S. military and economic intervention have driven millions of Central Americans to flee their homes.

articles.title: Guatemala: United Fruit | Under the Shadow, Ep. 2
articles.description: The dreams of a democratic Guatemala were dashed by a 1954 CIA coup against President Jacobo Arbenz spurred by the landed interests of the United Fruit Company.

articles.title: Nearshoring and the Militarization of the U.S.-Mexico Border
articles.description: As corporate boosters push to shift supply chains from China to northern Mexico, military expansion in the borderlands secures manufacturing zones for transnational capital.

articles.title: The Myth of Demilitarization in Costa Rica
articles.description: Following the abolition of the Army, narratives celebrating Central America’s most peaceful nation have masked a militarized policing model shaped by U.S.-sponsored counterinsurgency.

articles.title: How the People of Vieques Evicted the U.S. Navy
articles.description: A Puerto Rican demilitarization activist reflects on the decades-long struggle to urge U.S. forces to withdraw from the island and the ongoing challenges Viequenses face today.

articles.title: NACLA Editors’ Picks: The Best Photos of 2023
articles.description: A collection of the best original photography NACLA published online in 2023, documenting struggles for memory, Indigenous rights, Palestinian solidarity, and defense of land and territory.

articles.title: Zapatistas at 30: Building and Inspiring Autonomy
articles.description: For decades, the EZLN has informed struggles down and up the continent. Amid climate chaos and endless war, they continue to imagine and create better worlds.

articles.title: EZLN a 30 años: constructor e inspirador de autonomías
articles.description: Durante décadas, los zapatistas han servido como luz orientadora para luchas a lo largo del continente. En medio del caos climático y la guerra interminable, siguen imaginando y construyendo mundos mejores.

articles.title: Chile Rejects Far-Right Constitution
articles.description: Once again, Chileans voted no on a constitutional referendum. The Pinochet-era constitution endures while the path to reform remains uncertain.

articles.title: COP28: Increase in Oil Production in Brazil May Nullify Gains from Zero Deforestation
articles.description: New calculation shows CO2 emissions from burning Equatorial Margin oil are 3 times Brazil’s goal for 2030.

articles.title: Puerto Rico’s New Leftist Alliance Poses a Threat to US Imperialism
articles.description: The Puerto Rican Independence Party’s (PIP) latest assembly marks a turning point in electoral strategy and a challenge to the colonial paradigms that have long held the archipelago hostage.

articles.title: We Don’t Choose to Be Land Defenders, We Are Born Land Defenders
articles.description: Kichwa activist Leo Cerda discusses the crucial role of national and global alliances in transitioning from extractive fossil fuels to greener alternatives.

articles.title: Editors’ Picks 2023: The Best of NACLA’s Online Coverage
articles.description: Spanning 12 countries and themes of anti-Blackness, Indigenous rights, defense of democracy, extractivism, the Latinx diaspora, and more, join us in taking a look back at our most memorable web stories of 2023.

articles.title: Call for Pitches / Convocatoria: ¿Dónde están?
articles.description: [CLOSED] NACLA is currently accepting proposals for an issue on disappearance in Latin America and the Caribbean. Send us your pitches by January 4, 2024.

articles.title: El partido MAS de Bolivia se encamina hacia una ruptura
articles.description: Las luchas internas en el Movimiento al Socialismo amenazan con dividir al partido, y los movimientos sociales históricos de Bolivia se ven obligados a elegir bando.

articles.title: Is NACLA Part of Your New Year’s Resolutions?
articles.description: We need your support. Help left journalism thrive in 2024 by donating or becoming a monthly sustainer today!

articles.title: Bolivia’s MAS Party Careens Toward a Split
articles.description: As infighting within the Movement Toward Socialism threatens to split the party, Bolivia’s historic social movements are forced to choose sides.

articles.title: The Falklands/Malvinas and Argentina’s Thatcherite Turn
articles.description: Argentina’s new president, Javier Milei, draws on authoritarian tactics reminiscent of Trump and Bolsonaro, but his ideology has deeper roots in Thatcherism, raising questions about the Falklands/Malvinas.

articles.title: Chainsaw Government: What to Expect from Argentina’s Javier Milei
articles.description: Supporters cheered as the new far-right libertarian president pledged to push austerity measures. But with progressive forces vowing to resist, his shock treatment could backfire.

articles.title: Panama Protests: A Model for Pushing Back Extractive Capitalism
articles.description: From dampening appetite for foreign investment to enlivening environmental struggles in neighboring countries, the recent victory of Panama’s historic anti-mining movement reverberates beyond borders.

articles.title: Women Organize Against the “Double Punishment” of Colombian Prisons
articles.description: Formerly incarcerated women lead the struggle to rethink Colombia’s broken prison system. What promise does a new restorative justice law hold?

articles.title: Hurricane Milei
articles.description: How can we understand the political shift in Argentina that led to an extreme right-wing outsider coming to power? Here are seven key points for unpacking the unprecedented election.

articles.title: Letter From a Mapuche Woman to Her Palestinian Sisters
articles.description: A Mapuche leader or weychafe expresses solidarity with Palestinian women in an open letter penned from the impotence of distance and the certainty of resistance.

articles.title: The Silences of Dispossession: Agrarian Change and Indigenous Politics in Argentina (Review)
articles.description: Mercedes Biocca’s insightful book reveals dynamics of resistance and acquiescence to soybean expansion in two Indigenous communities in the heart of Northern Argentina.

articles.title: Panama’s Massive Environmental Awakening
articles.description: The Canadian-owned mine at the center of a national uprising will be shut down. But differing environmentalisms in the isthmus may now be on a collision course against one another.

articles.title: “A Movement Is Built by Many”
articles.description: Over 100 activists and storytellers from across the Americas gathered in the heart of the Ecuadorian Amazon to forge an alliance against extractivism and illegal mining.

articles.title: The Banality of Henry Kissinger
articles.description: Henry Kissinger helped orchestrate the demise of Chilean democracy in 1970. His legacy reflects a ruthless prioritization of U.S. hegemony over democratic principles.

articles.title: Militarism Matters
articles.description: Understanding structures of domination helps us better support efforts to transform them. Read more in “Militarized Democracy Refracted,” the Winter 2023 issue of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: The Instruction: How the López Obrador Administration Blew Up the Ayotzinapa Investigation (Part I)
articles.description: Mexico’s president promised to deliver truth and justice for the 43 disappeared students and their families. The former special prosecutor reveals what happened instead.

articles.title: Latin Grammys Debuts in Spain Against a Backdrop of Latinophobia
articles.description: The city of Sevilla gave the award show 19 million to host the event and now expects a $530 million profit.

articles.title: Politización feminista, freno de emergencia en Argentina
articles.description: El candidato ultraderechista Javier Milei se enfrentará a Sergio Massa en una segunda vuelta electoral en Argentina el 19 de noviembre. Un análisis feminista pone de relieve lo que está en juego en la votación.

articles.title: Feminist Politicization Slows Far Right Momentum in Argentina
articles.description: Ultra-right politician Javier Milei will face Sergio Massa in a run-off election in Argentina on November 19 amid currency devaluation and growing indebtedness. A feminist analysis brings the stakes of the vote into focus.

articles.title: Argentina y Ecuador: adversarios similares, diferentes resultados
articles.description: Los resultados electorales en Ecuador y Argentina generan esperanza e incertidumbre desde el ciclo progresista que se ha instaurado los últimos años en América Latina.

articles.title: Argentina and Ecuador: A Journey to the Center
articles.description: Viewed in light of Latin America’s recent progressive wave, diverging electoral results in Ecuador and Argentina generate hope and uncertainty.

articles.title: Bukele’s War Against the Past
articles.description: President Nayib Bukele leverages an antagonistic stance against El Salvador’s past to consolidate his political power.

articles.title: Free Trade Firepower: The Growing Hemispheric Gun Trade
articles.description: Legal U.S. firearm sales to Latin American countries are on the rise, arming violent actors in Mexico, Guatemala, and elsewhere. U.S. gun export policies need more than tweaking.

articles.title: After Servitude: Elusive Property and the Ethics of Kinship in Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: Mareike Winchell’s ethnography of post-hacienda life in Bolivia’s Ayopaya province reveals the complex afterlives of servitude, but fails to weigh the comparative scale between deference and refusal.

articles.title: Gustavo Petro Holds Firm on Palestine
articles.description: Despite Colombia’s deep historical ties to both Israel and the United States, Petro has been one of the most outspoken leaders in support of Palestine, calling for peace both at home and abroad.

articles.title: Argentina Stalls the Extreme Right (For Now)
articles.description: Peronism made a comeback in Argentina’s first-round presidential election. Libertarian Javier Milei failed to secure an outright win and now faces Sergio Massa in the runoff.

articles.title: República Dominicana y Haití en la encrucijada del Río Masacre
articles.description: El intento del gobierno dominicano de reforzar su hegemonía sobre el río fronterizo desató una inesperada resistencia en Haití.

articles.title: Dominican Republic and Haiti at the Crossroads of the Massacre River
articles.description: The Dominican government’s attempt to reinforce its hegemony over the border river unleashed unexpected resistance in Haiti.

articles.title: María Corina Machado’s Triumph: An Opposition Shipwreck?
articles.description: An advocate of oil privatization who previously called for foreign intervention won Venezuela’s opposition primaries in a landslide. Many questions remain on the path to the 2024 elections.

articles.title: El triunfo de Machado: ¿Naufragio de los partidos políticos?
articles.description: Arrasó las elecciones primarias en Venezuela una promotora de la privatización del petroleo quien ha apoyado la intervención extranjera. Cara a las elecciones de 2024, muchas preguntas quedan pendientes.

articles.title: “Somos Sur”: Mapuche and Palestinian Chileans Stand in Solidarity with Gaza
articles.description: Latin American leaders are split on Israel’s bombardment of Gaza. But in Chile, the Palestinian diaspora, Indigenous communities, and others have stood with the people of Gaza.

articles.title: Textures of Terror (Review)
articles.description: Victoria Sanford’s book is a powerful testimony to the historical roots of routine violence against women in Guatemala, portraying the life, struggles, and personality of human beings who are otherwise lost in dire statistics.

articles.title: Forward Ever: 40 Years on from the End of the Revolution and the U.S. Invasion of Grenada
articles.description: In the throes of the Cold War, a tiny Caribbean island dared to wage a revolutionary experiment. As the Revo imploded, the United States invaded.

articles.title: The Paraguay-Paraná Waterway Controversy and the Costs of Unilateralism
articles.description: Argentina and Paraguay face off in a diplomatic row over river tolls that raises questions about the future of regional relations.

articles.title: The Victory of Businessman Daniel Noboa in Ecuador
articles.description: After a meteoric rise amid an exceptionally violent campaign, a young political outsider beat Correismo once again, ushering in an uncertain interim government.

articles.title: La victoria del empresario Daniel Noboa Azín en Ecuador
articles.description: Tras un ascenso meteórico en medio de una campaña excepcionalmente violenta, un joven desconocido volvió a derrotar al correismo, dando paso a un incierto gobierno provisional.

articles.title: Who Makes Class in Class War? (Review)
articles.description: Mark Steven’s book Class War: A Literary History expands our understanding of class war but completely misses the role of ideology and organizing in class politicization.

articles.title: Remembering Longtime Editor Fred Rosen
articles.description: Naclistas share memories of working and walking alongside Fred Rosen, who dedicated decades to NACLA. Fred died at age 81 in Cuernavaca, Mexico, on September 12, 2023.

articles.title: In Brazil, Agribusiness Lobby and Far-Right Politicians Sow Climate Disinformation
articles.description: Rural associations, media outlets, and digital channels provide a platform for scientists who espouse climate misinformation.

articles.title: Defensores y defensoras del agua ganan una victoria clave contra la minería
articles.description: Una decisión judicial histórica ha suspendido el proyecto de extracción de oro de Loma Larga en Ecuador, pero la lucha continúa.

articles.title: Landmark Settlement Holds Bolivian Ex-President Accountable for Human Rights Abuses
articles.description: After a 16-year legal battle, former head of state Gonzalo “Goni” Sánchez de Lozada must compensate the families of victims extrajudicially killed in the 2003 Gas War.

articles.title: A Tribute to Fred Rosen, Committed Naclista and Anti-War Activist
articles.description: Before coming to NACLA in 1992, longtime NACLA editor Fred Rosen was a war resister who found his way to left journalism. Fred passed away on September 12, 2023 at age 81.

articles.title: Policing and Disinformation Gone Awry in Ecuador
articles.description: Protection rackets and political influencers feed an insecurity crisis and promote racist narratives ahead of the country’s October 15 presidential runoff.

articles.title: Fred Rosen, Presente!
articles.description: Remembering longtime NACLA editor Fred Rosen, whose commitments to left journalism, social justice movements, and radical economic analysis helped make NACLA what it is today.

articles.title: Water Defenders in Ecuador Win Key Anti-Mining Victory
articles.description: A historic court decision suspended the long-disputed Loma Large gold mining project in Ecuador’s paramo, but the struggle continues.

articles.title: Apartheid in the Caribbean
articles.description: In the Dominican Republic, policies toward Haitians and Dominicans of Haitian descent bear ominous parallels with Israel’s racist repression of the Palestinian people.

articles.title: Remembering Grenada’s Revolution Beyond Cold War Narratives
articles.description: Forty years after the U.S. invasion, centering Caribbean perspectives on the rise and demise of a revolutionary movement holds the possibility of stepping out from empire’s shadow and imagining alternative futures.

articles.title: The Predatory Perils of Cannabis Legalization in Jamaica
articles.description: Medicinal marijuana markets present a major economic opportunity for Jamaica. Without steps to combat inequities, traditional ganja growers will be left behind.

articles.title: Haiti as Empire’s Laboratory
articles.description: As the United States and its allies push renewed foreign intervention, the uses and abuses of the first Black republic as a testing ground of imperialism offer stark warnings. Haiti still struggles to be free.

articles.title: FEMA’s Quickly Depleting Funds Underline Why Puerto Rico Needs Energy Justice Now
articles.description: As the archipelago marks the anniversary of Hurricanes María and Fiona, the impacts of agency capture, colonialism, and climate chaos highlight the need for community-based rooftop solar.

articles.title: Chilean Campesinos Pursue Justice as Far Right Rises
articles.description: Linked to disappearances in Paine, José Antonio Kast, a rising star in Chile’s far-right Republicano Party, gains national prominence with Pinochet-era nostalgia.

articles.title: Estadio Azteca and the 2026 World Cup in Mexico
articles.description: Activists and residents surrounding the Azteca Stadium managed to suspend a proposed expansion, but concerns about mega-projects, gentrification, and defense of territory in Mexico City remain.

articles.title: Chileans March for Memory as Wounds Remain Open 50 Years After the Coup
articles.description: Scenes from Santiago capture the ongoing struggle for truth and justice, half a century after the beginning of a reign of state terror under Pinochet’s dictatorship.

articles.title: Remembering Chile’s U.S.-backed Coup, 50 Years On
articles.description: Fifty years on, overcoming the legacy of Pinochet and the coup remains extremely difficult. Explore NACLA’s coverage.

articles.title: “It’s Not 30 Pesos, It’s 30 Years”
articles.description: Fifty years on from the coup that installed dictatorship and neoliberalism in Chile, protest art from the streets of Santiago underlines the present reverberations of an authoritarian past.

articles.title: “A Heavy Past”: Filmmaker Patricio Guzmán Reflects on Chile’s 1973 Coup
articles.description: Fifty years after documenting the Allende government and the events of 1973, Guzmán says that if he were to make a film about Chile today “it would be a film of questions.”

articles.title: “Un pasado pesado latente”: El cineasta Patricio Guzmán, a 50 años del golpe de Estado en Chile
articles.description: El cineasta que documentó el gobierno de Allende y los hechos del año 1973 reflexiona que si hiciera una película sobre Chile hoy, “sería una película de interrogaciones.”

articles.title: Remembering the Women Victims of the Pinochet Dictatorship in Chile
articles.description: As emboldened far-right denialists dismiss the horrors of state terrorism, seeking truth and justice for systematic sexual political violence remains urgent, 50 years after the 1973 coup.

articles.title: Chile’s Coup d’État as a Refoundational Project
articles.description: Days after the bombing of La Moneda on September 11, 1973, the military junta set a priority that would define Chile’s trajectory for decades: drafting a new constitution.

articles.title: Chile Still Wrestling With Demons 50 Years After the Coup
articles.description: Amid right-wing backlash and setbacks in the historic effort to rewrite the dictatorship-era constitution, Pinochet’s shadow still hangs heavy.

articles.title: Petro’s Government One Year In
articles.description: The election of Colombia’s first leftist president marked a fundamental shift in the country’s political landscape. Has Petro’s administration fulfilled its pledges one year into office?

articles.title: Anti-Blackness Knows No Borders and Neither Should Haitian Migrant Rights Advocacy
articles.description: The recently launched Hemispheric Network for Haitian Migrants’ Rights connects Haitian leaders across borders to advocate for the rights of Haitian migrants and confront discriminatory policies.

articles.title: Crossing Caribbean Waters in the Shadow of Empire
articles.description: Unauthorized migration across Hispanophone Caribbean, rendered through art, highlights the neocolonial and neoliberalism violence shaping mobility, displacement, and borders.

articles.title: Set Fear on Fire: Notes on Translation
articles.description: Chilean feminist collective LASTESIS, creators of the viral interactive performance “A Rapist in Your Path,” presented the English translation of their book in New York. Translator Camila Valle reflects on the feminist and political implications of South-North translation.

articles.title: Quemar el Miedo: Notas sobre la traducción
articles.description: En marzo, colectivo feminista chileno LASTESIS presentó la traducción al inglés de su libro en Nueva York. Traductora Camila Valle analiza implicaciones feministas de traducción Sur-Norte.

articles.title: The Afterlives of Empire in the Caribbean
articles.description: In the long shadow of empire and imperialism, the region continues to reshape and rebuild itself. Read more in the Fall 2023 issue of the NACLA Report on the Americas.

articles.title: Guatemala celebra el regreso de la “Primavera Democrática” con amenazas de violencia
articles.description: Bernardo Arévalo ganó la segunda vuelta en Guatemala de manera aplastante pero enfrenta una transición complicada debido a informes de planes de asesinato y maniobras judiciales en curso.

articles.title: Guatemalans Celebrate Return of “Democratic Spring” as Potential Violence Threatens Transition
articles.description: Bernardo Arévalo won Guatemala’s runoff in a landslide but faces a troubled transition amid reported assassination plots and ongoing judicial maneuvers.

articles.title: Ecuador Votes to Keep Yasuní Oil in the Ground in Historic Referendum
articles.description: Ecuadorians have chosen to safeguard the biodiverse Yasuní National Park from oil drilling, marking a major triumph for grassroots Indigenous and environmental activists against the fossil fuel consensus.

articles.title: Ecuador Heads to Presidential Runoff with Opposing Visions on the Ballot
articles.description: Ecuadorian voters will now choose between two very distinct paths forward: a revival of social programs or more neoliberal economic policy.

articles.title: Judicialización ensombrece las elecciones guatemaltecas que rompen paradigmas
articles.description: A pesar de intervención y persecución legal, encuestas favorecen al candidato reformista sobre la opción del establecimiento para la segunda vuelta. Aún se vislumbran esfuerzos judiciales para descarrilar la democracia.

articles.title: The Strikers of Coachella: A Rank-and-File History of the UFW Movement (Review)
articles.description: Paiz’s book provides a balanced history of the United Farm Workers movement, without losing sight of the myriad ways it brought people together to demand, conceive, and build better worlds.

articles.title: Lawfare Casts Shadow Over Paradigm-Shifting Guatemalan Election
articles.description: Despite court intervention and legal persecution, a reformist candidate is favored to win the runoff over an establishment option. Judicial efforts to derail democracy still loom.

articles.title: Ecuador on the Brink
articles.description: The assassination of a presidential candidate days before the country’s snap elections shocks Ecuador. How do we make sense of the violence?

articles.title: The Hard Right Captivates the Argentine Electorate
articles.description: The Argentine primary elections caused a political earthquake, with libertarian Javier Milei taking first place and Peronism coming in third. Never before has the radicalized right won so many votes.

articles.title: In El Salvador, Workers Fight to Protect Public Services
articles.description: President Nayib Bukele is dismantling vital public services that millions of Salvadorans rely on, and cracking down on the unions that defend them.

articles.title: Jujuy Stands Up Against Multinational Mining Companies and Anti-Democratic Reforms
articles.description: Protests in northern Argentina continue against controversial constitutional reforms that criminalize protest and clear the way for lithium extraction at the expense of Indigenous rights.

articles.title: Walking Alongside an Andean Radical in Times of Revolt in Peru
articles.description: The life of the renowned campesino activist Hugo Blanco helps us see through the limitations of state reform and better hear the often-silenced voices of resisting communities.

articles.title: Atropellados por la transformación: El Tren Maya en México
articles.description: A medida que las expropiaciones para el controvertido megaproyecto se aceleran, las implicaciones geopolíticas y de seguridad salen a la luz.

articles.title: En Defensa de Las Costas y El Patrimonio Indígena de Puerto Rico
articles.description: La socióloga y activista ambiental Alegna Malavé Marrero habla sobre la lucha contra la privatización y corrupción que amenaza los recursos naturales y la identidad cultural de su país.

articles.title: Will Correísmo Succeed in Surfing the Second Progressive Wave?
articles.description: With two weeks to go before Ecuador’s presidential elections, what obstacles does Correísmo face in defeating its right-wing opponents? Will Luisa González become Ecuador’s first elected woman president?

articles.title: ¿El Correísmo logrará surfear la segunda ola progresista?
articles.description: Faltando dos semanas para las elecciones presidenciales en Ecuador, ¿cuáles son los obstáculos que padece el correísmo? ¿Conseguirá la correísta Luisa González la victoria para convertirse en la primera presidenta electa?

articles.title: Global Race for Lithium Lands in Rural Brazil
articles.description: International mining companies arrive in Jequitinhonha Valley, promising development and provoking concerns about local compensation and water scarcity.

articles.title: And Yet He Loves Us
articles.description: “It doesn’t matter how much silicone you have in your breasts. God loves you too.” In deeply religious Brazil, evangelical churches are booming. But in the shadow of this conservative revolution, freedom is also thriving at Brazil’s first church for trans people.

articles.title: Trampled by Transformation: Mexico’s Tren Maya
articles.description: As expropriations for the controversial megaproject accelerate, impacted communities assess the security and geopolitical implications.

articles.title: Política de drogas y retos de paz en Colombia
articles.description: Lxs cultivadores de coca, marihuana y amapola del Departamento del Cauca exigen participar en la definición de una nueva política progresiva de drogas.

articles.title: Drug Policy and Pathways to Peace in Colombia
articles.description: Coca, marijuana, and poppy growers in the Cauca Department demand to participate in the elaboration of progressive new drug policy.

articles.title: Disrupting the Patrón: Indigenous Land Rights and the Fight for Environmental Justice in Paraguay’s Chaco (Review)
articles.description: Joel Correia’s ethnography provides vibrant testimony of the struggles of the Sanapaná and Enxet peoples as they navigate complex dynamics of dispossession and neglect.

articles.title: Protecting Puerto Rico’s Coastlines & Indigenous Heritage
articles.description: Sociologist and environmental activist Alegna Malavé Marrero talks about the battle against privatization and corruption threatening natural resources and the nation’s cultural identity.

articles.title: Una hoja de ruta hacia la protección forestal en el posconflicto colombiano
articles.description: Retos de gobernabilidad complejos se convergen en la Amazonia colombiana. Defender los ecosistemas y los pueblos que los habiten tiene que ser parte del proceso de paz con grupos armados.

articles.title: Salvar la Amazonia es salvar la democracia en Brasil
articles.description: Durante el gobierno de Bolsonaro, los criminenes ambientales y el autoritarianismo se incrementaron. Hoy, defender la la floresta también implica rescatar el estado de derecho brasileno.

articles.title: Saving the Amazon to Save Democracy
articles.description: Under Bolsonaro, environmental crimes skyrocketed amid a slide toward authoritarianism. Now, defending the rainforest also means rescuing Brazil’s democratic rule of law.

articles.title: Amazonia, imaginarios y utopías
articles.description: Sin saberlo, la globalización del esfuerzo para salvar la Amazonia ha marginado otros ecosistemas. Es necesario repensar la relación entre la sociedad y la naturaleza.

articles.title: Defensores indígenas da floresta na mira
articles.description: Em tempos de turbulência política, as elites recorrem à violência. Quando o poder de Bolsonaro caiu, ataques anti-indígenas na Amazônia explodiram, alimentados por narrativas de ódio.

articles.title: Indigenous Defenders in the Crosshairs
articles.description: In times of political turbulence, elites often turn to violence. As Bolsonaro’s grip on power faltered, anti-Indigenous attacks in the Amazon exploded, fueled by narratives of hate.

articles.title: “Queremos continuar vivendo em nossa floresta como ela é”
articles.description: “Antigamente, no lugar onde viviam nossos antepassados, a floresta era assim, estava tudo bem e nós, Yanomami dessa geração, queremos continuar existindo assim”.

articles.title: Pathways from Deforestation to Restoration
articles.description: The science is clear: rehabilitating the Amazon rainforest is essential to mitigating climate change and reversing biodiversity loss. Indigenous knowledge must play a central role.

articles.title: Desafios e soluções para enfrentar a crise climática e a perda de biodiversidade na Amazônia
articles.description: Reabilitar a floresta amazônica é essencial para mitigar as mudanças climáticas e reverter a perda de biodiversidade. Os conhecimentos indígenas são fundamentais.

articles.title: “La selva es un ser viviente consciente”
articles.description: Mientras el capitalismo verde busca aprovechar de soluciones falsas, una comunidad indígena de Ecuador resiste con una propuesta innovadora para proteger la Amazonia.

articles.title: We Must Remain Vigilant
articles.description: Lula’s government is pushing back after encroachment on Indigenous territories intensified under Bolsonaro. For a Munduruku leader, grassroots territorial struggle remains critical.

articles.title: “A gente tem que ser vigilante”
articles.description: O governo de Lula está tomando medidas após a intensificação da invasão de territórios indígenas sob o governo de Bolsonaro. Para uma liderança munduruku, a luta territorial de base é fundamental.

articles.title: The Amazon’s Forgotten Cities
articles.description: Despite the enduring image of the Amazon as a vast jungle frontier, urbanized spacesand the millions of people that call them homeare central to the past and future of the rainforest.

articles.title: Verónica Gago’s Territories of Revolt
articles.description: A conversation with the Argentine feminist scholar and activist in New York: Latin America’s feminisms “have fundamentally altered what we understand about politics.”

articles.title: Los territorios de revuelta de Verónica Gago
articles.description: Una conversación con la académica y activista feminista argentina en Nueva York: Los feminismos latinoamericanos “han alterado fundamentalmente lo que entendemos por política”.

articles.title: 2023 Mid-Year Impact Report
articles.description: With support from our readers in 2022, here’s what we’ve been up to at NACLA so far this year.

articles.title: Puerto Rican Women Push the Boundaries of Latinx Art and Culture in New York City
articles.description: Four prominent Latinx art institutions celebrate important anniversaries under the visionary leadership of Puerto Rican Women.

articles.title: Guns, Gangs, and Neocolonialism in Haiti
articles.description: Hundreds of thousands of guns made in the United States propel a gang war that has turned Haiti’s capital Port-au-Prince into a war zone.

articles.title: Ybor City: Crucible of the Latina South (Review)
articles.description: Sarah McNamara’s book traces the politics of Cuban immigrants and their descendants, the central role of women, and histories of labor organizing in a Tampa area cigar making community.

articles.title: Colombian Indigenous Communities Fight to Reclaim Land from Irish Packaging Giant
articles.description: The Irish-based multinational Smurfit Kappa’s vast tracts of monoculture forestry in Colombia tie Ireland into global webs of extraction and exploitation.

articles.title: In Uruguay, Struggle for Memory and Accountability Continues, 50 Years On
articles.description: On June 27, 1973, a coup plunged Uruguay into dictatorship. Decades later, human rights movements continue to demand justice for the crimes committed under the reign of state terror.

articles.title: Of Light and Struggle: Social Justice, Human Rights, and Accountability in Uruguay (Review)
articles.description: Debbie Sharnak’s book traces the shifting meanings of human rights in Uruguay’s descent into authoritarianism and continued struggle for justice and accountability.

articles.title: Las sombras del pasado se ciernen sobre las elecciones de 2023 en Guatemala
articles.description: Las elecciones del 25 de junio en Guatemala están plagadas de retrocesos antidemocráticos y dominadas por élites tradicionales, lo que genera dudas sobre su legitimidad.

articles.title: Hidrocracia en el centro de las elecciones de la Ciudad de Guatemala
articles.description: Una candidata a alcaldesa distinta desafía la privatización de la infraestructura urbana del agua e intenta unir a una izquierda históricamente divida.

articles.title: “For Us, Extractivism is Lethal”
articles.description: Activists and experts reflect on how extractive economies perpetuate colonial domination in Ecuador and Bolivia today.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Divided Electoral Landscape
articles.description: Ecuador’s failed rightwing government provides a narrow opportunity for the left and environmental movements to regroup ahead of snap elections in August.

articles.title: Thelma Cabrera: “We Are Fighting for a Plurinational State and Well-being of the Peoples”
articles.description: In Guatemala, an Indigenous candidate was barred from running for president. Her movement’s struggle, however, goes beyond elections.

articles.title: Thelma Cabrera: “Estamos luchando por un Estado plurinacional para el bien vivir de los pueblos”
articles.description: En Guatemala, se ha impedido la candidatura presidencial de una líder indígena. Pero la lucha de su movimiento va más allá de las elecciones.

articles.title: Aspirante presidencial Tamara Adrián: por una Venezuela donde caben todos
articles.description: En una Venezuela con los ojos puestos en las elecciones presidenciales de 2024, la abogada y activista trans propone un futuro inclusivo como candidata a las primarias de la oposición en octubre.

articles.title: Trans Presidential Hopeful Envisions a Venezuela Where Everyone Fits
articles.description: Ahead of Venezuela’s 2024 presidential elections, lawyer and trans activist Tamara Adrián has put her hat in the ring as a candidate in this year’s opposition primaries.

articles.title: Hydrocracy at the Center of Guatemala City’s Elections
articles.description: An unlikely candidate for mayor challenges the privatization of urban water infrastructure and seeks to unite a historically divided left.

articles.title: La democracia guatemalteca hecha jirones
articles.description: Un defensor de los derechos humanos indígenas que ganó apoyo sin precedentes en las últimas elecciones con propuestas para sacudir el statu quo está ahora fuera de la boleta.

articles.title: The Shadows of the Past Hang over Guatemala’s 2023 Elections
articles.description: Guatemala’s June 25 elections are troubled by anti-democratic backsliding and dominated by traditional elites, raising questions about their legitimacy.

articles.title: Slave Labor Accusations Come Back to Haunt Volkswagen in Brazil
articles.description: Prosecutors in Brazil are seeking reparations for decades-old human rights violations allegedly committed by Volkswagen in the Amazon during the military dictatorship.

articles.title: En medio de la inflación, trabajadores costarricenses enfrentan jornadas más largas y recortes de horas extraordinarias
articles.description: Tras años de afianzamiento neoliberal, una propuesta de ley está a punto de erosionar derechos laborales de larga data en el sector privado, precarizando a la clase trabajadora.

articles.title: Retrospective (Review)
articles.description: Juan Gabriel Vásquez’s latest novel-biography tells the extraordinary life of the Colombian guerrillero-turned-filmmaker Sergio Cabrera.

articles.title: Despite Conservative Victory, Paraguay’s Presidential Elections Signal Growing Discontent
articles.description: The status quo party is headed back to the top office, but exhaustion of the country’s development model spells shifting geopolitical relations and an uncertain political future.

articles.title: Lula Won, but Bolsonarismo Still Looms Large
articles.description: Bolsonaro’s return to Brazil generated less-than-expected fanfare, but the far-right ideology he represents remains very much alive.

articles.title: Gangsters of Capitalism (Review)
articles.description: Jonathan Katz’s book about the career of a decorated Marine turned critic attests to the symbiotic relationship between militarism and U.S. commercial expansion.

articles.title: Amid Inflation, Costa Rican Workers Face Longer Workdays and Cuts to Overtime
articles.description: After years of neoliberal entrenchment, a proposed law is poised to erode longstanding labor rights in the private sector, making the working-class more precarious.

articles.title: La Amazonia al borde de un precipicio
articles.description: A través de perspectivas de líderes sociales, investigaciones y análsis, el nuevo número del NACLA Report aborda lo que está en juego en la Amazonia y lo que se necesita para evitar una crisis más profunda.

articles.title: Amazonia on the Brink
articles.description: Our Summer 2023 issue of the NACLA Report brings movement voices together with research and analysis to lay out what’s at stake in the Amazon and how to avert a deeper crisis.

articles.title: Far Right Holds Chile Hostage
articles.description: The ultra-conservative Republican Party won a majority on Chile’s new Constitutional Council, delivering a major blow to President Gabriel Boric’s transformative platform.

articles.title: Elecciones anticipadas en Ecuador después de que el Presidente disuelva la legislatura
articles.description: Para evitar una votación de destitución, Guillermo Lasso eligió la opción nuclear. En medio de la polarización y de un prolongado estancamiento político, el futuro es incierto.

articles.title: Communal Resistance and Land Theft Mark Lead up to Guatemala Elections
articles.description: Mass protests against taxation measures and the forced displacement of Indigenous communities set the backdrop for Guatemala’s upcoming presidential elections.

articles.title: “Rompamos El Silencio”
articles.description: El gobierno Salvadoreño recientemente se desligó del programa de inmersión lingüística Cuna Nahuat, continuando así la larga historia de supresión y invisibilización de los pueblos originarios de El Salvador.

articles.title: “Rompamos El Silencio”
articles.description: The recent dismantling of the Cuna Nahuat Indigenous language program in El Salvador is the latest in a long history of erasure for Salvadoran Indigenous communities.

articles.title: The Pursuit of Memory and Justice in Bojayá
articles.description: Twenty-one years after the Bojayá Massacre destroyed their town, survivors in the community of Bellavista Nueva in northwestern Colombia recount their story on their own terms.

articles.title: Argentina 1985: The Revival of Democracy (Review)
articles.description: Santiago Mitre’s feature film about holding the perpetrators of dictatorship accountable in Argentina humanizes a pivotal moment in Latin Americanand worldhistory.

articles.title: Transgressive Notes from Ecuador’s Prisons
articles.description: President Guillermo Lasso’s Commission for Penitentiary Dialogue and Pacification was a failure. Now, a new Prison Observatory seeks to generate broad-based solutions to Ecuador’s prison crisis.

articles.title: El Salvador: Surfing in the “New Land of the Free?”
articles.description: The expansion of the Surf City tourism project towards the eastern and unexploited part of the country raises concerns over democracy, sustainability, and land ownership.

articles.title: Rural Communities and the Making of Modern Peru (Review)
articles.description: In his new book, historian Javier Puente chronicles how rural communities in the Andean highlands played a key role in the making of state power in twentieth-century Peru.

articles.title: Movimiento indígena en Colombia: entre altas expectativas y firmes demandas frente al actual gobierno
articles.description: Para la/os líderes/as de la principal organización indígena de Colombia, la elección de Gustavo Petro y Francia Márquez ha marcado una gran victoria. Pero, la movilización continúa.

articles.title: La lucha y La Memoria: Uplifting Feminist Movements Through a Decolonial Lens (Review)
articles.description: In this collection of testimony, poetry, short stories, and analysis, Marisa Ruiz Trejo examines Chiapas, Central America, and the Caribbean through a critical feminist perspective.

articles.title: The Causes Behind the Ciudad Juárez Migrant Detention Center Fire
articles.description: The fire that killed 40 people on March 27 is the foreseeable consequence of binational immigration enforcement measures by the United States and Mexico.

articles.title: Licencias Hoy, Papeles Mañana: Driver’s Licenses and Civil Rights in Indiana
articles.description: Organizers from the immigrant rights group Cosecha Indiana push to make Indiana the 19th state to expand access to driver’s licenses to undocumented residents.

articles.title: The INE Debate and the Formation of the Neoliberal Democracy in Mexico
articles.description: Although Mexico’s electoral institute was originally born out of struggles for democracy, it has since become a guardian of the neoliberal Mexican state.

articles.title: Promesas y prohibicionismo en la nueva política de drogas de Colombia
articles.description: Gustavo Petro ha denunciado que la guerra contra las drogas liderada por los EE.UU. es un fracaso total. Queda pendiente el pasar de palabras a acciones.

articles.title: Indigenous Activists’ Cautious Optimism and Unwavering Demands for Colombia
articles.description: For leaders of Cauca’s Indigenous movement, the election of Gustavo Petro and Francia Márquez is a major victory. But mobilization must continue.

articles.title: The Reinvention of the Latin American Right
articles.description: Across the hemisphere and beyond, right-wing forces are leveraging the power of internationalism to galvanize hardline “resistance” against a new wave of leftist governments.

articles.title: Anarchists vs. the State
articles.description: As Latin America swings left, activists keep alive a long anarchist tradition of critiquing the limits of state power. For them, the real alternatives are in communities, workplaces, and the streets.

articles.title: How Mexican Feminists Became Enemies of the State
articles.description: Despite intense repression and transphobic division, a powerful feminist movement is shifting the terms of debate on gender violence.

articles.title: Cómo las feministas mexicanas se volvieron enemigas del estado
articles.description: A pesar de la intensa represión y la división transfóbica, un poderoso movimiento feminista está cambiando el debate sobre la violencia de género.

articles.title: Peru’s Media Faces a Crisis Within a Crisis
articles.description: With Peru’s mainstream media concentrated in a few hands, citizens turn to the internet to challenge hegemonic narratives. The results are not always utopian.

articles.title: The Drive Behind Tesla’s New Manufacturing Plant in Mexico
articles.description: Tesla’s plan to open a Gigafactory in Monterrey is welcomed by local business elites, but will only deepen processes of labor devaluation and technological dependency.

articles.title: Call for Pitches: Militarism in the Americas / Convocatoria: El militarismo en las Américas
articles.description: NACLA is currently accepting proposals for an issue on militarism in the Americas. Send us your pitches by April 21, 2023.

articles.title: La izquierda latinoamericano se realinea en nuevo terreno político
articles.description: En el mundo multipolar, los gobiernos progresistas de hoy enfrentan desafíos y oportunidades muy distintos al primer ciclo. ¿Cómo afrontarán los retos?

articles.title: The Latin American Left Turns Its Back on Dictatorship
articles.description: The Latin American Left has largely distanced itself from Nicaragua’s Ortega. Still, understanding the shift from revolution to authoritarianism remains complex.

articles.title: Pink Tide 2.0? Latin America’s New Wave of Leftist Governments
articles.description: Latin America’s resurgent Left faces steep challenges. What are the lessons from the first Pink Tide and the prospects for transformative change?

articles.title: Pink Tide 2.0? Latin America’s New Wave of Leftist Governments
articles.description: Latin America’s resurgent Left faces steep challenges. What are the lessons from the first Pink Tide and the prospects for transformative change?

articles.title: El Salvador’s State of Exception Turns One
articles.description: Rather than address the root causes of violence, President Nayib Bukele’s prolonged state of emergency militarizes Salvadoran society and exacerbates state persecution of vulnerable communities.

articles.title: Movimentos sociais lutam para a vida do rio Tocantins na Amazônia
articles.description: Planos de implantar corredor logístico no rio Tocantins, no norte do Brasil, ameaçam o ecossistema na Amazônia. Movimentos sociais esperam abertura de diálogo com novo governo progressista.

articles.title: The Small Matter of Suing Chevron (Review)
articles.description: Suzana Sawyer’s new book uncovers the machinations of corporate power and judicial imperialism in a decades-long struggle for accountability in the Amazon.

articles.title: Militarized Security and a Cartel Apology in Matamoros
articles.description: The abduction and murder of U.S. citizens in the border city of Matamoros is part of a larger pattern of violence with impunity by state and criminal actors.

articles.title: En Honduras, el gobierno de izquierda enfrenta grandes desafíos
articles.description: Xiomara Castro llegó a la presidencia con la promesa de refundar el país tras más de una década de neoliberalismo agresivo. A pesar de grandes retos, la esperanza no se ha frustrada aun.

articles.title: International Feminist Strike in Argentina
articles.description: A chronicle of International Women’s Day in Buenos Aires, focused on the demands of sexual dissidences.

articles.title: CARNE y ARENA (Virtually present, Physically invisible) – Review
articles.description: Alejandro González Iñárritu’s virtual reality debut invites audiences to immerse themselves in the stories and journeys of refugees and migrants crossing the MexicoU.S. border.

articles.title: ¿Estamos Saliendo Adelante? Assessing Bolivia’s Macroeconomic Stability
articles.description: Falling central bank reserves spark speculation and threaten inflation, increasing frictions within the MAS party.

articles.title: Paro Internacional Feminista en Argentina
articles.description: Una crónica del Día Internacional de la Mujer en Buenos Aires con enfoque en las demandas y reivindicaciones de las disidencias sexuales.

articles.title: Water For All: Community, Property, and Revolution in Modern Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: Sarah T. Hines’s 140-year history of water conflict in Cochabamba, Bolivia paints a picture of environmental world-building that is plodding, messy, and sorely needed.

articles.title: A Conversation with ELN Commander and Peace Negotiator Aureliano Carbonell
articles.description: National Liberation Army Commander Aureliano Carbonell speaks about prospects for peace in the guerrilla organization’s second round of dialogues with the Colombian government.

articles.title: Transnational Guarani Land Defense and Solidarity
articles.description: Guarani community leaders from Brazil and Paraguay come together to strengthen alliances and share experiences of fighting for their ancestral territories.

articles.title: Latin America’s New Left Surge
articles.description: Our latest issue of the NACLA Report explores lessons from the Pink Tide and what lies ahead for the diverse Lefts in Latin America today.

articles.title: Love, Community, and Alienation in Nudo Mixteco (Review)
articles.description: One of Mexico’s best feature films of 2022 sees Ángeles Cruz come into herself as a filmmaker, forging a new path for Indigenous representation in Mexican cinema.

articles.title: Peru: Infinite Protest and Indolent Elites
articles.description: The anti-government protests that erupted in December have a clear political agenda: new elections and the convening of a constituent assembly.

articles.title: In Gentrified Alphabet City, a New Latino Social Club and Art Space Opens its Doors
articles.description: La Sala de Pepe opens a portal to the past, drawing from a long history of Latino social spaces and working to preserve the cultural spirit of the Lower East Side.

articles.title: After Stories: Transnational Intimacies of Postwar El Salvador (Review)
articles.description: Irina Carlota Silber’s second book is a meditation on ethnography, politics, and El Salvador’s post-insurgent generation.

articles.title: Brazil’s First-Ever Ministry of Indigenous Peoples Launched Amid a State of Emergency
articles.description: Among the historic ministry’s first challenges are a large-scale gold mining crackdown and an investigation of genocide against the Yanomami people.

articles.title: Nuevo proceso constituyente en Chile gira a la derecha
articles.description: Partidos debilitados y el legado de la dictadura nuevamente dejan al proceso constitucional en Chile obstaculizado por una crisis de representación.

articles.title: LGBTQ Politics in Nicaragua: Revolution, Dictatorship, and Social Movements (Review)
articles.description: Karen Kampwirth’s new book examines nearly five decades of LGBTQ organizing, culture, persecution, and resistance in Nicaragua.

articles.title: Chile’s New Constitutional Process Shifts to the Right
articles.description: Hollowed-out political parties and the legacy of dictatorship once again leave Chile’s constitutional process hindered by a crisis of representation.

articles.title: Líderes Hondureñas en la mira
articles.description: En el contexto de una cultura de impunidad, mujeres figuras públicas están siendo asesinadas en Honduras en tasas alarmantes.

articles.title: The Heart of Our Earth: Community Resistance to Mining in Latin America (Review)
articles.description: In the face of the massive intensification of extractive industries, Tom Gatehouse’s forthcoming book presents a sweeping study of key mining resistance movements across Latin America.

articles.title: Is Colombia One Step Away from a Fracking Ban?
articles.description: While lawmakers debate a proposed fracking prohibition, deepening struggles over the social and environmental impacts of oil and gas extraction loom.

articles.title: The Perfect Spy Was a Cuban Agent (Review)
articles.description: Jim Popkin’s Code Name Blue Wren recounts the astounding story of Defense Intelligence Agency mole Ana Montes, told by a veteran investigative reporter.

articles.title: El espía perfecto era un agente cubano
articles.description: Code Name Blue Wren (Nombre en Clave Chochín Azul) es la asombrosa historia de Ana Montes, espía infiltrada en la agencia de inteligencia militar de Estados Unidos, contada por un veterano reportero de investigación.

articles.title: El Salvador Arrests Prominent Anti-Mining Activists
articles.description: The government of Nayib Bukele opens civil war wounds by arresting five water defenders linked to the historic community of Santa Marta, raising speculation about a possible reversal of the country’s metals mining ban.

articles.title: Honduran Women Leaders in the Crosshairs
articles.description: Amid a widespread culture of impunity, women public figures are killed in Honduras at an alarming rate.

articles.title: A Legacy of Canadian Intervention in Haiti, 20 Years On
articles.description: As Canada and other foreign powers consider renewed intervention in Haiti, the history of the Ottawa Initiative offers an urgent reminder of the catastrophic consequences.

articles.title: The Investigative Brigade: Hunting Human Rights Criminals in Post Pinochet Chile (Review)
articles.description: Bonnefoy’s book documents the struggle in post-dictatorship Chile to bring human rights perpetrators to justice amid a democratic transition still monopolized by the military.

articles.title: Indigenous Protesters Campaign to Make “Chineo” A Hate Crime in Argentina
articles.description: Women and Indigenous groups denounce widespread sexual violence perpetrated against Indigenous girls and women in northern Argentina.

articles.title: Brazil on Fire: An Autopsy of Bolsonaro’s Failed Coup (Update 3)
articles.description: The failed January 8 “invasion” of Brasília has united many behind Lula’s agenda to rebuild the country. But the battle for Brazil’s future isn’t over yet.

articles.title: Despite Indigenous Resistance, Mexico Authorizes Mining Concessions in Protected Areas
articles.description: President Andrés Manuel López Obrador entered office promising not to grant new mining licenses. But concessions have been authorized in Indigenous territories.

articles.title: Call for Pitches: Afterlives of Empire / Convocatoria: Las huellas del imperio
articles.description: NACLA is currently accepting proposals for an issue on empire in the Caribbean. Send us your pitches by February 15, 2023.

articles.title: Brasília and Washington

articles.title: “The Major Challenge to Brazilian Democracy Today Is Bolsonarismo”
articles.description: In the wake of the attack on Brasília, both the Lula administration and civil society have a major role to play in combatting the forces propelling Brazil’s far right.

articles.title: Book Talk on Cuban Immigration Gets Heated in Miami
articles.description: An event at Florida International University provokes a backlash, stoking longstanding disputes between academia and the Cuban exile community in Florida.

articles.title: Distortion and Subversion: Punk Rock Music and the Protests for Free Public Transportation in Brazil (Review)
articles.description: Rodrigo Lopes de Barros’ book serves as a guide for the Brazilian underground youth movements around the turn of the century, both in their political and cultural dimensions.

articles.title: Popular Organizing is the Only Way to Stop Bolsonarismo
articles.description: The essence of the phenomenon tied to Jair Bolsonaro’s rise is extreme, and it is a potent force for radicalizing people towards authoritarian and violent positions.

articles.title: What’s Next for Bolivia After Camacho’s Arrest?
articles.description: A controversial, high-profile detention reveals deep divides within the MAS and an uncertain path forward amid an ongoing conservative backlash.

articles.title: ¿Que sigue para Bolivia tras la detención de Camacho?
articles.description: Una controvertida detención pone de manifiesto las profundas divisiones existentes en el MAS y el incierto camino a seguir en medio de una reacción conservadora.

articles.title: Peru: The Country of Failed Transitions
articles.description: Peru’s latest political crisis is rooted in the failure of key transitions to consolidate democracy and basic social coexistence.

articles.title: Anti-Abortion Organizing in Colombia
articles.description: Colombia now has one of the most liberal abortion regimes in the Americas, but with conservative groups rallying in opposition, the future of the country’s abortion rights is far from secure.

articles.title: Afro-Dominicanas Against Death

articles.title: The Kings of the World (Review)
articles.description: Laura Mora’s new film depicting five boys on a journey to claim a small piece of land severs the violent myths governing heterosexuality in Colombian society, crafting potent worlds of queer possibility, care, and liberation in the face of violence and dispossession.

articles.title: The Institutionalization of Anti-Haitianism in Dominican History and Education

articles.title: Navigating Apathy and Attacks in the Struggle for Migrant Rights in Chile

articles.title: The Importance of Lula’s Presidency in an Increasingly Multipolar World
articles.description: The inauguration of the leftist two-time former leader as Brazil’s president is a source of hope for social and environmental movements worldwide.

articles.title: NACLA Editors’ Picks 2022
articles.description: Join us in taking a look back at our most memorable web stories of 2022.

articles.title: NACLA Editors’ Photo Picks 2022
articles.description: Examples of the best original photography NACLA published in 2022, both online and in our quarterly magazine, the NACLA Report.

articles.title: Untangling Elite Opposition to Indigenous Rights in Chile
articles.description: Throughout Chile’s constitutional process, right-wing rhetoric has rejected Indigenous recognitions and representation in defense of the status quo.

articles.description: En medio del proceso de cambio en curso, un analista de los movimientos sociales chilenos reflexiona sobre las múltiples izquierdas y las relaciones de poder que existen hoy en Chile.

articles.title: Land Conflicts Targeting Indigenous Communities Intensify in Northern Guatemala
articles.description: Waves of evictions and the criminalization of Q’eqchi’ Maya communities propel rural poverty and forced migration.

articles.description: Six years after the peace accords, former guerrillas develop alternative economies and livelihoods through micro-breweries and cultural spaces.

articles.description: Mientras el capitalismo y el colonialismo se reacomodan en Chile, las comunidades mapuche continuan la lucha para recuperar sus tierras ancestrales.

articles.description: Dos libros sobres las demandas feministas de cara al proceso constituyente en Chile cobran incluso más vigencia después del plebiscito del 4 de septiembre.

articles.title: Help NACLA Grow in 2023!
articles.description: We have big plans for the next year. Help us reach our fundraising goal to keep NACLA thriving!

articles.title: As Colombia Resumes Negotiations with the ELN, the Path Towards Peace Lies through Venezuela
articles.description: The centerpiece of Petro’s “total peace,” talks with the ELN will require considerable cooperation between Bogotá and Caracas.

articles.description: In Guatemala and Honduras, regional anti-mining networks have become key players in struggles to combat extractivism and the criminalization of activists.

articles.description: In Brazil, the most interacted-with posts on social media contradict trustworthy information about Brazilians’ voting intentions.

articles.title: Help NACLA go Back to School this fall!
articles.description: Your support helps us bring crucial news and analysis to new readers this school year. Will you donate today to help us reach our goal?

articles.title: The Condor Trials: Transnational Repression and Human Rights in South America (Review)
articles.description: Francesca Lessa’s book follows the trials of perpetrators of The Condor Plan, the transnational network of state agents that used torture and violence against the Latin American left during the 1970s.

articles.title: Behind Bolivia’s Less Violent Cocaine Trade
articles.description: Narcotrafficking is a bloody business throughout Latin America, but less so in Bolivia.

articles.title: Legacies of War: Violence, Ecologies, and Kin (Review)
articles.description: Kimberly Theidon’s book explores the conjoined legacies of war-time violence against women, children, and nature in Peru and Colombia.

articles.title: Women Win Decades-Long Clean Air Battle in Chile’s Own “Chernobyl”
articles.description: Activists in Quintero-Puchuncaví Bay “sacrifice zone” hail closure of Codelco plant that poisoned their community.

articles.title: After Nearly 100 Years, Argentina Acknowledges State Massacre of Indigenous Communities
articles.description: The 1924 attack was finally proven and recognized during the Napalpí truth trials held this year, and reparations have begun.

articles.title: Recent Paro in Cusco is Latest Expression of Political Agency in Peru
articles.description: Indigenous and campesino communities turn to strikes, road blockages, and other confrontational tactics to reclaim their political agency.

articles.title: Pachamama Politics: Campesino Water Defenders and the Anti-Mining Movement in Ecuador (Review)
articles.description: A new book traces the rise of Indigenous and peasant demands for a moratorium on mining in Ecuador.

articles.title: The Moment of Truth for Chile’s New Constitution
articles.description: Although an overwhelming majority of Chileans supported the need for a Constitutional Convention, the results of the September 4 vote on the new charter remain unpredictable.

articles.title: Tell Mother I’m in Paradise (Review)
articles.description: From upper-class Catholic upbringing to opposing the U.S.-backed military regime, a Salvadoran political prisoner tells her extraordinary story of a life in the revolutionary struggle.

articles.title: Brazil Promises Millions for Pro-Gun Content
articles.description: At a national convention for gun rights activists, a public official from the Brazilian Secretariat of Culture pledged state resources to arms lobbyists.

articles.title: Is Venezuela “Fixed”? Despite Recovery, Sanctions Leave Lasting Impact
articles.description: A look at at Venezuela’s reviving economy and what that means for its people and politics after years of financial crisis.

articles.title: ¿Venezuela se arregló? Impacto de sanciones y medidas de ajuste
articles.description: Después de años sufriendo de la crisis financiera, la economía venezolana se revive e impacta al pueblo y la política.

articles.title: Reclamaciones Mapuche y la política de tierra chilena (reseña)
articles.description: El libro de Kelly Bauer documenta como los gobiernos chilenos responden a las reclamaciones Mapuche de forma inconsistente, favoreciendo en muchos casos a las élites económicas y políticas.

articles.title: Negotating Autonomy: Mapuche Territorial Demands and Chilean Land Policy (Review)
articles.description: Kelly Bauer’s book documents how post-dictatorship governments in Chile have responded inconsistently to Mapuche claims, often favoring political and economic elites.

articles.title: A New Colombia: The Rise of the Left
articles.description: Gustavo Petro and Francia Márquez’ victory in Colombia is unquestionably historic. As the country’s first leftist leaders, they will face significant challenges.

articles.title: ¡Hay otra Colombia¡ La Emergencia de la Izquierda
articles.description: La victoria de Gustavo Petro y Francia Márquez en Colombia es indudablemente histórica. Como los primeres líderes izquierdistas del país, se enfrentan con retos significativos.

articles.title: Meet the Ex-Rebel Women Searching for Peace in Colombia
articles.description: Over 80,000 people remain missing from the civil war. These former FARC fighters search for answers in the very conflict zones they once fought in.

articles.title: For Colombians in Ecuador, Displacement is Ongoing, and Refuge is Elusive
articles.description: Although the international resettlement system considers Ecuador to be a safe country, refugees tell a different story.

articles.title: Whose Brazil? Web Exclusive
articles.description: Exclusive content and additional resources accompanying our Summer 2022 issue of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: Supremacia branca à brasileira
articles.description: Tanto a ideologia do mérito quanto o mito da democracia racial são os consensos ideológicos que sustentam a supremacia branca sem que esta seja vista como tal.

articles.title: Resistance and Solidarity in Quilombo Territory
articles.description: For a quilombola leader and fisherwoman, Black and Indigenous struggles for land and life are essential to collective survival in the face of the climate crisis.

articles.title: Defesa da terra para o futuro da humanidade
articles.description: Uma liderança indígena nos explica o que é o território e porque a demarcação de terras indígenas e as alianças com o povo negro são tão fundamentais.

articles.title: Resistência e Solidariedade no Território Quilombola
articles.description: Para uma líder e pescadora quilombola, as lutas pela terra e pela vida são essenciais para a sobrevivência coletiva em meio à crise climática.

articles.title: O loop das desigualdades
articles.description: A pandemia Covid-19 expôs ainda mais como a violência estrutural baseada em raça, gênero, classe e geografia no Brasil ameaça a vida das mulheres negras.

articles.title: 10 anos da Lei de Cotas: Avanços e desafios na educação superior pública brasileira
articles.description: Na última década, a Lei 12.711 obteve importantes avanços, mas permanecem grandes desafios para garantir sua continuidade.

articles.title: Wins and Uncertainties After 10 Years of Affirmative Action
articles.description: Brazil’s so-called Quota Law has expanded access to higher education for low-income and Black students. The future of the policy now hangs in the balance.

articles.title: Não sou bicho, sou mulher!
articles.description: O direito humano ao próprio corpo é aquilo que perdemos quando somos escravizadas e/ou presas.

articles.title: “They Give Us Work but They Abuse Us”
articles.description: In Colombia’s booming gig economy, temporary immigration measures make little difference in the lives of vulnerable Venezuelan workers.

articles.title: Black Feminist Tide
articles.description: As electoral candidates and movement leaders, Black women in Brazil are reshaping political power and forging new, affirming representations in the process.

articles.title: Veracruz Decriminalized Abortion, but Activists Remain Crucial in Ensuring Access
articles.description: Veracruz is one of seven states that has decriminalized abortion in Mexico. With obstacles to access, many collectives accompany and defend the right to choose (or refuse) maternity.

articles.title: From the Archives: Anti-Racist Struggles in Brazil
articles.description: Select coverage of Brazil’s anti-racist struggles through the decades from the archives of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: Serán las dueñas de la tierra (Review)
articles.description: A new documentary highlights the many difficulties facing an emerging generation of young farmers in Puerto Rico.

articles.title: In Ecuador, Indigenous-led National Strike Intensifies
articles.description: Ten days into the strongest challenge yet to President Guillermo Lasso’s government, the protests only appear to be growing.

articles.title: No One is Safe in Bukele’s Gang War
articles.description: With tens of thousands jailed and scores of reports of human rights abuses, Nayib Bukele’s project slides ever closer to a permanent state of exception.

articles.title: Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira’s Last Journey
articles.description: A suspect has admitted to killing the journalist and Indigenous expert while they were reporting in the Amazon. Agência Pública retraces the steps of their final trip.

articles.title: How Diaspora Voters Rallied to Support Colombia’s Francia Márquez
articles.description: Ignored by the media and lacking a political machine to back her, the vice presidential candidate looked abroad to garner support.

articles.title: Um retrato de política e resistência negra no Brasil
articles.description: Leia a introdução à última edição do NACLA Report.

articles.title: Portraits of Black Politics and Resistance in Brazil
articles.description: Read the editor’s introduction to the latest issue of the NACLA Report, “Whose Brazil? Black Politics and Resistance in an Imperiled Democracy.”

articles.title: Rhythms of the Left in Latin America
articles.description: For the latest wave of the Left in power sweeping the region, the challenges are steep. Radical politics will continue to emerge from the streets.

articles.title: Biden Bungles Bolivia
articles.description: While the Biden administration struggles to impugn Trump for his attempted coup, its approach to Bolivia is undermining that country’s efforts to bring their own coup perpetrators to justice.

articles.title: Gastropolitics and the Specter of Race: Stories of Capital, Culture and Coloniality in Peru (Review)
articles.description: María Elena García’s new book exposes the intensely colonial and patriarchal politics underlying Peru’s much-praised gastronomic boom.

articles.title: Summit of the Americas Underlines Widespread Discontent with U.S. Policy
articles.description: Criticisms of the United States and OAS underlined the need to revive regional integration initiatives independent of U.S. influence.

articles.title: Gustavo Petro es la única opción democrática en la elección presidencial de Colombia
articles.description: La segunda vuelta será una competencia estrecha entre izquierdista Gustavo Petro y el populista de extrema derecha Rodolfo Hernández, para quién los grupos tradicionales de poder van anunciando su apoyo.

articles.title: Nicaraguan Government Outlaws Feminist Groups Serving Vulnerable People
articles.description: Women’s rights groups supporting marginalized and impoverished people have been banned by Ortega’s authoritarian regime.

articles.title: The Mexican Health Care System Under the Administration of AMLO
articles.description: Upon taking office, López Obrador promised to transform Mexico’s health care system and provide long-promised universal coverage.

articles.title: El Sistema de Salud Mexicano Bajo el Gobierno de AMLO
articles.description: Al asumir el cargo, López Obrador prometió transformar el sistema de salud de México y brindarla cobertura universal prometida durante mucho tiempo por el estado.

articles.title: The Tribe: Portraits of Cuba (Review)
articles.description: Through the stories of everyday citizens, writer and activist Carlos Manuel Álvarez highlights the island’s diversity of people and experiences and the failures of the state.

articles.title: Dispatches from the Field: Web Exclusive
articles.description: Exclusive content and additional resources accompanying our Winter 2021 issue of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: Fighting for Food Sovereignty in Guatemala
articles.description: The rising costs of consumer goods has stoked the country’s peasant movement.

articles.title: Chile’s Identity Crisis: Mapuche Still Under Fire
articles.description: After vowing to do things differently, the Boric administration has invoked a familiar playbook in Mapuche territory. The difficult of breaking with the status quo speaks to the entrenched power of the Right.

articles.title: Elecciones colombianas: ¿Es el fin del uribismo?
articles.description: Aunque Gustavo Petro ha ganada la primera vuelta, la derrota histórica del conservadurismo no asegura el triunfo de la izquierda colombiana.

articles.title: Colombia Elections: The End of Uribismo?
articles.description: While Gustavo Petro secured a monumental first-round win, the historic defeat of conservativism does not guarantee a triumph for the Left in the second round.

articles.title: Colombian Election Offers a Historic Opportunity to Protect Land Rights Defenders
articles.description: The leftist frontrunners in the May 29 presidential election have promised to prioritize environmental policies, including implementing a regional treaty to foster a sustainable economy.

articles.title: The Impasse of the Latin American Left (Review)
articles.description: As the Left in power returns, the lessons of the Pink Tide have become increasingly relevant. Recognizing governments’ well-conceived policies as well as their errors is key to understanding the comeback.

articles.title: Rising Tension as Colombians Head to Polls Sunday
articles.description: Political polarization, demonstrations of power by armed groups, security threats, and false alarms as campaigns close a week before polls.

articles.title: Remembering Elizabeth Dore (1946-2022)
articles.description: Remembering a longtime historian of Latin America who gave voice to ordinary people.

articles.title: Coup: A Story of Violence and Resistance in Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: Farthing and Becker’s book sheds new light on the violence of the interim government as well as the heroic resistance of Bolivian social movements.

articles.title: How Latin American Women Are Winning the Battle for Abortion Rights
articles.description: Argentina, Colombia, and Mexico have legalized or decriminalized abortion. Could Chile be next?

articles.title: Jogo de Bicho: Brazil’s Popular but Illegal Lottery Game
articles.description: Although the game is historically tied to criminal groups, Brazil’s Congress is debating a proposal to legalize it.

articles.title: Caribeños at the Table (Review)
articles.description: For Caribbean immigrants in New York City, dynamics of race, class, and gender significantly impact both food practices and health.

articles.title: Prisioneros Políticos de la Narrativa de Ortega
articles.description: En Nicaragua, la historia del gobierno luchando contra el imperio capitalista estadounidense expone contradicciones profundas.

articles.title: Political Prisoners to Ortega’s Narrative
articles.description: In Nicaragua, the story of a government fighting against the U.S. capitalist empire exposes deep contradictions.

articles.title: Decolonial Groundwork: On Geo Maher’s Anticolonial Eruptions
articles.description: An archive of creative rebellion reminds us how maneuvering within imperialism’s blindspots helps pry open space for resistance and liberation.

articles.title: Peru: The Broken Dream of Transformative Government?
articles.description: Though Pedro Castillo’s victory initially seemed like a glimmer of hope, the reality of the past eight months has been disappointing.

articles.title: Perú: ¿El Sueño Roto de un Gobierno de Cambios?
articles.description: Aunque al principio la victoria de Pedro Castillo parecía una indicación de esperanza, la realidad en los últimos ocho meses ha sido una decepción.

articles.title: Argentina 20 Years After La Crisis del 2001
articles.description: As the government closed a new agreement with the International Monetary Fund, Argentine society revisited painful memories from its biggest financial crisis in recent history.

articles.title: Los Muertos y el Periodista (Review)
articles.description: In Oscar Martínez’s new book, the editor-in-chief of El Faro reflects on his years of reporting in and out of El Salvador, questioning journalistic practices, analyzing the ethics of his actions, and grappling with the violence he has witnessed.

articles.title: UK Must Engage with Argentina Over Future of Falkland Islands
articles.description: For Argentina, it’s position in the decades-old dispute over the British Overseas Territory remains clear: Las Malvinas son Argentinas.

articles.title: Quechua Sports Journalist Takes Language Revitalization to New Spaces
articles.description: From Peru’s Andes, soccer commentator Qara Q’ompo’s broadcasts combat stigma while tapping into oral knowledge to expand Quechua’s reach.

articles.title: Las dinámicas cambiantes de violencia: ¿el legado más poderoso de la revolución?
articles.description: Se ha prestado muy poca atención a los cambios drásticos en el panorama de seguridad en Venezuela, incluyendo la vigilancia policial transformada, las cifras astronómicas del crimen y una proliferación de grupos armados.

articles.title: Reality of Dreams: Post-Neoliberal Utopias in the Ecuadorian Amazon
articles.description: A conversation with author Japhy Wilson about his recent book examining Rafael Correa’s plans for the Amazon during his time as president of Ecuador.

articles.title: Integration with the United States or Latin American Independence?
articles.description: At the last Community of Latin American and Caribbean States meeting, Mexico’s president proposed contradicting relationships with North America.

articles.title: In Bolivia, Ex-President Áñez’s Trial Sparks Debate About Justice
articles.description: Critics say her prosecution on coup charges violates due process, but the Bolivian state has a responsibility to seek justice.

articles.title: Disobedient Histories: Descendants of Perpetrators in Argentina Join the Human Rights Movement
articles.description: Decades after the return to democracy, the children of dictatorship-era human rights abusers have ignited a new movement for truth, memory, and justice throughout Latin America and beyond.

articles.title: Latin America and the New Non-Aligned Movement
articles.description: While the Global North has responded as a unified front to the war in Ukraine, in Latin America, countries are choosing their own paths.

articles.title: For Hemispheric Unity, a Change in U.S. Foreign Policy is Needed
articles.description: In the face of a new war in Europe, shoring up support in Latin America will not be as easy as the Biden administration thinks.

articles.title: Living Without Fear: Francia Márquez and Black Feminist Politics in Colombia
articles.description: With her message of inclusion, the Afro-Colombian environmental activistwho earned the third most votes in the March primaries and a VP nominationis making history.

articles.title: La Encrucijada’s Dilemma: Greenwashing Oil Palm in Chiapas
articles.description: On Mexico’s Pacific coast, the government and businesses are trying to legalize a huge area of illegally-cultivated oil palm by reducing the size of an environmental reserve.
