articles.description: Following a new report, families of the 43 students criticized the government of President Andrés Manuel López Obrador for failing to follow through on promises to finally solve the case.

articles.title: Costa Rica’s Rush to the Right
articles.description: With second round presidential elections on April 3, the country faces high levels of voter abstention, an increasingly right-wing Congress, and a choice between two candidates ringed by controversy.

articles.title: Brazilian Youth Fight to Decolonize Climate Justice
articles.description: Activists from Brazil’s urban peripheries are among the hardest hit by the climate crisis, and they are becoming increasingly active in the fight against it.

articles.title: Progressive Politics Makes Gains in Colombia’s Conservative Antioquia
articles.description: After a record-breaking presidential primary, the Colombian Left has a fighting chance at winning the top office and challenging the legacy of the country’s most powerful politician.

articles.title: Latin American Newsrooms Develop Creative Strategies for Survival
articles.description: From strengthening their bonds with their readers to redefining their workflows, newsrooms in the region are determined to keep their editorial work going despite media monopolies, and organizational or financial challenges.

articles.title: ¿De la resistencia hacia la presidencia?
articles.description: En Colombia, el candidato que lidera las encuestas tiene una tarea clave: canalizar el estallido social hacia un apoyo en las urnas.

articles.title: Indigenous Groups Occupy Bogotá Park in Protest
articles.description: Members of Colombia’s Emberá community, many of whom are victims of conflict, have camped for months in Colombia’s capital to generate attention and demand change.

articles.title: Hacer el arte dentro del bochinche
articles.description: Para una artista venezolana, la fotografía ofrece un medio democrático de expresión y experimentación desde distintas disidencias.

articles.title: Making Art Amid Mayhem
articles.description: For a Venezuelan artist, photography offers a democratic means of expression and experimentation from differing viewpoints.

articles.title: Downtown Juárez (Review)
articles.description: The militarized war on drugs, strict U.S. immigration policy, maquiladora development, and misogyny are all part of the cocktail fueling cycles of crime and abuse in a violent border city.

articles.title: The Venezuelan Opposition’s Elusive Quest for Power
articles.description: Persistent internal conflicts have prevented Chavismo’s detractors from organizing a serious political proposal to successfully contest power. Can the opposition democratize?

articles.title: Will Popular Power Survive?
articles.description: Popular power was a cornerstone of the Bolivarian Revolution. Facing co-optation, crisis, and decline, its future remains in question.

articles.title: Territories of Extreme Violence in Ecuador’s War on Drugs
articles.description: Official narratives explaining deadly riots in Ecuador’s prisons fail to recognize the state’s role in fomenting the violence.

articles.title: En busca del centro perdido venezolano
articles.description: Con el país atrapado en una batalla campal en la que el ganador se lo lleva todo, no sorprende que los votantes se sientan apáticos. Las soluciones deben venir del espacio entre los extremos.

articles.title: The Rise and Fall of Barrio Adentro
articles.description: With Hugo Chávez’s celebrated health care project progressively in ruins, the pandemic underlines the need to rebuild.

articles.title: The Many Faces of Chavismo
articles.description: Beyond polarized interpretations, revisiting Chavismo’s long origins and many mutations reveals a political project marked more by adaptation and contradiction than by rigid ideological lines.

articles.title: Campaigning for a More Dignified Colombia
articles.description: In Colombia, women candidates for the presidency are using their campaigns to advocate against violence.

articles.title: From the Archives: Hugo Chávez and Chavismo
articles.description: Coverage of Venezuela spanning two decades from the archives of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: Chavismo Revisited: Web Exclusive
articles.description: Exclusive content and additional resources accompanying our Spring 2022 issue of the NACLA Report.

articles.title: The Feminist Revolution That Wasn’t
articles.description: Chavismo co-opted women’s rights rhetoric but failed to deliver on core feminist demands. For grassroots movements, abortion access remains a key struggle.

articles.title: La revolución que no fue feminista
articles.description: El chavismo cooptó un discurso de derechos de la mujer, pero no respondió a las demandas feministas fundamentales. La lucha por el acceso al aborto sigue siendo clave.

articles.title: La poesía y la política en territorio kari’ña
articles.description: Una artista indígena utiliza testimonios orales y el mundo a su alrededor para conectarse con la naturaleza, revitalizar su lenguaje y preservar la cultura de su comunidad.

articles.title: La oposición venezolana y su escurridiza búsqueda por el poder
articles.description: Por parte de la oposición, conflictos internos persistentes han impedido la coordinación de una propuesta politica sería para contestar el poder con éxito. ¿Puede democratizarse para lograr su meta?

articles.title: ¿Logrará sobrevivir el poder popular?
articles.description: El poder popular fue la piedra angular de la revolución bolivariana. En un contexto de cooptación, crisis y declive, su futura esta en duda.

articles.title: Displaced and Insecure: Understanding the Exodus and Its Effects
articles.description: From brain drain to mental health issues, the mass migration of millions of Venezuelans has far-reaching impacts at home and abroad.

articles.title: Desplazadxs y insegurxs: el éxodo venezolano y sus impactos
articles.description: Desde una pérdida de talento hasta problemas de salud mental, la migración masiva de millones de venezolanos ha tenido impactos importantes dentro y fuera del país.

articles.title: La esperanza perdura en el barrio
articles.description: En medio de una gran crisis y la profunda caída del índice de aprobación de Nicolás Maduro, ¿qué es lo que alimenta la lealtad de quienes aún apoyan a su gobierno?

articles.title: Venezuela’s Missing Center
articles.description: With the country caught in a pitched winner-take-all contest, it is unsurprising that voters feel apathetic. Solutions must come from the space between the extremes.

articles.title: El chavismo, el movimiento estudiantil y el devenir de la izquierda
articles.description: La polarización partidista arrasó con toda autonomía en el mundo universitario durante más de 15 años. La reconstrucción de una izquierda plural y desde abajo resulta clave para propulsar una agenda de luchas tanto para estudiantes como profesores.

articles.title: Chavismo, Student Movements, and the Future of the Left
articles.description: Partisan polarization wiped out autonomous stances within Venezuela’s universities for more than 15 years. Rebuilding a pluralist Left from the grassroots is key to reclaiming combative struggles.

articles.title: Rompiendo la polarización por los derechos LGBTQI
articles.description: En Venezuela, los movimientos que luchan contra la homofobia y transfobia han logrado unir fuerzas de manera transversal, superando diferencias ideológicas y partidistas.

articles.title: Breaking Through for LGBTQI Rights
articles.description: Movements fighting homophobia and transphobia in Venezuela offer an example of organizing that successfully joins forces across ideological and partisan lines.

articles.title: Gaining Ground in the Struggle Against Extractivism
articles.description: From oil to mining, resource exploitation is the central battlefield for Venezuela’s land and environmental movements.

articles.title: Avanzando la lucha contra el extractivismo
articles.description: Del petróleo hasta la minería, la explotación de recursos naturales sigue siendo el campo de batalla clave para los movimientos ambientales y en defensa de la tierra.

articles.title: El chavismo en el mundo
articles.description: La Revolución Bolivariana sacudió el mapa geopolítico. El mayor desafío de Venezuela en materia de política exterior es reconstruir sus relaciones dañadas en el hemisferio.

articles.title: Chavismo in the World
articles.description: The Bolivarian Revolution shook up the geopolitical map. Rebuilding Venezuela’s fractured relations in the hemisphere remains its chief foreign policy challenge.

articles.title: El ascenso y descenso de Barrio Adentro
articles.description: Con el celebrado programa de salud de Hugo Chávez cada vez más en ruinas, la pandemia destaca la necesidad de renovarlo.

articles.title: Navigating Alternate Realities
articles.description: When political tensions run high and misinformation proliferates, there’s no better way to get a clear view than with your own eyes.

articles.title: Chavismo Revisited
articles.description: Read the editor’s introduction to our latest issue of the NACLA Report, “Chavismo Revisited,” focused on Venezuela in the 20 years since the April 2002 failed coup.

articles.title: Making Livable Worlds (Review)
articles.description: Afro-Puerto Rican women on the frontlines of climate justice struggles have the knowledge and practices to build a more sustainable future for all.

articles.title: Making Livable Worlds (Reseña)
articles.description: Las mujeres afropuertorriqueñas en primera línea de la lucha contra el cambio climático tienen el conocimiento y las prácticas para construir un futuro más sostenible para todos.

articles.title: Making the Dominican Republic Great Again?
articles.description: The Dominican government has always aligned itself with white supremacism, following the United States’ lead on immigration policies towards Haitians.

articles.title: Latinx Podcasts on the Rise
articles.description: Latin America has a long and enduring radio tradition. Now, podcasts centering Latinx voices are gaining popularity in the United States.

articles.title: Environmental Justice in the Age of Unnatural Disaster
articles.description: The recent mudslides in Petrópolis are just the latest examples of the issues of unequal access to land and precarious housing in Brazil.

articles.title: Confronting a Century of Global Change in Rural Panama (Review)
articles.description: One village’s shift from subsistence farming community to link in the global economic chain offers important perspective on Panama’s political, economic, and social transformations.

articles.title: Why Haiti Advocacy Needs New Strategies
articles.description: As the Biden administration continues to ignore Haitian civil society proposals for a pathway out of crisis, confronting white supremacy across borders is essential.

articles.title: Cooking Revolutions in the Community Pot
articles.description: In Argentina, culinary cooperatives provide food and support for vulnerable communities.

articles.title: Cocinando Revoluciones en la Olla Popular
articles.description: En Argentina, cooperativas culinarias brindan comida y apoyo para comunidades vulneradas.

articles.title: Colombia Joins the Green Wave
articles.description: In a historic win for reproductive rights, Colombia’s highest court strikes down law that made abortion a crime.

articles.title: International Labor Solidarity in Action (Review)
articles.description: Decades of joint organizing between two independent labor unionsone in Mexico and one in the United Statesshow the power of fighting for workers’ rights across borders.

articles.title: The Devastating Costs of Puerto Rico’s Solar “Farms”
articles.description: The road to achieving sustainable energy in Puerto Rico should not be paved by bulldozing agricultural lands and coastal plains.

articles.title: For Venezuelans in Colombia, the Long Path to Legal Residency
articles.description: Although providing the 10-year residence permits promised last year has been a complicated process, many maintain hope for a more stable future.

articles.title: Remembering María Elena Moyano: 30 Years Later
articles.description: Decades after her shocking murder, the Afro-Peruvian activist and organizer’s legacy for affirming life and collective dignity is more relevant than ever.

articles.title: Guatemala on Trial: Maya Land Rights Case Reaches International Court
articles.description: After a decades-long fight against transnational mining interests and state repression, the community’s case could set a new precedent for Indigenous land and resource rights.

articles.title: An Independent Union Wins Landslide Victory Among Mexican GM Workers
articles.description: The vote by auto workers in Guanajuato to replace the previous employee-friendly union with the National Auto Workers Union in early February is a breakthrough for labor rights.

articles.title: “I Left Venezuela to Defend the Constitution”: An Interview with Luisa Ortega Díaz
articles.description: The former Attorney General discusses how her country has changed since Chávez and what is required to restore democracy.

articles.title: Fact Checking Power, April 11, 2002 and Beyond
articles.description: In the face of a fraught conflict with missteps and misinformation on both sides, empirically informed analysis offers one tool to cut through the noise.

articles.title: ¿Combatir Narcos o Criminalizar la Indigeneidad?
articles.description: En la Muskitia hondureña, los indígenas han sido durante mucho tiempo objeto de violencia e injusticia por parte de la lucha del gobierno contra el narcotráfico.

articles.title: Doña Lucía Hiriart: First Lady of the Pinochet Dictatorship
articles.description: After a life of power behind the scenes, Pinochet’s wife recently died at 98.

articles.title: Libel Conviction in Peru: A ‘Dagger’ for Investigative Journalism
articles.description: A recent defamation case against a Peruvian journalist and publisher has sparked concern about freedom of speech.

articles.title: Why the Opposition Win in Barinas, Venezuela Matters
articles.description: In a race that proves taking power through the ballot box is possible, the radical opposition is the biggest loser.

articles.title: Por Qué Importa el Resultado de Barinas, Venezuela
articles.description: En un evento que muestra las posibilidades de asistir a las urnas, la oposición radical es la que más pierde.

articles.title: A 25 Años de los Acuerdos de Paz, Democracia Débil en Guatemala

articles.title: La autonomía de los pueblos indígenas y el gobierno de AMLO
articles.description: El gobierno del presidente Andrés Manuel López Obrador apoya la autonomía indígena solo cuando se encuentre políticamente conveniente.

articles.title: Global Anti-Abortion Coalition Targets the Organization of American States
articles.description: The conservative backlash against efforts to expand sexual and reproductive rights in the Americas threatens a dangerous regression in human rights.

articles.title: Memory on Chile’s Frontlines
articles.description: The historic uprising against inequality reclaimed past struggles and forged new tools for present resistance.

articles.title: Biden’s New Immigration System Overlooks Mexican Refugees
articles.description: Thousands of Mexican asylum seekers fleeing violence, forced disappearances, and internal displacement remain in limbo.

articles.title: Where is the State of Emergency?
articles.description: In Puerto Rico, two recent femicides sparked renewed feminist organizing to demand concrete action on gender violence.

articles.title: The Frontier Effect (Review)
articles.description: Teo Ballvé unpacks the discourse of state absence in Northern Colombian and argues that statebuilding does not necessarily result in peacebuilding.

articles.title: From Trump to Biden in Latin America
articles.description: The Biden administration holds significant political tools for navigating relations with Latin America. How will it leverage this power?

articles.title: What Does Chile’s New Left Want?
articles.description: The Left’s victories on May 15 and 16 showed the strength of the generation that became politicized in the 2011 student protests. Many of them now will take office at the local level and will play a significant role in the drafting of the new Magna Carta.

articles.title: Madalena (Review)
articles.description: Brazilian filmmaker Madiano Marcheti’s feature debut expands the meanings of environmental justice in Brazil.

articles.title: Against Forgetting
articles.description: Read the editors’ introduction to our latest print issue of the NACLA Report, “Against Forgetting: Mobilizing Memory for Reckoning and Repair.”

articles.title: Cuban Memory Wars (Review)
articles.description: Michael Bustamante’s new book explores contested narratives of the Cuban Revolution, focusing on its first two decades in power.

articles.title: Fordlândia and Capitalism’s Fantasy in the Amazon
articles.description: Henry Ford’s 20th century rubber plantation and company town pioneered a model of destructive forest industrialization still in full force in Brazil’s Amazon.

articles.title: Colombian Uprising Takes Aim at Inequality
articles.description: Mass demonstrations in Colombia continue for the third straight week. After defeating the unpopular tax reform that sparked the movement, protesters push new demands.

articles.title: “We Are Here by Force”: Maya Ixil Activists Fight for Asylum and Justice
articles.description: The stories of two Guatemalan asylum seekers highlight the deep roots of forced migration from Central America and the U.S. role in the ongoing displacement.

articles.title: Electing Chile’s Constitutional Convention: “Nothing About Us Without Us”
articles.description: The rules for rewriting the Pinochet-era Constitution set new standards in creating space for women, Indigenous peoples, and people with disabilities.

articles.title: The Colombian State Misrepresents Its Enemy
articles.description: The government’s response to massive protests repeats old tropes of smearing the organizers as criminals. Labels like “urban terrorist” or “extreme vandals” aim to justify the excessive repression.

articles.title: Sexual Violence: A Weapon to Silence Women Protesting in Colombia
articles.description: A young woman shares her story of suffering sexual violence at the hands of a riot police officer in Cali, one case in a broader misogynist problem.

articles.title: Bukele Responds to Avalanche of International Criticism: “The People Voted for This”
articles.description: On their first day in office, allies of President Nayib Bukele flexed their supermajority in the Legislative Assembly to replace officials in key state institutions, tightening the ruling party’s grip.

articles.title: Elecciones en Perú: entre un nuevo pacto social y la continuidad fujimorista
articles.description: Pedro Castillo y Keiko Fujimori se enfrentan en una segunda vuelta polarizada. Más allá de los candidatos, un bloque nacional popular-rural clama a ser escuchado.

articles.title: Between Change and Continuity in Peru (disponible en español)
articles.description: Pedro Castillo and Keiko Fujimori face off in a polarized second round. Beyond the candidates, a national popular-rural bloc clamors to be heard.

articles.title: NACLA Supports Striking NYU Student Workers
articles.description: Our staff supports GSOC and calls on NYU to meaningfully engage in contract negotiations.

articles.title: U.S. Policy Toward Central America Continues Legacy of Displacement (disponible en español)
articles.description: U.S.-sponsored militarization and neoliberal policies will not lead to structural changes in Guatemala and instead preserve the status quo that forces many to migrate.

articles.title: La política de EE. UU. hacia Centroamérica continúa el legado del desplazamiento
articles.description: La militarización patrocinada por Estados Unidos y las políticas neoliberales no conducirán a cambios estructurales en Guatemala y, en cambio, preservarán el status quo que obliga a muchos a migrar.

articles.title: “I Am the Darker Brother”: Michèle Stephenson’s “Stateless” Documentary
articles.description: In an interview, lawyer-advocate Rosa Iris Diendome discusses her work defending the citizenship status of Dominicans of Haitian descent, which filmmaker Michèle Stephenson chronicles in her recent documentary Stateless.

articles.title: Niños guatemaltecos refugiados, ayer y hoy
articles.description: Decenas de miles de niños guatemaltecos buscaron refugio en México durante el enfrentamiento armado interno en su país, una historia que suele pasarse por alto en el debate actual sobre migración infantil.

articles.title: Exiliados, refugiados, desplazados: infancia y migración en las Américas
articles.description: Entender la historia de la migración infantil y los discursos asociados a este fenómeno es fundamental para hacer frente a las políticas migratorias injustas en todo el hemisferio.

articles.title: Hijos que llegan de lejos
articles.description: Décadas después de haber sido separados a la fuerza de sus madres, los niños dados en adopción transnacional durante la dictadura militar chilena siguen buscando sus orígenes.

articles.title: Los orígenes de una temprana línea directa de la escuela a la deportación
articles.description: Apelar a la inocencia de la infancia como argumento para su protección ayudó a garantizar el acceso a la educación para los niños indocumentados. Pero está complicada política de la infancia también terminó por reforzar, sin quererlo, la criminalización.

articles.title: Infancia y migración en las Américas: Una serie especial
articles.description: Esta serie examina las políticas de migración infantil en el hemisferio desde una perspectiva historica y contemporanea.

articles.title: Comércio internacional de grãos ameaça a Amazônia brasileira
articles.description: Um esforço para expandir a infraestrutura de exportação no Arco Norte do Brasil está destinado a desencadear novos conflitos fundiários. Comunidades amazônicas resistem.

articles.title: Prison Writing of Latin America (Review)
articles.description: Joey Whitfield uses the writings of incarcerated people from across Latin America to show how penal systems reinforce the socio-racial hierarchy of the colonial age.

articles.title: Feminist Political Movement Organizes for Change in Colombia
articles.description: Estamos Listas supports feminist candidates for public office and is taking action to end violence against women in Colombia.

articles.title: Fighting for Survival, Building for Power
articles.description: For members of Nou Pap Dòmi, a collective within Haiti’s PetroChallengers movement, the anti-corruption struggle is a space to imagine the kind of society they seek to create.

articles.title: How the Right Returned to Power in Ecuador
articles.description: Widespread resistance to Correísmo and the Indigenous movement’s promotion of the null vote created the conditions for Guillermo Lasso’s victory in the presidential run-off.

articles.title: Who is Peru’s Frontrunner Pedro Castillo?
articles.description: The little-known candidate, branded as a leftist, emerged as the victor of Peru’s first round election. But his leftism is a façade for the reactionary Right.

articles.title: What Will Happen to Cesar, Colombia When the Mines Leave?
articles.description: With the Swiss mining company Glencore announcing they will close their coal mines in Cesar, residents fear that the company will skip out on its legal obligations to affected communities.

articles.title: Trapped in the Imperial Grip
articles.description: The United States’ systemic anti-Blackness at home and abroad shatters illusions of democracy in Haiti. Achieving true independence demands solidarity.

articles.title: Movement Against Mining Gains Ground in Ecuador
articles.description: In February elections, a referendum to protect water passed and left-wing candidates received strong support. Mining will be a key issue for the next president.

articles.title: The Union of Land Workers is Creating a New Food Paradigm in Argentina
articles.description: The Union is connecting people in the Buenos Aires Metropolitan Area with fresh, organically-grown fruits and vegetables.

articles.title: The Political Anatomy of Haiti’s Armed Gangs
articles.description: In Port-au-Prince, botched NGO and military inventions have fragmented urban space, triggering an explosive proliferation of violent armed groups.

articles.title: Mexican Police Who Massacred Guatemalan Migrants Get Their Guns from the U.S.
articles.description: Guns exported from the U.S. to Mexico end up in the hands of state security forces who commit human rights atrocities. Existing mechanisms for monitoring and controlling the trade are ineffective.

articles.title: Republicans Left a Minefield in Venezuela (disponible en español)
articles.description: Diesel shortages stemming from harsh sanctions introduced at the end of the Trump administration threaten to paralyze Venezuela.

articles.title: Republicanos dejan un campo minado en Venezuela
articles.description: Una escasez de diésel, resulto de severas sanciones introducidas al fina de la administración de Trump, podría paralizar a Venezuela.

articles.title: Organizing Against the Immigration Cruelty Consensus
articles.description: Migrant exoduses from Haiti illuminate how authoritarianism, globalization, and anti-Blackness shape mobility in the Americas and U.S. border policy, regardless of the government in power.

articles.title: One dead and dozens injured at Peru football stadium during pre-match event
articles.description:
Initial reports suggested parts of arena’s wall had collapsed, but Alianza Lima says there were no structural failures

One person has been killed and dozens more injured at the Alejandro Villanueva Stadium in Lima, Peru, according to the football club Alianza Lima.

Hundreds of fans were attending a “flag-waving event” on Friday around the stadium, a day before a derby match between the home team Alianza Lima and local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

articles.title: ‘It’s not just Flávio’: is surname-dropping son downplaying Bolsonaro connection?
articles.description:
Many Brazilians believe there is a ploy to free the younger Bolsonaro from baggage of his father’s name to return the family to power

He possesses one of the most famous family names in Latin American politics. But when the Brazilian senator took to the stage at a conservative conference in Grapevine, Texas, last weekend it was only his forename that was on people’s lips.

For many years the prevailing debate about the Maya centred upon why their civilisation collapsed. Now, many scholars are asking: how did the Maya survive?

A Canadian woman and her seven-year-old daughter, who were held for nearly three weeks in a notorious detention center by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), were released on Thursday evening after posting a bond of $9,500.

A Radio-Canada reporter noticed his maple syrup tasted odd; testing revealed it was adulterated with cane sugar

US moves towards reestablishing working relations between two countries after abducting President Nicolás Maduro

The US has lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, in the latest step towards normalising relations between the two countries after US forces abducted her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro, and his wife.

articles.title: ‘The US is no longer the go-to place’: How Korean culture is taking Latin America by storm
articles.description:
Everything Korean – from K-pop and skincare to food and clothing – is booming in popularity in Chile, Mexico and Brazil

On the polished flagstones of a Santiago cultural centre’s forecourt, four Chilean girls dance in energetic union, counting their steps aloud in Korean.

In front of them, a YouTube video with 1.3bn views plays atop a speaker throbbing to the beat of How You Like That, by the K-pop megastars Blackpink.

articles.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
articles.description:
Lewis’s son Avi Lewis was elected leader of progressive New Democratic party a day before his father’s death

Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer.

Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88.

Submersible hydroelectric technology deployed across the Great Lakes could become a key cog in clean energy efforts, supporters say, amid surging electricity demand and costs.

articles.title: Two Women review – sex comedy remake is French-Canadian answer to Confessions of a Window Cleaner
articles.description:
This tale of a pair of women who tire of their partners and arrange sex with strangers is not sexy enough for erotica and not real enough to be drama

Chloé Robichaud’s new film is a remake of the 1970 French-Canadian sex comedy of the same name (the French title is Deux Femmes en Or) and it hasn’t travelled well: silly, clumsy and dated.

articles.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
articles.description:
Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living

Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods.

Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate

articles.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
articles.description:
Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities

At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said.

Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded.

articles.title: O controle da corrupção e o corte de orelhas
articles.description: A análise das origens do controle parlamentar de contas públicas -o qual teve como palco a Guerra Civil Inglesa (1642-1651)- é particularmente instigante pelo seu simbolismo. As demandas por controle e transparência foram protagonizadas por William Prynne, membro da oposição no Parlamento. Prynne teve as orelhas cortadas como punição à audácia de querer que o governo prestasse contas, como relatado por Jacob Soll, em "The Reckoning: Financial Accountability and the Rise and Fall of Nation".
